description:
This package provides a gui program as well as a rviz plugin to publish static transforms.
  Both support the transformation between various Euler angle representations.
  The rviz plugin also allows to configure the transform with an interactive marker.

This package provides a set of simple math utilities to work
        with angles. The utilities cover simple things like
        normalizing an angle and conversion between degrees and
        radians. But even if you're trying to calculate things like
        the shortest angular distance between two joint space
        positions of your robot, but the joint motion is constrained
        by joint limits, this package is what you need. The code in
        this package is stable and well tested. There are no plans for
        major changes in the near future.

description:
A ROS wrapper of the AprilTag 3 visual fiducial detection
    algorithm. Provides full access to the core AprilTag 3 algorithm's
    customizations and makes the tag detection image and detected tags' poses
    available over ROS topics (including tf). The core AprilTag 3 algorithm is
    extended to allow the detection of tag bundles and a bundle calibration
    script is provided (bundle detection is more accurate than single tag
    detection). Continuous (camera image stream) and single image detector nodes
    are available.

description: Composite arm procedure
readme: \page arm Arm Behavior Overview Astrobee equipped 3DoF perching arm, attached top bottom payload bay. perching arm enables Astrobee grasp handrails International Space Station (ISS). grasped remaining degrees freedom pan/tilt mechanism. propulsion localization turned completely, saving significant energy computational resources. Control arm achieved three-layer driver stack. firmware (avionics) lowest level, executes dedicated microcontroller. direct control servos. firmware communicates serial mid-level driver (perching_arm) runs mid-level processor (MLP). mid-level driver acts bridge, converting serial commands generic sensor_msgs::JointState messages. abstraction integrates perching arm ROS ecosystem, allowing generic visualization tools, simulation software controllers. highest level arm behavior node). enforces high-level logic arm usage convenient means interacting arm. Internal behavior arm behavior callee single action-based entry point controlling arm, called ff_msgs::ArmAction “arm action” accepts command, executes background, sends periodic feedback. task completes result returned callee. services, actions non-blocking exhibit superior performance nodelet contexts. action supports goal, fully-preemptible. means goal preempts current goal previous goal’s callee notified preemption). careful interact arm entity perch behavior. Fundamentally, arm action commands: ARM_STOP Stop action underway. ARM_DEPLOY Deploy arm pan tilt ARM_STOW Stow arm home position. ARM_PAN Pan specific degrees. ARM_TILT Tilt specific degrees. ARM_MOVE Move specific pan tilt GRIPPER_CALIBRATE Instruct firmware find gripper end-stops. GRIPPER_SET Set gripper percentage open. GRIPPER_OPEN Open gripper. GRIPPER_CLOSE Close gripper. description:
A bond allows two processes, A and B, to know when the other has
    terminated, either cleanly or by crashing.  The bond remains
    connected until it is either broken explicitly or until a
    heartbeat times out.

The State Machine Compiler (SMC) from http://smc.sourceforge.net/

The class_loader package is a ROS-independent package for loading plugins during runtime and the foundation of the higher level ROS "pluginlib" library. class_loader utilizes the host operating system's runtime loader to open runtime libraries (e.g. .so/.dll files), introspect the library for exported plugin classes, and allows users to instantiate objects of said exported classes without the explicit declaration (i.e. header file) for those classes.

description:
This package contains a C++ parser for the Collada robot
    description format. The parser reads a Collada XML robot
    description, and creates a C++ URDF model. Although it is possible
    to directly use this parser when working with Collada robot
    descriptions, the preferred user API is found in the urdf package.

description: The CRANE-X7 bringup package
The CRANE-X7 control package
readme: crane_x7_control CRANE-X7のためのパッケージ、 crane_x7_ros のcontrolパッケージです。 crane_x7_controlはU2D2(USB-RS485変換ケーブル)を使用してCRANE-X7のモータ制御を行います。 U2D2はLinuxに接続すると/dev/ttyUSB0として認識されるので、次のコマンドでアクセス権限を変更します。 sudo chmod /dev/ttyUSB0 ケーブルの接続ポート名はデフォルトで/dev/ttyUSB0です。 /dev/ttyUSB1)を使う場合は次のコマンドを実行します。 roslaunch crane_x7_control crane_x7_control.launch port:=/dev/ttyUSB1 crane_x7_controlはデフォルト200Hz周期で制御しています。 制御周期を変更する場合はcrane_x7_control/src/hardware.cppを編集してください。 #define CONTROL_HZ rostopic /crane_x7/crane_x7_control/crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint/current subscribed [/crane_x7/crane_x7_control/crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint/current] average rate: min: max: std dev: window: average rate: min: max: std dev: window: 実際の制御周期が200Hzに達せず、100Hz程度に低くなってしまう場合、USB通信ポートのlatency_timerを変更してください。 cat /sys/bus/usb-serial/devices/ttyUSB0/latency_timer echo /sys/bus/usb-serial/devices/ttyUSB0/latency_timer cat /sys/bus/usb-serial/devices/ttyUSB0/latency_timer 参考資料：https://emanual.robotis.com/docs/en/software/dynamixel/dynamixel_wizard2/#usb-latency-setting crane_x7_controlは/crane_x7をルートとするネームスペースにパラメータやトピックを定義します。 各jointについて次のトピックを配信します。 current：電流値[mA] dxl_position：現在角度[360/4096度] temp：温度[度] dynamic_reconfigure crane_x7_controlはdynamic_reconfigureに対応しています。次のコマンドでrqt_reconfigureを起動してアクセスすると各Jointのサーボパラメータを変更することができます。 rosrun rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure 各パラメータの詳細についてはROBOTIS公式のXM430およびXM540のサーボマニュアルを参照して下さい。 XM430-W350 XM540-W270 CRANE-X7は位置制御モードと電流制御モードに対応しています。基本設定は位置制御モードになっていますが、複数箇所の設定変更を行うことで電流制御モードへ切り替えることが可能です。 サーボモータのOperating Mode（Address：11）を3：位置制御モードから0：電流制御モードへ設定変更する Dynamixel Wizard crane_x7_controlの設定変更 crane_x7_control/config/crane_x7_control.yamlのハンド部分について次のように変更する gripper_controller: type: "position_controllers/GripperActionController" type: "effort_controllers/GripperActionController" publish_rate: joint: crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint gains: crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint: action_monitor_rate: state_publish_rate: stall_velocity_threshold: goal_tolerance: stall_timeout: dynamixel_port: port_name: "/dev/ttyUSB0" baud_rate: joints: crane_x7_shoulder_fixed_part_pan_joint crane_x7_shoulder_revolute_part_tilt_joint crane_x7_upper_arm_revolute_part_twist_joint crane_x7_upper_arm_revolute_part_rotate_joint crane_x7_lower_arm_fixed_part_joint crane_x7_lower_arm_revolute_part_joint crane_x7_wrist_joint crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint crane_x7_shoulder_fixed_part_pan_joint: center: home: effort_const: mode: Python code, generate nice documentation custom Sphinx template Sphinx document Python package Sphinx, add package.xml: <doc_depend>cras_docs_common</doc_depend> <doc_depend>rosdoc_lite</doc_depend> <export> <rosdoc config="rosdoc.yaml"/> </export> Create file rosdoc.yaml content: builder: sphinx sphinx_root_dir: doc Create folder doc, create file doc/conf.py content: Configuration file Sphinx documentation builder. import os.environ['CRAS_DOCS_COMMON_SPHINX_PACKAGE_PATH'] os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))) default, ctu-vras theme custom theme, set CRAS_DOCS_COMMON_SPHINX_THEME_PATH CRAS_DOCS_COMMON_SPHINX_HTML_THEME include master configuration cras_docs_common.sphinx_docs_conf import Create doc/index.rst content: documentation master file |project| |description| Python API toctree:: :maxdepth: modules Indices tables :ref:`genindex` :ref:`modindex` :ref:`search` Finally, create file doc/modules.rst actual modules documented, cras package Submodules cras.ctypes\_utils module automodule:: cras.ctypes_utils :members: :undoc-members: :show-inheritance: Sphinx config package shortcuts automatically links ROS message definitions docs.ros.org. :roswiki:`cras_py_common`, :rosdep:`rosdep2_api.html`, :tf2_msgs:`TFMessage`, :class:`genpy.Message` :class:`tf2_msgs.msg.TFMessage`. automatically loads catkin_sphinx extensions ShLexer cmake.

Subscribed Topics ulc_cmd (dataspeed_ulc_msgs/UlcCmd) Input command ULC. addition speed steering command inputs, topic configures behavior ULC. user turn speed steering components ULC switch shifting steering modes, configure longitudinal lateral acceleration limits. cmd_vel (geometry_msgs/Twist) Simplified interface ULC. messages topic command ULC: speed component ULC activated tracks linear.x field geometry_msgs/Twist message, assuming units steering component ULC activated yaw rate mode tracks angular.z field geometry_msgs/Twist message, assuming units rad/s longitudinal lateral acceleration limits default settings outlined ULC User’s Guide cmd_vel_stamped (geometry_msgs/TwistStamped) header incoming geometry_msgs/TwistStamped messages treated identically cmd_vel topic. can_rx (can_msgs/Frame) topic listens traffic Dataspeed ADAS Kit bus data publish messages ulc_report topic. Published Topics can_tx (can_msgs/Frame) topic transmits ULC command configuration messages Dataspeed ADAS Kit bus control ULC running ADAS Kit firmware. messages constructed based user publishing command messages input topics. ulc_report(dataspeed_ulc_msgs/UlcReport) Feedback data ULC running ADAS Kit firmware.

readme: Dynamic dynamic reconfigure python Register variables dynamic reconfigure server run server fly. .cfg files CMakeLists.txt tinkering! import rospy ddynamic_reconfigure_python.ddynamic_reconfigure import DDynamicReconfigure def dyn_rec_callback(config, level): rospy.loginfo("Received reconf call: str(config)) return config '__main__': rospy.init_node('test_ddynrec') Create D(ynamic)DynamicReconfigure ddynrec DDynamicReconfigure("example_dyn_rec") Add variables description, default min, max, edit_method) ddynrec.add_variable("decimal", "float/double variable", ddynrec.add_variable("integer", "integer variable", ddynrec.add_variable("bool", "bool variable", True) ddynrec.add_variable("string", "string variable", "string dynamic variable") enum_method ddynrec.enum([ ddynrec.const("Small", "int", small constant"), ddynrec.const("Medium", "int", medium constant"), ddynrec.const("Large", "int", large constant"), ddynrec.const("ExtraLarge", "int", extra large constant")], enum ddynrec.add_variable("enumerate", "enumerate variable", edit_method=enum_method) Start server ddynrec.start(dyn_rec_callback) rospy.spin() rosrun ddynamic_reconfigure_python example.py. Run rosrun rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure play values. couple examples class scripts folder.

description:
Dynamixel-Workbench is dynamixel solution for ROS.
    This metapackage allows you to easily change the ID, baudrate and operating mode of the Dynamixel.
    Furthermore, it supports various controllers based on operating mode and Dynamixel SDK.
    These controllers are commanded by operators.
This package contains examples of applying the 'dynamixel_workbench_toolbox' library developed on the basis of dynamixel_sdk to various operating modes of Dynamixel.
This package contains nodes that control the Dynamixel by communicating with the server registered in the 'dynamixel_workbench_controllers' package

This package is composed of 'dynamixel_item', 'dynamixel_tool', 'dynamixel_driver' and 'dynamixel_workbench' class.
    The 'dynamixel_item' is saved as control table item and information of Dynamixels.
    The 'dynamixel_tool' class loads its by model number of Dynamixels.
    The 'dynamixel_driver' class includes wraped function used in DYNAMIXEL SDK.
    The 'dynamixel_workbench' class make simple to use Dynamixels

description: This package includes ROS messages and services for dynamixel_workbench packages

dynamixel_interface aims to provide a fast, scalable and easily configurable
    interface for controlling the Robotis 'Dynamixel' brand of servo motors. It
    is implemented in c++ and is based on the official robotis dynamixel_sdk.
    The controller is designed to handle many dynamixels at once and can
    synchronously communicate with dynamixels spread across multiple serial ports.
    The controller can run in either position, velocity or torque control modes
    and is easily configured with an external yaml file.
readme: Dynamixel Interface: Fast, Scalable Dynamixel Driver package aims provide scalable easily configurable interface controlling dynamixel’s multiple serial devices. description: This package is wrapping version of ROBOTIS Dynamixel SDK for ROS. The ROBOTIS Dynamixel SDK, or SDK, is a software development library that provides Dynamixel control functions for packet communication. The API is designed for Dynamixel actuators and Dynamixel-based platforms.
The DYNAMIXEL SDK ROS example package

Embeds the TCLAP library inside the ecl. This is a very convenient
     command line parser in templatised c++.

Introduces a compile time concept checking mechanism that can be used
     most commonly to check for required functionality when passing
     template arguments.

The containers included here are intended to extend the stl containers.
    In all cases, these implementations are designed to implement
    c++ conveniences and safety where speed is not sacrificed. 

    Also includes techniques for memory debugging of common problems such
    as buffer overruns.

Some fast/convenient type converters, mostly for char strings or strings.
     These are not really fully fleshed out, alot of them could use the addition for
     the whole range of fundamental types (e.g. all integers, not just int, unsigned int).
     
     They will come as the need arises.

A set of tools and interfaces extending the capabilities of c++ to 
    provide a lightweight, consistent interface with a focus for control
    programming.

This includes a suite of programs demo'ing various aspects of the
     ecl_core. It also includes various benchmarking and utility programs for
     use primarily with embedded systems.
Provides an extensible and standardised framework for input-output devices.
This provides an Eigen implementation for ecl's linear algebra.

Template based exceptions - these are simple and practical
     and avoid the proliferation of exception types. Although not
     syntatactically ideal, it is convenient and eminently practical.
Cross platform filesystem utilities (until c++11 makes its way in).

The formatters here simply format various input types to a specified
   text format. They can be used with most streaming types (including both
   ecl and stl streams).

Any tools relating to mathematical geometry. 
     Primarily featuring polynomials and interpolations.

Interprocess mechanisms vary greatly across platforms - sysv, posix, win32, there
  are more than a few. This package provides an infrastructure to allow for developing 
  cross platform c++ wrappers around the lower level c api's that handle these 
  mechanisms. These make it not only easier to utilise such mechanisms, but allow it 
  to be done consistently across platforms.
Ecl frontend to a linear matrix package (currently eigen).

This package provides simple support to cmath, filling in holes
    or redefining in a c++ formulation where desirable.

Metaprogramming tools move alot of runtime calculations to be shifted to
    compile time. This has only very elementary structures at this stage.

Provides a signal/slot mechanism (in the same vein as qt sigslots, 
     boost::signals etc for intra-process communication. These include 
     some improvements - they do not need a preprocessor, are fully type safe,
     allow for simple connections via a posix style string identifier 
     and are multithread-safe.
Common statistical structures and algorithms for control systems.

These are lightweight text streaming classes that connect to standardised
     ecl type devices.

This package provides the c++ extensions for a variety of threaded 
     programming tools. These are usually different on different 
     platforms, so the architecture for a cross-platform framework
     is also implemented.

Timing utilities are very dependent on the system api provided for their use.
	This package provides a means for handling different timing models. Current support
	
	- posix rt : complete.
	- macosx : posix timers only, missing absolute timers.
	- win : none.
Extends c++ type traits and implements a few more to boot.
Includes various supporting tools and utilities for c++ programming.

These tools inspect and describe your system with macros, types 
     and functions.
Color codes for ansii consoles.

These are a very simple version of some of the functions in ecl_converters 
     suitable for firmware development. That is, there is no use of new, 
     templates or exceptions.

This library provides lean and mean error mechanisms. 
    It includes c style error functions as well as a few 
    useful macros. For higher level mechanisms, 
    refer to ecl_exceptions.

Most implementations (windows, posix, ...) have slightly different api for 
     low level input-output functions. These are gathered here and re-represented 
     with a cross platform set of functions.
Libraries and utilities for embedded and low-level linux development.

This avoids use of dynamic storage (malloc/new) and thread safety (mutexes) to
     provide a very simple sigslots implementation that can be used for *very*
     embedded development.

Provides a portable set of time functions that are especially useful for 
     porting other code or being wrapped by higher level c++ classes.

Contains transforms (e.g. differential drive inverse kinematics)
    for the various types of mobile robot platforms.

This stack aims to bring the common tools and algorithms needed to
    develop navigation algorithms, in particular slam. It does not focus on
    the end-point solution, rather the tools needed to create a variety of
    end-point solutions.

Collection of cmake/make build tools primarily for ecl development itself, but also
     contains a few cmake modules useful outside of the ecl.

Maintains the ecl licenses and also provides an install
     target for deploying licenses with the ecl libraries.
Tools and utilities for ecl development.

  using Eigen datatypes in STL containers
Bindings between Numpy and Eigen using Boost.Python

(format-time-string (current-time))))) (file-exists-p logdir) (make-directory logdir (write-file (expand-file-name filename logdir)))))) (add-hook 'kill-emacs-hook 'euslime-save-logs) Cheat sheet slime buffer [TAB] completion describe/ apropos apropos package definition [RET] macroexpansion ,quit quit session ,restart-inferior-lisp restart session ,rossetip set ros hostname ,rossetmaster set ros master uri editing buffers TAB completion load expression load-file repl buffer slime buffers quit buffer [RET] select option Build Source Setup Clone code mkdir ~/catkin_ws/src euslime_ws/src git clone https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/euslime.git Install dependencies rosdep install --from-paths --ignore-src Build ~/catkin_ws catkin build Configure emacs init file source directory ~/.emacs.el (add-to-list 'load-path "~/catkin_ws/src/euslime") (require 'euslime-config) (setq inferior-euslisp-program "roseus") (slime-setup '(slime-fancy slime-banner slime-repl-ansi-color)) Run Source package source ~/euslime_ws/install/setup.bash open emacs type command: euslime Run tests Run tests tests/euslime_tests.py RUN TESTS EUSLISP PROGRAM tests/euslime_tests.py eus tests/euslime_tests.py irteusgl tests/euslime_tests.py roseus RUN SINGLE TEST tests/euslime_tests.py eus.test_eval_1 RUN MATCHING TESTS tests/euslime_tests.py eus.test_eval_* tests/euslime_tests.py *.test_eval_1 Works Euslime composed layers software, ultimately connecting emacs interface EusLisp interpreter. description:
This metapackage depends on the SMACH library and ROS SMACH integration
    packages.

SMACH is a task-level architecture for rapidly creating complex robot
    behavior. At its core, SMACH is a ROS-independent Python library to build
    hierarchical state machines. SMACH is a new library that takes advantage of
    very old concepts in order to quickly create robust robot behavior with
    maintainable and modular code.

this package contains a set of messages that are used by the introspection
    interfaces for smach.

The smach_ros package contains extensions for the SMACH library to
    integrate it tightly with ROS.  For example, SMACH-ROS can call
    ROS services, listen to ROS topics, and integrate
    with
This metapackage depends on the SMACH visualization tools.
maintainers: Jonathan Bohren, ROS Orphaned Package Maintainers

description:
The smach viewer is a GUI that shows the state of hierarchical
    SMACH state machines. It can visualize the possible transitions
    between states, as well as the currently active state and the
    values of user data that is passed around between states. The
    smach viewer uses the SMACH debugging interface based on
    the
maintainers: Jonathan Bohren, ROS Orphaned Package Maintainers

description: FCL: the Flexible Collision Library
readme: FCL Flexible Collision Library Linux Windows Coverage FCL library performing types proximity queries pair geometric models composed triangles. Collision detection: detecting models overlap, optionally, triangles overlap. Distance computation: computing minimum distance pair models, distance closest pair points. Tolerance verification: determining models closer tolerance distance. Continuous collision detection: detecting moving models overlap movement, optionally, time contact. Contact information: collision detection continuous collision detection, contact information (including contact normals contact points) returned optionally. FCL features interface Compilable linux win32 makefiles Microsoft Visual projects generated cmake) special topological constraints adjacency information required input models list model’s triangles Supported object shapes: box sphere ellipsoid capsule cone cylinder convex half-space plane mesh octree (optional, octrees represented octomap library http://octomap.github.com) Installation compiling FCL, Eigen libccd collision checking convex objects https://github.com/danfis/libccd) installed. libccd, compile github version zip file webpage, bug fixing included zipped version. optional libraries installed optional capability FCL. octree collision, install octomap library https://octomap.github.io/. CMakeLists.txt generate makefiles Linux Visual studio projects windows. command line, run mkdir build build cmake linux, compile code. windows, generate visual studio project compile code. Interfaces starting proximity computation, set geometry transform objects involving computation. geometry object represented mesh soup, set set mesh triangles vertice indices std::vector<Vector3f> vertices; std::vector<Triangle> triangles; code set vertices triangles BVHModel template class mesh geometry, default OBBRSS template typedef BVHModel<OBBRSSf> Model; std::shared_ptr<Model> geom std::make_shared<Model>(); add mesh data BVHModel structure geom->beginModel(); geom->addSubModel(vertices, triangles); geom->endModel(); transform object includes rotation translation: rotation matrix translation vector Matrix3f Vector3f code setting transform configured Transform3f pose Transform3f::Identity(); pose.linear() pose.translation() geometry transform, combine obtain collision object instance //geom geometry transform object std::shared_ptr<BVHModel<OBBRSSf>> geom Transform3f //Combine CollisionObjectf* obj CollisionObjectf(geom, objects set, perform proximity computation proximity queries FCL follow common pipeline: set query request data structure run query function request input. result returned query result data structure. collision checking, set CollisionRequest data structure, run collision function: objects CollisionObjectf* CollisionObjectf* set collision request structure, default setting CollisionRequest request; result returned collision result structure CollisionResult result; perform collision test collide(o1, request, result); setting collision request, user easily choose return contact information slower) return binary collision results faster). distance computation, pipeline objects CollisionObjectf* CollisionObjectf* set distance request structure, default setting DistanceRequest request; result returned collision result structure DistanceResult result; perform distance test distance(o1, request, result); continuous collision, FCL requires goal transform initial transform included collision object data structure). pipeline distance/collision: objects CollisionObjectf* CollisionObjectf* goal transforms Transform3f tf_goal_o1 Transform3f tf_goal_o2 set continuous collision request structure, default setting ContinuousCollisionRequest request; result returned continuous collision result structure ContinuousCollisionResult result; perform continuous collision test continuousCollide(o1, tf_goal_o1, tf_goal_o2, request, result); FCL supports broadphase collision/distance groups objects avoid square complexity. collision, broadphase algorithm return collision pairs. distance, return pair minimum distance. FCL CollisionManager structure manage objects involving collision distance operations. Initialize collision manager group objects. FCL implementations CollisionManager. Generally, DynamicAABBTreeCollisionManager provide performance. BroadPhaseCollisionManagerf* manager1 DynamicAABBTreeCollisionManagerf(); Initialize collision manager group objects. BroadPhaseCollisionManagerf* manager2 DynamicAABBTreeCollisionManagerf(); add objects collision manager, BroadPhaseCollisionManager::registerObject() function add object std::vector<CollisionObjectf*> objects1 for(std::size_t objects1.size(); manager1->registerObject(objects1[i]); choose BroadPhaseCollisionManager::registerObjects() function add set objects std::vector<CollisionObjectf*> objects2 manager2->registerObjects(objects2); order collect information broadphase, CollisionManager requires settings: callback collision distance; intermediate data store information generated broadphase computation. convenience, FCL default callbacks satisfy call data satisfy collision distance queries. collision DefaultCollisionCallback DefaultCollisionData distance DefaultDistanceCallback DefaultDistanceData. default collision/distance data structs simply containers include request distance structures query type mentioned DefaultCollisionData collision_data; DefaultDistanceData distance_data; Setup managers, related initializing broadphase acceleration structure objects input manager1->setup(); manager2->setup(); Examples queries Collision query object groups collision numbers manager2->collide(manager1, &collision_data, DefaultCollisionFunction); int n_contact_num collision_data.result.numContacts(); Distance query object groups minimum distance manager2->distance(manager1, &distance_data, DefaultDistanceFunction); double min_distance distance_data.result.min_distance; collision query group manager1->collide(&collision_data, DefaultCollisionFunction); distance query group manager1->distance(&distance_data, DefaultDistanceFunction); Collision query object group entire group manager2->collide(objects1[0], &collision_data, DefaultCollisionFunction); Distance query object group entire group manager2->distance(objects1[0], &distance_data, DefaultDistanceFunction); examples, refer test folder: test_fcl_collision.cpp: provide examples collision test test_fcl_distance.cpp: provide examples distance test test_fcl_broadphase.cpp: provide examples broadphase collision/distance test test_fcl_frontlist.cpp: provide examples frontlist collision acceleration test_fcl_octomap.cpp: provide examples collision/distance computation octomap data data types.

description:
The public fetch_drivers package is a binary only release.

    fetch_drivers contains both the drivers and firmware for the fetch and freight research robots.
    There should be no reason to use these drivers unless you're running on a fetch or a freight research robot.
    This package, is a cmake/make only package which installs the binaries for the drivers and firmware.
maintainers: Carl Saldanha, Eric Relson, Sarah Elliott, Fetch Robotics Open Source Team
readme: Fetch Binary Drivers public binary version drivers firmware Fetch Research Platforms. Fetch Research Platforms. Commonly Fetch Freight. Fetch arm. drivers firmware package https://docs.fetchrobotics.com/ Fetch Drivers catkin package folder called fetch_drivers, fetch_binary_drivers. we’ve previously releasing packages depend fetch_drivers. changing distribute drivers. package needed you’re Fetch Research Platforms. goal support Fetch Research Platform customers improved, automated build release process. enable updates faster. discussed ROSCon talk “Hermetic Robot Deployment Multi-Stage Dockers” @levavakian @bluryi internal build/test/deployment Docker: Video, Slides. public repository, designed consume output private fetch_drivers repository enable Fetch Research Platform users access drivers/firmware faster official ros packages. create output private package, special build job runs docker container ensure don’t accidentally pull private dependencies, doesn’t output additional commercial robot drivers. Previously, built drivers private buildbot, hosted packages site. manually synced mirror ros packages. allowed ensure tested versions dependancies mirror. process automated https://docs.fetchrobotics.com information.

Step Include FMIAdapter.h fmi_adapter package code. #include "fmi_adapter/FMIAdapter.h" Step Instantiate adapter class path FMU file desired simulation step size. step-size argument omitted, default step size FMU file ros::Duration stepSize(0.001); fmi_adapter::FMIAdapter adapter(fmuPath, stepSize); Step Create subscribers timers set FMU’s input values. ros::Subscriber subscriber nHandle.subscribe<std_msgs::Float64>("angle_x", [&adapter](const std_msgs::Float64::ConstPtr& msg) adapter.setInputValue("angleX", ros::Time::now(), msg->data); angle_x topic angleX input variable FMU. adapter.getInputVariableNames() list input variables. Step Create timer subscriber triggers simulation FMU adapter.doStepsUntil(..). ros::Timer timer nHandle.createTimer(ros::Duration(0.01), [&](const ros::TimerEvent& event) adapter.doStepsUntil(event.current_expected); double adapter.getOutputValue("angleY"); publish topic adapter.getOutputVariableNames() list output variables. Step Set parameters initial values FMU: adapter.setInitialValue("dampingParam", adapter.setInitialValue("angleX", function adapter.initializeFromROSParameters(nodeHandle) initialize parameters ROS parameters. note characters FMU parameter names supported ROS replaced FMIAdapter::rosifyName(name). readme: foxglove_bridge High performance ROS ROS WebSocket bridge Foxglove WebSocket protocol, written Motivation Live debugging ROS systems traditionally relied running ROS tooling rviz. requires GUI connected peripherals robot, replicating ROS environment network-connected development machine including version ROS, custom message definitions, overcome limitation remote debugging web tooling non-ROS systems, rosbridge developed. rosbridge suffers performance problems high frequency topics and/or large messages, protocol support full visibility ROS systems interacting parameters full graph publishers subscribers. foxglove_bridge Foxglove WebSocket protocol, similar protocol rosbridge ability support additional schema formats ROS .msg ROS .idl, parameters, graph introspection, non-ROS systems. bridge written designed high performance overhead minimize impact robot stack. Installation Note: project active development binary releases foxglove_bridge outdated. latest features bug fixes, building foxglove_bridge source. foxglove_bridge package ROS Melodic Noetic, ROS Humble Rolling. Earlier releases ROS supported due API design and/or performance limitations. package installed command: sudo apt install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-foxglove-bridge Running bridge run bridge node, recommended launch file: ROS roslaunch --screen foxglove_bridge foxglove_bridge.launch port:=8765 <launch> Including launch file <include file="$(find foxglove_bridge)/launch/foxglove_bridge.launch"> <arg name="port" arguments </include> </launch> ROS ros2 launch foxglove_bridge foxglove_bridge_launch.xml port:=8765 <launch> Including launch file <include file="$(find-pkg-share foxglove_bridge)/launch/foxglove_bridge_launch.xml"> <arg name="port" arguments </include> </launch> Configuration Parameters configure behavior bridge. parameters set initialization launch file command line, modified runtime. port: TCP port bind WebSocket server valid TCP port number, random port. Defaults address: host address bind WebSocket server Defaults listening interfaces default. Change IPv6) accept connections local machine. tls: true, Transport Layer Security (TLS) encrypted communication. Defaults false. certfile: Path certificate TLS. Required tls set true. Defaults keyfile: Path private key TLS. Required tls set true. Defaults topic_whitelist: List regular expressions (ECMAScript grammar) whitelisted topic names. Defaults service_whitelist: List regular expressions (ECMAScript grammar) whitelisted service names. Defaults param_whitelist: List regular expressions (ECMAScript grammar) whitelisted parameter names. Defaults client_topic_whitelist: List regular expressions (ECMAScript grammar) whitelisted client-published topic names. Defaults send_buffer_limit: Connection send buffer limit bytes. Messages dropped connection’s send buffer reaches limit avoid queue outdated messages building Defaults use_compression: websocket compression (permessage-deflate). recommended leave turned increases CPU usage per-message compression yields compression ratios robotics data. Defaults false. capabilities: List supported server capabilities. Defaults [clientPublish,parameters,parametersSubscribe,services,connectionGraph,assets]. asset_uri_allowlist: List regular expressions (ECMAScript grammar) allowed asset URIs. resource_retriever resolve package://, file:// http(s):// URIs. Note list carefully configured confidential files accidentally exposed websocket connection. extra security measure, URIs consecutive dots disallowed construct URIs retrieval confidential files allowlist configured strict package://<pkg_name>/../../../secret.txt). Defaults ["^package://(?:\w+/)*\w+\.(?:dae|fbx|glb|gltf|jpeg|jpg|mtl|obj|png|stl|tif|tiff|urdf|webp|xacro)$"]. (ROS max_update_ms: maximum number milliseconds wait polling roscore topics, services, parameters. Defaults (ROS service_type_retrieval_timeout_ms: Max number milliseconds retrieving services type information. Defaults (ROS num_threads: number threads ROS node executor. controls number subscriptions processed parallel. means thread CPU core. Defaults (ROS min_qos_depth: Minimum depth QoS profile subscriptions. Defaults set limit subscriber’s QoS depth computed summing depths publishers. (ROS max_qos_depth: Maximum depth QoS profile subscriptions. Defaults (ROS best_effort_qos_topic_whitelist: List regular expressions (ECMAScript) topics forced ‘best_effort’ QoS. Unmatched topics ‘reliable’ QoS publishers ‘reliable’, ‘best_effort’ publishers ‘best_effort’. Defaults (match (ROS include_hidden: Include hidden topics services. Defaults false. (ROS disable_load_message: publish loaned message publishing client message. Defaults true. Building source Fetch source install dependencies <path/to/your/ros_ws> git clone https://github.com/foxglove/ros-foxglove-bridge.git src/ros-foxglove-bridge rosdep update rosdep install --ignore-src --default-yes --from-path src ROS catkin_make source install/local_setup.bash roslaunch --screen foxglove_bridge foxglove_bridge.launch ROS colcon build --event-handlers console_direct+ --symlink-install source install/local_setup.bash ros2 launch foxglove_bridge foxglove_bridge_launch.xml Clients Foxglove connects foxglove_bridge live robotics visualization. Examples: CMakeLists.txt {.sourceCode .cmake} find_package(catkin REQUIRED COMPONENTS std_msgs message_generation genmypy) package.xml {.sourceCode .xml} <build_depend>genmypy</build_depend> CLI genmypy Usage: genmypy {msg,srv,module} positional arguments: {msg,srv,module} msg Generate stub files .msg files srv Generate stub files .srv files module Generate __init__.pyi msg/srv directory optional arguments: show message exit Examples: {.sourceCode Message files genmypy msg custom_msgs custom_msgs/msg/Custom.msg genmypy msg std_msgs –out-dir /opt/ros/noetic/share/std_msgs/msg/Header.msg genmypy msg sensor_msgs –out-dir -Istd_msgs:/opt/ros/noetic/share/std_msgs/msg -Isensor_msgs:/opt/ros/noetic/share/sensor_msgs/msg /opt/ros/noetic/share/sensor_msgs/msg/PointCloud2.msg Service files genmypy srv custom_msgs custom_msgs/srv/Custom.msg genmypy srv nav_msgs –out-dir -Istd_msgs:/opt/ros/noetic/share/std_msgs/msg -Isensor_msgs:/opt/ros/noetic/share/sensor_msgs/msg /opt/ros/noetic/share/sensor_msgs/srv/SetCameraInfo.srv Module files genmypy module custom_msgs/msg genmypy module –module-finder /opt/ros/noetic/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/std_msgs/msg/ `genmypy msg` `genmypy srv`: {.sourceCode Usage: genmypy {msg,srv} [--out-dir OUT_DIR] [--include-path INCLUDE_PATH] package files [files positional arguments: package Package files files Files generate stubs optional arguments: show message exit --out-dir OUT_DIR Output directory. option unset, stub file generated directory input. --include-path INCLUDE_PATH, INCLUDE_PATH Include paths processing files genmypy module: {.sourceCode Usage: genmypy module [–out-dir OUT_DIR] package_dir Positional arguments: package_dir Package directory create init.pyi Optional arguments: show message exit –out-dir OUT_DIR Output directory. option unset, init.pyi generated directory package_dir.

tf is a package that lets the user keep track of multiple coordinate
frames over time. tf maintains the relationship between coordinate
frames in a tree structure buffered in time, and lets the user
transform points, vectors, etc between any two coordinate frames at
any desired point in time.
authors: Tully Foote, Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Wim Meeussen

description:
This package contains a set of conversion functions to convert
common tf datatypes (point, vector, pose, etc) into semantically
identical datatypes used by other libraries. The conversion functions
make it easier for users of the transform library (tf) to work with
the datatype of their choice. Currently this package has support for
the Kinematics and Dynamics Library (KDL) and the Eigen matrix
library. This package is stable, and will get integrated into tf in
the next major release cycle (see roadmap).
A metapackage to bring in the default packages second generation Transform Library in ros, tf2.
tf2 unit tests

tf2 is the second generation of the transform library, which lets
    the user keep track of multiple coordinate frames over time. tf2
    maintains the relationship between coordinate frames in a tree
    structure buffered in time, and lets the user transform points,
    vectors, etc between any two coordinate frames at any desired
    point in time.
authors: Tully Foote, Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Wim Meeussen

description:
The hector_localization stack is a collection of packages, that provide the full 6DOF pose of a robot or platform.
    It uses various sensor sources, which are fused using an Extended Kalman filter.

    Acceleration and angular rates from an inertial measurement unit (IMU) serve as primary measurements.
    The usage of other sensors is application-dependent. The hector_localization stack currently supports
    GPS, magnetometer, barometric pressure sensors and other external sources that provide a geometry_msgs/PoseWithCovariance
    message via the poseupdate topic.
hector_pose_estimation provides the hector_pose_estimation node and the hector_pose_estimation nodelet.

hector_pose_estimation_core is the core package of the hector_localization stack. It contains the Extended Kalman Filter (EKF)
    that estimates the 6DOF pose of the robot. hector_pose_estimation can be used either as a library, as a nodelet or as a standalone node.

message_to_tf translates pose information from different kind of common_msgs message types to tf. Currently the node supports nav_msgs/Odometry, geometry_msgs/PoseStamped and sensor_msgs/Imu messages as input.
    The resulting transform is divided into three subtransforms with intermediate frames for the footprint and the stabilized base frame (without roll and pitch).
hector_components_description contains URDF xacro macros for robot components, so they are easily attachable to robot models.
hector_models contains (urdf) models of robots, sensors etc.
hector_sensors_description contains URDF xacro macros for sensors, so they are easily attachable to robot models and usable in gazebo.
hector_xacro_tools
hector_compressed_map_transport provides means for transporting compressed map data through the use of image_transport.
hector_geotiff provides a node that can be used to save occupancy grid map, robot trajectory and object of interest data to RoboCup Rescue compliant GeoTiff images.
Contains launch files for the hector_geotiff mapper.
hector_geotiff_plugins contains plugins that extend geotiff maps generated by hector_geotiff.
hector_imu_attitude_to_tf is a lightweight node that can be used to publish the roll/pitch attitude angles reported via a imu message to tf.
hector_imu_tools provides some tools for processing IMU messages

hector_map_server provides a service for retrieving the map, as well as for raycasting based obstacle queries (finds next obstacle in the map, given start and endpoint
    in any tf coordinate frame).

hector_map_tools contains some functions related to accessing information from OccupancyGridMap maps.
    Currently consists of a single header.

hector_mapping is a SLAM approach that can be used without odometry as well as on platforms that exhibit roll/pitch motion (of the sensor, the platform or both).
    It leverages the high update rate of modern LIDAR systems like the Hokuyo UTM-30LX and provides 2D pose estimates at scan rate of the sensors (40Hz for the UTM-30LX).
    While the system does not provide explicit loop closing ability, it is sufficiently accurate for many real world scenarios. The system has successfully been used on
    Unmanned Ground Robots, Unmanned Surface Vehicles, Handheld Mapping Devices and logged data from quadrotor UAVs.
hector_marker_drawing provides convenience functions for easier publishing of visualization markers.
hector_nav_msgs contains messages and services used in the hector_slam stack.
The hector_slam metapackage that installs hector_mapping and related packages.
hector_slam_launch contains launch files for launching hector_slam with different robot systems/setups/postprocessing scenarios.
hector_trajectory_server keeps track of tf trajectories extracted from tf data and makes this data accessible via a service and topic.

description:
ROS package for LDS(HLS-LFCD2).
    The LDS (Laser Distance Sensor) is a sensor sending the data to Host for the simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM). Simultaneously the detecting obstacle data can also be sent to Host. Coal #include "coal/math/transform.h" #include "coal/mesh_loader/loader.h" #include "coal/BVH/BVH_model.h" #include "coal/collision.h" #include "coal/collision_data.h" #include <iostream> #include <memory> Function load convex mesh `.obj`, `.stl` `.dae` file. function imports object file BVHModel, point cloud hierarchically transformed tree bounding volumes. leaves tree individual points point cloud stored `.obj` file. BVH collision detection. computational efficiency, prefer work convex hull point cloud. insures underlying object convex fast collision detection algorithms GJK EPA called object. creating BVH structure point cloud, function computes convex hull. std::shared_ptr<coal::ConvexBase> loadConvexMesh(const std::string& file_name) coal::NODE_TYPE bv_type coal::BV_AABB; coal::MeshLoader loader(bv_type); coal::BVHModelPtr_t bvh loader.load(file_name); bvh->buildConvexHull(true, return bvh->convex; int main() Create coal shapes. Coal supports primitive shapes: boxes, spheres, capsules, cylinders, ellipsoids, cones, planes, halfspace convex meshes convex hulls clouds points). supports BVHs (bounding volumes hierarchies), height-fields octrees. std::shared_ptr<coal::Ellipsoid> shape1 std::make_shared<coal::Ellipsoid>(0.7, std::shared_ptr<coal::ConvexBase> shape2 loadConvexMesh("../path/to/mesh/file.obj"); Define shapes' placement space coal::Transform3s T1.setQuatRotation(coal::Quaternion3f::UnitRandom()); T1.setTranslation(coal::Vec3s::Random()); coal::Transform3s coal::Transform3s::Identity(); T2.setQuatRotation(coal::Quaternion3f::UnitRandom()); T2.setTranslation(coal::Vec3s::Random()); Define collision requests results. collision request set parameters collision pair. set positive negative security margin. distance shapes security margin, shapes considered collision. Setting positive security margin usefull motion planning, prevent shapes close physics simulation, allowing negative security margin usefull stabilize simulation. coal::CollisionRequest col_req; col_req.security_margin collision result stores result collision test (signed distance shapes, witness points location, normal coal::CollisionResult col_res; Collision call coal::collide(shape1.get(), shape2.get(), col_req, col_res); access collision result populated std::cout "Collision? col_res.isCollision() (col_res.isCollision()) coal::Contact contact col_res.getContact(0); penetration depth account security margin. penetration depth true signed distance separates shapes. distance takes account security margin, simply add std::cout "Penetration depth: contact.penetration_depth std::cout "Distance shapes including security margin: contact.penetration_depth col_req.security_margin std::cout "Witness point shape1: contact.nearest_points[0].transpose() std::cout "Witness point shape2: contact.nearest_points[1].transpose() std::cout "Normal: contact.normal.transpose() calling collision test, important clear previous results stored collision result. col_res.clear(); return Python translated python python bindings Coal: import numpy import coal Optional: Pinocchio library rigid body algorithms library handy module. installed simply `conda conda-forge install pinocchio`. Installing pinocchio installs coal. import pinocchio pin def loadConvexMesh(file_name: str): loader coal.MeshLoader() bvh: coal.BVHModelBase loader.load(file_name) bvh.buildConvexHull(True, return bvh.convex "__main__": Create coal shapes shape1 coal.Ellipsoid(0.7, shape2 loadConvexMesh("../path/to/mesh/file.obj") Define shapes' placement space coal.Transform3s() T1.setTranslation(pin.SE3.Random().translation) T1.setRotation(pin.SE3.Random().rotation) coal.Transform3s(); arrays works T1.setTranslation(np.random.rand(3)) T2.setRotation(pin.SE3.Random().rotation) Define collision requests results col_req coal.CollisionRequest() col_res coal.CollisionResult() Collision call coal.collide(shape1, shape2, col_req, col_res) Accessing collision result populated print("Is collision? {col_res.isCollision()}) col_res.isCollision(): contact: coal.Contact col_res.getContact(0) print("Penetration depth: contact.penetration_depth) print("Distance shapes including security margin: contact.penetration_depth col_req.security_margin) print("Witness point shape1: contact.getNearestPoint1()) print("Witness point shape2: contact.getNearestPoint2()) print("Normal: contact.normal) running collision call, important clear col_res.clear() Acknowledgments development Coal actively supported Gepetto team @LAAS-CNRS, Willow team @INRIA extent, Eureka Robotics.

Features Install Examples Contribute Publications Authors Features Combines advantages Ipopt Snopt Eigen: Ipopt Snopt Eigen :heavy_check_mark: high-quality solvers nonlinear optimization :heavy_check_mark: modern, intuitive formulations vectors matrices API inconvenient error-prone (raw pointers, index management, jacobian construction) :heavy_check_mark: highly efficient implementations linking exporting difficult Solver independent formulation variables constraints Eigen (highly efficient) Automatic index management formulation variable- constraint-sets Integration: pure cmake find_package(ifopt) catkin/ROS (optional) light-weight lines code) easy extend optimization problem consists multiple independent variable- constraint-sets. set represents common concept, set variables represents spline coefficients, footstep positions. Similarly, constraint-set groups similar constraints ifopt users define sets independently separate classes builds problem sets. worrying adapting indices adding removing sets). find (variable-sets x0_lower x0_upper (bounds variable-set arg min c0(x0,x1)+c1(x0,x1) (cost-terms g0_lower g0(x0,x1) g0_upper (constraint-set g1_lower g1(x0,x1) g0_upper (constraint-set Supplying derivative information greatly increases solution speed. ifopt define derivative cost-term/constraint-set respect variable-set independently. ensures order variable-sets vector, derivative information valid. “Jacobian blocks” supplied ConstraintSet::FillJacobianBlock() build complete Jacobian cost constraints. graphical overview UML Install easiest install ROS binaries you’re set! description: camera_calibration_parsers contains routines for reading and writing camera calibration parameters.

This package provides a C++ interface for camera calibration
     information.  It provides CameraInfo, and handles SetCameraInfo
     service requests, saving and restoring the camera calibration
     data.
Common code for working with images in ROS.

image_transport should always be used to subscribe to and publish images. It provides transparent
     support for transporting images in low-bandwidth compressed formats. Examples (provided by separate
     plugin packages) include JPEG/PNG compression and Theora streaming video.

polled_camera contains a service and C++ helper classes for implementing a polled
     camera driver node and requesting images from it. The package is currently for
     internal use as the API is still under development.

camera_calibration allows easy calibration of monocular or stereo
     cameras using a checkerboard calibration target.

Contains nodelets for processing depth images such as those
     produced by OpenNI camera. Functions include creating disparity
     images and point clouds, as well as registering (reprojecting)
     a depth image into another camera frame.
image_pipeline fills the gap between getting raw images from a camera driver and higher-level vision processing.
Single image rectification and color processing.
authors: Patrick Mihelich, Kurt Konolige, Jeremy Leibs

description:
A simple viewer for ROS image topics. Includes a specialized viewer
  for stereo + disparity images.
Stereo and single image rectification and disparity processing.
authors: Patrick Mihelich, Kurt Konolige, Jeremy Leibs

description:
Compressed_depth_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for transparently sending
    depth images (raw, floating-point) using PNG compression.

Compressed_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for transparently sending images
    encoded as JPEG or PNG.
authors: Patrick Mihelich, Julius Kammerl, David Gossow

description:
A set of plugins for publishing and subscribing to sensor_msgs/Image topics
    in representations other than raw pixel data. For example, for viewing a
    stream of images off-robot, a video codec will give much lower bandwidth
    and latency. For low frame rate tranport of high-definition images, you
    might prefer sending them as JPEG or PNG-compressed form.
authors: Patrick Mihelich, Julius Kammerl, David Gossow

description:
Theora_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for
    transparently sending an image stream encoded with the Theora codec.
authors: Julius Kammerl, Patrick Mihelich, Ethan Dreyfuss

description: ImageZero is a fast lossless image compression algorithm for RGB color photos.
readme: ImageZero ImageZero high-performance lossless RGB color image compressor/decompressor. Compared PNG, compresses bit PPM files times faster, decompresses PPM times faster, compression rate good PNG natural photos. ImageZero suited “flat” (computer generated) graphics grayscale images. code “early work progress”, image format, library API fully outlined contact author, AUTHORS file. feedback appreciated. code originally https://github.com/cfeck/imagezero modified build shared library part catkin package.

description: A plugin to image_transport for transparently sending images encoded with ImageZero.
A library that provides convenient methods for manipulating ROS images with ImageZero
imu_pipeline
Processors for sensor_msgs::Imu data
Node/nodelet combination to transform sensor_msgs::Imu data from one frame into another.

Filter which fuses angular velocities, accelerations, and (optionally) magnetic readings from a generic IMU device into a quaternion to represent the orientation of the device wrt the global frame. Based on the algorithm by Roberto G. Valenti etal. described in the paper "Keeping a Good Attitude: A Quaternion-Based Orientation Filter for IMUs and MARGs" available at http://www.mdpi.com/1424-8220/15/8/19302 .

Filter which fuses angular velocities, accelerations, and (optionally) magnetic readings from a generic IMU device into an orientation. Based on code by Sebastian Madgwick, http://www.x-io.co.uk/node/8#open_source_ahrs_and_imu_algorithms.
Various tools for IMU devices
RVIZ plugin for IMU visualization

description:
IRIS package for Localization and Mapping (LaMa).

        with a dynamic likelihood field and a 2D RB Particle Filter SLAM solution
        with multithreading.

ROS driver for a generic Linux joystick.
    The joy package contains joy_node, a node that interfaces a
    generic Linux joystick to ROS. This node publishes a "Joy"
    message, which contains the current state of each one of the
    joystick's buttons and axes.
authors: Morgan Quigley, Brian Gerkey, Kevin Watts, Blaise Gassend
readme: ROS Driver Generic Linux Joysticks joy package joy_node, node interfaces generic Linux joystick ROS. node publishes “Joy” message, current state joystick’s buttons axes. Supported Hardware node work joystick supported Linux. Published Topics joy (sensor_msgs/Joy): outputs joystick state. Device Selection parameters controlling device ~dev (string, default: “/dev/input/js0”) ~dev_name (string, default: (empty disabled)) ~dev_name empty, ~dev defines Linux joystick device read joystick events. ~dev_name defined, node enumerates joysticks, /dev/input/js*. joystick matching ~dev_name opened. joystick matches desired device device ~dev fallback. list names connected joysticks, invalid ~dev_name rosrun joy joy_node _dev_name:="*" output INFO]: joystick: LIS3LV02DL Accelerometer (/dev/input/js1). INFO]: joystick: Microsoft X-Box pad (/dev/input/js0). [ERROR]: find joystick Falling default device. node started device list. rosrun joy joy_node _dev_name:="Microsoft X-Box pad" Advanced Parameters ~deadzone (double, default: Amount joystick move considered off-center. parameter relative axis normalized means joystick move edge axis’s range axis output non-zero Linux deadzone processing, cases set ~autorepeat_rate (double, default: (disabled)) Rate joystick non-changing state resend previously message. ~coalesce_interval (double, default: Axis events received coalesce_interval (seconds) single ROS message. kernel sends axis motion separate event, coalescing greatly reduces rate messages option limit rate outgoing messages. Button events immediately avoid missing button presses. Information information Wiki page.

description:
The wiimote package allows ROS nodes to communicate with a Nintendo Wiimote
    and its related peripherals, including the Nunchuk, Motion Plus, and
    (experimentally) the Classic. The package implements a ROS node that uses
    Bluetooth to communicate with the Wiimote device, obtaining accelerometer
    and gyro data, the state of LEDs, the IR camera, rumble (vibrator),
    buttons, joystick, and battery state. The node additionally enables ROS
    nodes to control the Wiimote's LEDs and vibration for feedback to the human
    Wiimote operator. LEDs and vibration may be switched on and off, or made to
    operate according to a timed pattern.
authors: Andreas Paepcke, Melonee Wise, Mark Horn
readme: Wiimote Nodes Additional Documentation Testing procedure ROS Wiki Tutorials Teleop Wiimote wiimote_node.py Original Python version wiimote node. wiimote_node implementation designed focus reduced resource consumption. Default: Differences Python Implementation “/wiimote/nunchuk” “/wiimote/classic” topics published Nunchuk Classic Controller connected wiimote Wiimote data polled controller data required publish topic subscriber. Calibration joysticks (Nunchuk Classic Controller) polled find current position assumed center. joysticks rest theoretical center position. Joysticks (Nunchuk Classic Controller) minimum maximum values start theoretical minimum/maximum values dynamically updated joystick full range values reached joysticks due physical limitations. adjust, full range reached joysticks. Joysticks (Nunchuk Classic Controller) center deadzone scaled based granularity joystick; python code assumed Connecting accessory (Nunchuk, Classic Controller, MotionPlus) automatically start calibration sequence.

description: The respeaker_ros package
readme: respeaker_ros ROS Package Respeaker Mic Array Supported Devices Respeaker Mic Array Preparation Install package Assumed ROS properly installed. install package apt-get. sudo apt-get install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-respeaker-ros build source. mkdir ~/catkin_ws/src ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_3rdparty.git ~/catkin_ws source /opt/ros/kinetic/setup.bash rosdep install --from-paths src catkin config --init catkin build respeaker_ros source ~/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash Register respeaker udev rules access USB device permission user space. udev, give permission respeaker device automatically. run command followings install setting file: roscd respeaker_ros sudo $(rospack find respeaker_ros)/config/60-respeaker.rules /etc/udev/rules.d/60-respeaker.rules sudo systemctl restart udev re-connect device. Run generator script. rosrun respeaker_ros respeaker_gencfg.py updated configuration file $(rospack find respeaker_ros)/cfg/Respeaker.cfg. description: ROS wrapper for Python SpeechRecognition library

Subscribing Topics ~audio_topic (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) Audio stream data recognized. Topis set parameter ~audio_topic default audio. Parameters ~voice_topic (String, default: speech_to_text) Publishing voice topic ~audio_topic (String, default: audio) Subscribing audio topic ~enable_sound_effect (Bool, default: True) Flag enable disable sound play sound recognition. ~language (String, default: en-US) Language recognized ~engine (Enum[String], default: Google) Speech-to-text engine full options dynamic_reconfigure) ~energy_threshold (Double, default: Threshold Voice activity detection ~dynamic_energy_threshold (Bool, default: True) Adaptive estimation energy_threshold ~dynamic_energy_adjustment_damping (Double, default: Damping threshold dynamic VAD ~dynamic_energy_ratio (Double, default: Energy ratio dynamic VAD ~pause_threshold (Double, default: Seconds non-speaking audio phrase considered complete ~operation_timeout (Double, default: Seconds internal operation API request) starts times ~listen_timeout (Double, default: maximum number seconds wait phrase start giving ~phrase_time_limit (Double, default: maximum number seconds phrase continue stopping returning part phrase processed time limit reached ~phrase_threshold (Double, default: Minimum seconds speaking audio speaking audio phrase ~non_speaking_duration (Double, default: Seconds non-speaking audio sides recording ~duration (Double, default: Seconds waiting speech ~depth (Int, default: Depth audio signal ~n_channel (Int, default: Total number channels audio data mono, stereo) ~sample_rate (Int, default: Sample rate audio signal ~buffer_size (Int, default: Maximum buffer size store audio data speech recognition ~start_signal (String, default: /usr/share/sounds/freedesktop/stereo/bell.ogg) Path sound file bell start audio ~recognized_signal (String, default: /usr/share/sounds/freedesktop/stereo/message.ogg) Path sound file bell audio ~success_signal (String, default: /usr/share/sounds/freedesktop/stereo/message-new-instant.ogg) Path sound file bell successful recognition result ~timeout_signal (String, Explicit implicit. Simple complex. Complex complicated. Flat nested. Sparse dense. Readability counts. Special cases special break rules. practicality beats purity. Errors pass silently. explicitly silenced. face ambiguity, refuse temptation guess. preferably --obvious obvious Dutch. implementation hard explain, bad idea. implementation easy explain, good idea. Namespaces honking great idea Tweet text image tweet images. base64 image path inserted text, jump reply section. Image path wget https://github.com/k-okada.png /tmp/k-okada.png rostopic pub /tweet std_msgs/String "/tmp/k-okada.png" Base64 tweet image encoding base64. python. concatenate multiple base64 images spaces. import rospy import import std_msgs.msg import numpy import matplotlib.cm rostwitter.cv_util import extract_media_from_text rostwitter.cv_util import encode_image_cv2 rospy.init_node('rostwitter_sample') pub rospy.Publisher('/tweet', std_msgs.msg.String, queue_size=1) rospy.sleep(3.0) colormap matplotlib.cm.get_cmap('hsv') text 'Tweet images. description: A simple viewer for ROS image topics with draw-on features
readme: image_view2 image_view2 extended image_view. Features Draw markers image view Interact user direction Interaction Modes image_view2 support mode interact user. set interaction mode ~interaction_mode parameter change mode service interfaces. Rectangle mode (~interaction_mode:=rectangle) rectangle mode, user select region-of-interest dragging mouse pointer. Selected regions published image/screenrectangle (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) topic. user clicks view dragging pointer, clicked position published image/screenpoint (geometry_msgs/PointStamped) topic. Line mode (~interaction_mode:=line) line mode, user select line dragging mouse pointer. Selected line published image/line (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) topic. Polygon mode (~interaction_mode:=poly) polygon mode, user select series closed lines. User add line left-click close lines right-click. Selected polygon published image/poly (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) topic. Grabcut mode (~interaction_mode:=grabcut) grabcut mode, user select curves. typing Esc key, clear selected curves. selected red curve published image/foreground (sensor_msgs/Image) green publiehd image/background (sensor_msgs/Image). mode originally implemented select forground background pixels grabcut. Grabcut Rectangle mode (~interaction_mode:=grabcut_rect) grabcut rectangle mode, user select rectangles. typing Esc key, clear selected rectangles. selected red rectangle published image/foreground_rect (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) green publiehd image/background_rect (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped). mode originally implemented select forground background regions grabcut. ROS API Publising Topics image/marked (sensor_msgs/Image) Image marks drawed. image/screenrectangle_image (sensor_msgs/Image) Cropped image user selection Rectangle mode. readme: jsk_rosbag_tools Tools creating video rosbag compressing rosbag images. bag_to_video.py Create video rosbag. Usage usage: bag_to_video.py [--fps FPS] [--samplerate SAMPLERATE] [--channels CHANNELS] [--audio-topic AUDIO_TOPIC] [--image-topic IMAGE_TOPIC [IMAGE_TOPIC input_bagfile rosbag video positional arguments: input_bagfile optional arguments: show message exit output directory path filename. --image-topic interpreted directory file --fps FPS --samplerate SAMPLERATE, SAMPLERATE sampling rate --channels CHANNELS number input channels --audio-topic AUDIO_TOPIC --image-topic IMAGE_TOPIC [IMAGE_TOPIC Topic extract. rosrun jsk_rosbag_tools bag_to_video.py $(rospack find jsk_rosbag_tools)/samples/data/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.bag --samplerate --channels --audio-topic /audio --image-topic /head_camera/rgb/throttled/image_rect_color/compressed /tmp/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.mp4 bag_to_audio.py Create audio file rosbag. Usage usage: bag_to_audio.py [--samplerate SAMPLERATE] [--channels CHANNELS] [--audio-topic AUDIO_TOPIC] input_bagfile rosbag audio positional arguments: input_bagfile optional arguments: show message exit output filename. `--audio-topic`_info exists, samplerate channels. --samplerate SAMPLERATE, SAMPLERATE sampling rate --channels CHANNELS number input channels --audio-topic AUDIO_TOPIC rosrun jsk_rosbag_tools bag_to_audio.py $(rospack find jsk_rosbag_tools)/samples/data/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.bag --samplerate --channels --audio-topic /audio /tmp/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.wav video_to_bag.py Convert video file bagfile. Usage usage: video_to_bag.py output_file] [--topic-name TOPIC_NAME] [--compress] [--no-progress-bar] inputvideo Convert video bag. positional arguments: inputvideo optional arguments: show message exit output_file, output_file output bag file --topic-name TOPIC_NAME Converted topic --compress Compress Image flag. --no-progress-bar show progress bar. rosrun jsk_rosbag_tools video_to_bag.py /tmp/output_bag/head_camera--slash--rgb--slash--throttled--slash--image_rect_color--slash--compressed-with-audio.mp4 /tmp/output_bag/video.bag --compress compress_imgs.py Convert Image messages CompressedImage CompressedDepthImage. Usage usage: compress_imgs.py [--compressed-topics [COMPRESSED_TOPICS [COMPRESSED_TOPICS [--replace] [--no-progress-bar] input_bagfile Convert Image messages CompressedImage CompressedDepthImage positional arguments: input_bagfile input bagfile path optional arguments: show message exit output bagfile path --compressed-topics [COMPRESSED_TOPICS [COMPRESSED_TOPICS image topics compressed --replace --no-progress-bar show progress bar. rosrun jsk_rosbag_tools compress_imgs.py $(rospack find jsk_rosbag_tools)/samples/data/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.bag /tmp/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag-compressed.bag tf_static_to_tf.py Convert tf_static save rosbag. usage: tf_static_to_tf.py [--no-progress-bar] input_bagfile Convert tf_static save rosbag positional arguments: input_bagfile input bagfile path optional arguments: show message exit output bagfile path --no-progress-bar show progress bar. rosrun jsk_rosbag_tools tf_static_to_tf.py $(rospack find jsk_rosbag_tools)/samples/data/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.bag Note jsk_topic_tools static_tf_republisher.py republish /tf_static rosbag file. tf_static_to_tf.py approach rewrite rosbag file. merge.py Merges bagfiles. Usage usage: merge.py output_file] [--topics TOPICS] main_bagfile bagfile Merges bagfiles. positional arguments: main_bagfile path bagfile, main bagfile bagfile path bagfile merged main bagfile optional arguments: show message exit output_file, output_file output file --topics TOPICS, TOPICS topics merged main bag reindex bagfile rosrun jsk_rosbag_tools merge.py $(rospack find jsk_rosbag_tools)/samples/data/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.bag $(rospack find jsk_rosbag_tools)/samples/data/2022-05-07-hello-test.bag

The cmd_vel_smoother package
readme: cmd_vel_smoother Interpolate velocity command published periodically acceleration deceleration limit. Basically node interpolates velocity violate acceleration limit decelating received velocity. control loop base_controller cmd_vel seconds, controller stops moving motors safety. move_base node publishes cmd_vel continues publishing, controllers send signal motors stop “rattle” moves suddenly stops repeatedly) damages motors hardwares. Parameters ~x_acc_lim (Double, default: limit x-axis translational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~y_acc_lim (Double, default: limit y-axis translational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~yaw_acc_lim (Double, default: limit z-axis rotational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~desired_rate (Double, default: node interpolates velocity commands rate ~interpolate_max_frame (Int, default: Max message count published received velocity command. Discussion package originated discussion https://github.com/ros-planning/navigation/issues/405 looked detail implementation similar nodes turtlebot robots exist: https://github.com/yujinrobot/yujin_ocs/tree/devel/yocs_velocity_smoother Author Yuki Furuta «furushchev@jsk.imi.i.u-tokyo.ac.jp»

description: The contact_states_observer package
readme: contact_states_observer Euslisp server check grasping contact states. Grasp state checking grasping state checking, hand joint angle topics. users configure setting :set-grasp-state-param joint angles success-candidates failure-candidates checked joint names. observer program checks difference candidates current hand joint angles find nearest candidate. Finally, observer program publishes results users obtain :get-contact-states. Setting parameter (list (list :rarm Success failure candidates (list (list :success (list :fail Checked joint names (list rarm setting (list :larm larm setting list Contact force checking contact force state checking, force sensor thresholding. Observer program checks current force exceeds threshold. Observer program publishes results users obtain :get-contact-states. real robot Start real robot (rtcd ros bridge servoOn) Run program roseus (progn (ros::roseus "test_grasp_check") ROBOT Initialization. Set reset-pose. return '("On" (send *robot* :hand :rarm :reset-pose) (send :hand-angle-vector (send *robot* :hand-angle-vector) (send :hand-wait-interpolation) (unix:usleep Wait hand motion stopping (format '(:rarm :larm) (send csc :get-contact-states '(:rarm :larm))) Test2. Set hook-pose. return '("Off" (send *robot* :hand :rarm :hook-pose) (send :hand-angle-vector (send *robot* :hand-angle-vector) (send :hand-wait-interpolation) (unix:usleep Wait hand motion stopping (format '(:rarm :larm) (send csc :get-contact-states '(:rarm :larm))) Publishing Topics ore_contact_states (contact_states_observer/OREContactStates) Array object, robot, environment contact state. contact state consists rleg, rarm,.. state On/Off grasping, foot, hand contact) mode grasp, contact-force, contact_grasp_states (hrpsys_ros_bridge/ContactStatesStamped) Contact states contact point rleg, lleg, rarm, larm) grasp_states (contact_states_observer/GraspState) Grasp state

description: jsk_teleop_joy
readme: jsk_teleop_joy package Introduction package physical teleoperatoin joy sticks, game controllers MIDI instruments. Supported game controllers package supports game controllers: XBox360 controller Controller (Bluetooth) Controller (USB) order controller Bluetooth, instruction. Architecture jsk_teleop_joy jsk_teleop_joy main script joy_main.py) based plugin architecture. ROS packages plugins jsk_teleop_joy plugin represents function pose effector”. enable plugins choose plugin “Pushing Select Button”. jsk_teleop_joy plugins box VerboseStatus JoyPose6D JoyFootstep JoyFootstepPlanner JoyFootstepPlannerDemo JoyMoveIt JoyGoPos implement plugin write xml file define plugins. Export xml file ROS package manifest.xml package.xml. implement plugin Write xml file define plugins jsk_teleop_joy reads plugin definition xml file plugin.xml. xml <library> <class name="Foo" type="your_package.foo"> </class> </library> xml file <library> tag top level. define plugins <class> tag. attribute means plugin. choose plugins launch files type attribute means python class plugin. jsk_teleop_joy instantiate plugin class thie type Export xml file ROS package export xml file <export> tag <jsk_teleop_joy> tag manifest.xml package.xml. files, manifest.xml, package.xml, good examples. Implement plugin Finally, implement jsk_teleop_joy plugin. VerboseStatus Plugin good implement plugin. __init__ method plugins required inherits jsk_teleop_joy.joy_plugin.JSKJoyPlugin call JSKJoyPlugin.__init__ __init__ constructor. class VerboseStatus(jsk_teleop_joy.joy_plugin.JSKJoyPlugin): def __init__(self): jsk_teleop_joy.joy_plugin.JSKJoyPlugin.__init__(self, 'VerbosePlugin') joyCB method time jsk_teleop_joy receives /joy message, calls joyCB method active plugin. def joyCB(self, status, history): rospy.loginfo('analog left (status.left_analog_x, status.left_analog_y)) argument joyCB instance JoyStatus. JoyStatus XboxStatus, PS3Status PS3WiredStatus. means latest message /joy. classes provide interface don’t care controller user hand, argument joyCB (history), sequence JoyStatus. instance StatusHistory. means hisotry JoyStatus. SELECT button jsk_teleop_joy system occupies SELECT button SELECT button plugin. SELECT button switching plugins. MIDI controllers interactive_midi_config.py configure MIDI devices script interactively. movie Connect MIDI device. script asks device, choose device push buttons/slide faders order sensor_msgs/Joy. script maps buttons sensor_msgs/Joy/axes sensor_msgs/Joy/buttons type quit. configure saved /tmp/midi.yaml. Troubleshooting: Ubuntu18.04 pygame2.0.1 installed pip, python "import pygame.midi; pygame.midi.init()" returns error ALSA lib conf.c:3558:(snd_config_hooks_call) open shared library libasound_module_conf_pulse.so (/usr/lib/alsa-lib/libasound_module_conf_pulse.so: libasound_module_conf_pulse.so: open shared object file: file directory) ALSA lib seq.c:935:(snd_seq_open_noupdate) Unknown SEQ default case, pygame apt pip uninstall pygame apt-get install python-pygame RECOMMENDED: pip installed pygame, symbolic link /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/alsa-lib /usr/lib/alsa-lib: RECOMMED midi_write.py order control LEDs active faders, output MIDI commands computer. script midi_write.py helps dig command save configuration yaml file option. midi_config_player.py script publishes sensor_msgs/Joy /joy based yaml file configured interactive_midi_config.py midi_write.py. configs directory includes yaml files MIDI devices.

description: Convert audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) to spectrogram (sensor_msgs/Image)
readme: audio_to_spectrogram package converts audio data time-series data) spectrum spectrogram data. Usage command, publish audio, spectrum spectrogram topics. set correct args microphone configuration, mic_sampling_rate bitdepth. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram audio_to_spectrogram.launch data conversion pipeline audio_to_spectrum.py spectrum normalized_half_spectrum log_spectrum preprocess node(s) preprocessed spectrum spectrum_to_spectrogram.py spectrogram rosbag audio. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram sample_audio_to_spectrogram.launch Audio Amplitude Spectrum Spectrogram convert data audio spectrum spectrogram data package. rosbag force torque sensor sensing drill vibration. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram sample_wrench_to_spectrogram.launch Z-axis Force Amplitude Normalized Spectrum Spectrogram Source Spectrum Spectrogram Scripts audio_to_spectrum.py script convert audio spectrum. Publishing topics ~spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data calculated audio FFT. usual “amplitude spectrum”. https://ryo-iijima.com/fftresult/ details. ~normalized_half_spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data non-negative frequencies (0Hz-Nyquist frequency)) “normalized” (consistent amplitude original signal). details. https://ryo-iijima.com/fftresult/ https://stackoverflow.com/questions/63211851/why-divide-the-output-of-numpy-fft-by-n https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition/issues/2761#issue-1550715400 ~log_spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Log-scaled spectrum data. calculated applying log absolute FFT result. log applied “power spectrum”, don’t simplicity. details. https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition/issues/2761#issuecomment-1445810380 http://makotomurakami.com/blog/2020/05/23/5266/ Subscribing topics ~audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) Audio stream data microphone. audio format wave. Parameters ~mic_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate microphone. sampling rate audio topic. ~fft_sampling_period (Double, default: Period sample audio data FFT. ~n_channel (Int, default: Number channel microphone. ~target_channel (Int, default: Target channel. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits audio data. ~fft_exec_rate (Double, default: Rate execute FFT publish results. data_to_spectrum.py Generalized version audio_to_spectrum.py. script convert multiple message types spectrum. Publishing topics audio_to_spectrum.py. Subscribing topics ~input (AnyMsg) Topic message including data convert spectrum published.
