readme: Sound Classification ROS package classify sound stream. Contents Setup Usage Quick demo Setup Install ROS. Ubuntu Ubuntu Create workspace mkdir ~/sound_classification_ws/src ~/sound_classification_ws/src git clone https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition.git rosdep install --from-paths --ignore-src catkin build sound_classification source ~/sound_classification_ws/devel/setup.bash Install packages. cuda cupy needed chainer. installation guide JSK GPU highly recommended. Usage Check microphone parameters. device, n_channel, bitdepth mic_sampling_rate bash commands params device. card device device:="hw:0,0" arecord List CAPTURE Hardware Devices card PCH [HDA Intel PCH], device ALC293 Analog [ALC293 Analog] Subdevices: Subdevice subdevice n_channel, bitdepth sample_rate, Note sources means input microphone) sinks means output speaker) pactl list short sources alsa_input.pci-0000_00_1f.3.analog-stereo module-alsa-card.c s16le SUSPENDED Pass params launch file arguments launching `device:=hw:0,0 n_channel:=2 bitdepth:=16 mic_sampling_rate:=44100`). `/audio` topic computer publish `/audio`, set `use_microphone:=false` launch file launching. Save environmental noise train_data/noise.npy. subtracting noise, spectrograms clear. script, give sound sensor. update noise data everytime sound recognition, environmental sound differs everytime. noise samples roslaunch sound_classification save_noise.launch Publish audio spectrum spectrogram topics. set max/min frequency included spectrum high_cut_freq/low_cut_freq args audio_to_spectrogram.launch. gui:=true, spectrum spectrogram visualized. roslaunch sound_classification audio_to_spectrogram.launch gui:=true spectrogram quiet environment. Horiozntal axis time Vertical axis frequency |Spectrogram noise subtraction|Spectrogram noise subtraction| |![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86824253-d9e6df80-c0c8-11ea-8946-ca1367c1b1b0.png)|![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86824246-d81d1c00-c0c8-11ea-8c13-dc9660e89ea0.png)| Collect spectrogram classify. volume exceeds threshold, save spectrogram train_data/original_spectrogram/TARGET_CLASS. rosbag stream sound sources. Rosbag version (Recommended) recommend rosbag collect spectrograms. rosbag easy save_sound.launch parameters. target_class:=TARGET_CLASS, set class target sound. use_rosbag:=true filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG, save spectrograms rosbag. default, rosbag paused Press ‘Space’ key terminal start playing rosbag. rosbag ends, press ‘Ctrl-c’ terminate. newly saved spectrograms appended existing spectrograms. change threshold sound saving threshold:=xxx. smaller easily sound saved. Save audio rosbag roslaunch sound_classification record_audio_rosbag.launch filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG play rosbag collecting data export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://localhost:11311 roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG target_class:=TARGET_CLASS threshold:=0.5 setting `threshold:=0` `save_when_sound:=false`, collect spectrogram sound". play rosbag collecting no-sound data export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://localhost:11311 roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG target_class:=no_sound threshold:=0 save_when_sound:=false Stream version Recommended) collect spectrogram audio topic stream. `use_rosbag:=true`. args rosbag version. roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch save_when_sound:=true target_class:=TARGET_CLASS threshold:=0.5 save_data_rate:=5 Create dateaset chainer saved spectrograms. data augmentation executed. --number means maximum images class dataset. rosrun sound_classification create_dataset.py --number Visualize dataset. train arg train dataset (augmented dataset), test arg test dataset. spectrograms dataset visualized random order. rosrun sound_classification visualize_dataset.py test train/test Train dataset. Default model NIN (Recommended). vgg16, pretrained weights VGG16 downloaded scripts/VGG_ILSVRC_16_layers.npz time run script. rosrun sound_classification train.py --epoch Classify sounds. takes seconds neural network weights loaded. use_rosbag:=true filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG classify sound rosbag. roslaunch sound_classification classify_sound.launch fix class names' color classification result image order class names <rosparam> target_names: chip_bag] </rosparam> classification result: |no_sound|applause|voice| |![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86828259-ed487980-c0cd-11ea-9f51-7ccb0bc93321.png)|![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86828249-ec174c80-c0cd-11ea-854c-da1b0fa08e33.png)|![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86828260-ede11000-c0cd-11ea-9b4e-94ee7b1c1a5f.png)| Quick demo Sound classification demo laptop’s built-in microphone. create dataset rosbag files sample_rosbag/ directory. Classification gif Commands Setup environment write Microphone settings Usage section) Save environmental noise roslaunch sound_classification save_noise.launch Collect spectrograms sample rosbags. Press ‘Space’ start rosbag. no_sound class roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=$(rospack find sound_classification)/sample_rosbag/no_sound.bag target_class:=no_sound threshold:=0 save_when_sound:=false applause class roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=$(rospack find sound_classification)/sample_rosbag/applause.bag target_class:=applause threshold:=0.5 voice class roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=$(rospack find sound_classification)/sample_rosbag/voice.bag target_class:=voice threshold:=0.5 Create dataset rosrun sound_classification create_dataset.py --number Train (takes minites) rosrun sound_classification train.py --epoch Classify sound roslaunch sound_classification classify_sound.launch

description:
roseus_smach

     * Euslisp state machine class. it will be moved.
     * Message publisher for visualizing current state by smach_viewer.
     * Simple pickle dump script for debugging state machine.
     * Execute state machine as a action server.
readme: roseus_smach package includes euslisp implementation state machine smach. requirements roseus smach smach_viewer Optional visualization. install package manually: sudo apt install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-smach-viewer sample Sample codes sample directory. rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l simple state machine rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l (exec-smach-simple) nested state machine rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l (exec-smach-nested) state machine userdata rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l (exec-smach-userdata) sample/parallel-state-machine-sample.l state machine parallel action execution rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach parallel-state-machine-sample.l (demo) Writing Simple Smach(state-machine) codes #!/usr/bin/env roseus (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") (setq count (defun func-foo (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state FOO~%") (cond count (incf count) :outcome1) :outcome2))) (defun func-bar (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state BAR~%") :outcome2) (defun smach-simple (instance state-machine :init))) (send :add-node (instance state :init :FOO 'func-foo)) (send :add-node (instance state :init :BAR 'func-bar)) goal-states generated method (send :goal-state (list :outcome4 :outcome5)) select node start-node (send :start-state :FOO) nodes selected symbol (send :add-transition :FOO :BAR :outcome1) (send :add-transition :FOO :outcome4 :outcome2) (send :add-transition :BAR :FOO :outcome2) (send (smach-simple) :execute nil) Code Explained (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") line imports state-machine class, state class, transition class. (defun func-foo (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state FOO~%") (cond count (incf count) :outcome1) :outcome2))) (defun func-bar (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state BAR~%") :outcome2) lines define functions hooked state. Note functions called argument, alist declared user-data arguments, return equal transition action) state. (instance state-machine :init)) line creates state-machine instance binds (send :add-node (instance state :init :FOO 'func-foo)) line creates state add node state machine. create state instance, state hook state function. lisp object state names. case, state :FOO hooked func-foo. (send :goal-state (list :outcome4 :outcome5)) (send :start-state :FOO) lines define goal state(s) start state(s) state machine. create multiple start states list state, define multiple goal state. Goal state(s) returns Note state machine state. goal state considered outcome state machine. (send :add-transition :FOO :BAR :outcome1) line defines transition. arguments :add-transition method OUTCOME. line means “adding transition FOO BAR, FOO node returns outcome1”. (send (smach-simple) :execute nil) line sets execute state machine. order view check progress smach_viewer, function exec-state-machine Writing Nested Smach codes add child state-machine node state-machine. #!/usr/bin/env roseus (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") (defun smach-nested ((sm-top (instance state-machine :init)) (sm-sub (instance state-machine :init))) state instance include state-machine function (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init sm-sub)) (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init "BAS" 'func-bas)) (send sm-top :goal-state :outcome5) (send sm-top :start-state "BAS") (send sm-top :add-transition "BAS" :outcome3) (send sm-top :add-transition :outcome5 :outcome4) node instance args :add-node, :start-state, :add-transition ((foo-node (instance state :init "FOO" 'func-foo)) (bar-node (instance state :init "BAR" 'func-bar))) (send sm-sub :add-node foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-node bar-node) (send sm-sub :goal-state :outcome4) (send sm-sub :start-state foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node bar-node :outcome1) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node :outcome4 :outcome2) (send sm-sub :add-transition bar-node foo-node :outcome2)) sm-top (send (smach-nested) :execute nil) Code Explained #!/usr/bin/env roseus (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") previous line imports state-machine class, state class, transition class. ((sm-top (instance state-machine :init)) (sm-sub (instance state-machine :init))) line creates state-machine instance. sm-sub state machine acting state node) sm-top. easy understand state machine function: state machine called, executed, state machine processing eventually returns goal state return function. (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init sm-sub)) sm-sub instance hooked node sm-top line. line add state machine state function. (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init "BAS" 'func-bas)) (send sm-top :goal-state :outcome5) (send sm-top :start-state "BAS") (send sm-top :add-transition "BAS" :outcome3) lines define node, goal-state, start-state, transition sm-top. (send sm-top :add-transition :outcome5 :outcome4) Remember goal state(s) state-machine returns outcome return sm-sub goal state(s). goal state sm-sub :outcome4, return sm-sub :outcome4. line adds transition :outcome5 node returns :outcome4. ((foo-node (instance state :init "FOO" 'func-foo)) (bar-node (instance state :init "BAR" 'func-bar))) (send sm-sub :add-node foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-node bar-node) (send sm-sub :goal-state :outcome4) (send sm-sub :start-state foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node bar-node :outcome1) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node :outcome4 :outcome2) (send sm-sub :add-transition bar-node foo-node :outcome2)) lines define behavior sm-sub detail previous simple state machine Note (send sm-sub :goal-state :outcome4) defines goal state, defines return state machine. (send (smach-nested) :execute nil) Finally, sm-top executed Writing Simple Smach (make-state-machine) make-state-machine function easy-way define simple state machine. requires graph-list, func-map, initial-state, goal-states arguments. simple state machine written (defun smach-simple2 (setq (make-state-machine define graph, list (<from-node> <transition> <to-node>) <transition> corresponds node returns nil. '((:foo :outcome2 :outcome4) (:foo :outcome1 :bar) (:bar :outcome2 :foo)) define function map '((:foo 'func-foo) foo returns :outcome1 times returns :outcome2 (:bar 'func-bar)) bar returns :outcome2 initial state '(:foo) goal state '(:outcome4))))) node :foo :bar :outcome4 terminate node. node corresponds 'func-foo 'func-bar functions. function 'func-foo returns :outcome1 times returns :outcome2. function 'func-bar returns :outcome2. (:foo :outcome2 :outcome4) means :foo returns :outcome2, transit :outcome4. (:foo :outcome1 :bar) means :foo returns :outcome1, transit :bar. (:bar :outcome2 :foo) means :bar returns :outcome2, transit :foo. simplify state machine definition, recommend users t/nil return node, users able (:foo :outcome4) graph definition. (defun smach-simple3 (setq (make-state-machine '((:foo :outcome4) (:foo :bar) (:bar :foo)) '((:foo '(lambda (&rest args) (cond count (incf count) nil) foo returns nil times returns (:bar '(lambda (&rest args) bar returns '(:foo) '(:outcome4))))) tested roscd roseus_smach/sample roseus state-machine-ros-sample.l "(progn (setq count 0)(exec-state-machine (smach-simple2)))" roseus state-machine-ros-sample.l "(progn (setq count 0)(exec-state-machine (smach-simple3)))" check state machine behavior rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py` Writing Nested Smach (make-state-machine) write nested state machine make-state-machie function. add state-machine function maps, added node (defun smach-simple-nested (sm-top sm-sub) (setq sm-sub (make-state-machine '((:foo :outcome2 :outcome4) transitions (:foo :outcome1 :bar) (:bar :outcome2 :foo)) '((:foo 'func-foo) function maps (:bar 'func-bar)) '(:foo) initial '(:outcome4) goal (setq sm-top (make-state-machine '((:bas :outcome3 transitions :outcome4 :outcome5)) `((:bas 'func-bas) functon maps ,sm-sub)) set "nestaed state machine" '(:bas) initial '(:outcome5) goal sm-top)) roseus write '((:bas 'func-bas) functon maps sm-sub)) set "nestaed state machine" careful add machine. `(backquote) (comma). tested roscd roseus_smach/sample roseus state-machine-ros-sample.l "(progn (setq count 0)(exec-state-machine (smach-simple-nested)))"

Integrate robot’s perception query objects task planning system easily manage facts. Persistent storage facts map geometry backed local PostgreSQL database Convenient APIs common queries, instances type object Link code, import Python binding dependent ROS, easy integrate ROS projects Representation Knowledge broken concepts, instances attributes. Attributes tie entities store data attribute type. apple instance instance. attribute named is_on type entity_id, relates entity Attributes type entity_id called relations relate entities Attributes types entity_id function properties; relation is_graspable type bool storing simple fact entity. Poses special kind instance store coordinate direction. Regions special kind instance store list coordinates defining closed region. PostgreSQL preferred backend. Usage Integrate knowledge_representation API store facts observations robot API retrieve facts masse plan inspect application. Loading Knowledge Scripts bulk loading knowledge. helpful load map annotations, ontology initialize robot’s knowledge. populate_with_map loads SVG map annotations (marked image, ROS-style map). test/resources SVG files. future, provide simple browser-based annotator populate_with_[knowledge|owl|xml] support loading kinds ontologies. description: Provides nodes to assemble point clouds from either LaserScan or PointCloud messages
ROS Libraries for filtering specific kinds of laser scans
Filters a laser scan to remove points that are in the current static map

Assorted filters designed to operate on 2D planar laser scanners,
    which use the sensor_msgs/LaserScan type.

This package contains a class for converting from a 2D laser scan as defined by
    sensor_msgs/LaserScan into a point cloud as defined by sensor_msgs/PointCloud
    or sensor_msgs/PointCloud2. In particular, it contains functionality to account
    for the skew resulting from moving robots or tilting laser scanners.
authors: Tully Foote, Radu Bogdan Rusu, William Woodall

description: Library for interfacing to USB devices

The Las Vegas Surface Reconstruction Toolkit is an Open Source toolkit to reconstruct triangle meshes from unordered point clouds. It supports the generation of textured models either using colored point clouds or point clouds plus images and according calibration matrices. The LVR toolkit provides an Open Source C++ API for meshing and texture generation as well as an I/O interface to store the generated meshes in different exchange formats including Stanford PLY, Wavefront OBJ and Collada. In contrast to other meshing software, it focuses on reconstruction of large scale environments on city scale from high resolution point clouds.
authors: Thomas Wiemann, Sebastian Pütz, Alexander Mock, Lars Kiesow, Lukas Kalbertodt, Tristan Igelbrink, Johan M. von Behren, Dominik Feldschnieders, Alexander Löhr

The marker_msgs package contains messages usable to setup a marker/fiducial system. 
    The package distinguishes between two types of messages. 
    First messages to describe the properties of a marker/fiducial detection system and the detected markers. 
    Secondly messages used to represent a map of markers/features with covariances as it would be produced by a SLAM system or published by a map server for self-localization.

MAVLink message marshaling library.
  This package provides C-headers and C++11 library
  for both 1.0 and 2.0 versions of protocol.

  For pymavlink use separate install via rosdep (python-pymavlink).
readme: MAVLink MAVLink Micro Air Vehicle Message Marshalling Library. MAVLink lightweight, header-only message library communication drones and/or ground control stations. consists primarily message-set specifications systems (“dialects”) defined XML files, Python tools convert source code supported languages. additional Python scripts providing examples utilities working MAVLink data. Tip MAVLink suited applications limited communication bandwidth. reference implementation highly optimized resource-constrained systems limited RAM flash memory. field-proven deployed products serves interoperability interface components manufacturers. Quick start Generate headers install minimal MAVLink environment Ubuntu LTS enter terminal: Dependencies sudo apt install python3-pip Clone mavlink directory choice git clone https://github.com/mavlink/mavlink.git --recursive mavlink python3 pip install pymavlink/requirements.txt build MAVLink2 C-library message_definitions/v1.0/common.xml /mavlink directory shown: python3 pymavlink.tools.mavgen --lang=C --wire-protocol=2.0 --output=generated/include/mavlink/v2.0 message_definitions/v1.0/common.xml cmake include headers cmake, install locally, directory install: cmake -Bbuild -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=install -DMAVLINK_DIALECT=common -DMAVLINK_VERSION=2.0 cmake --build build --target install find_package dependency CMakeLists.txt: find_package(MAVLink REQUIRED) add_executable(my_program my_program.c) target_link_libraries(my_program PRIVATE MAVLink::mavlink) pass local install directory cmake (adapt directory structure): ../my_program cmake -Bbuild -DCMAKE_PREFIX_PATH=../mavlink/install full check examples/c. Note: target_link_libraries cmake, doesn’t “link” MAVLink header-only library. instructions Instructions libraries covered MAVLink Libraries (mavgen). Note: Installing MAVLink Toolchain explains install MAVLink Ubuntu platforms Windows, Generating MAVLink Libraries explains build MAVLink programming languages supported project. sub-topics MAVLink Libraries explain generated libraries. Key Links Documentation/Website (mavlink.io/en/) Discussion/Support Contributing License

description: 3-D/6-DOF localization for mobile robots with 3-D LIDAR(s)
readme: mcl_3dl Package summary mcl_3dl ROS node perform probabilistic 3-D/6-DOF localization system mobile robots LIDAR(s). implements pointcloud based Monte Carlo localization reference pointcloud map. node receives reference pointcloud environment map localizes 6-DOF yaw, pitch, roll) pose measured pointclouds assisted motion prediction odometry. supported motion model differential-wheeled-robot. node classic MCL; doesn’t implement adaptive feature KDL-sampling Algorithms fundamental algorithm mcl_3dl node Monte Carlo localization (MCL), aka particle filter localization. MCL represents probabilistic distribution estimated pose density weight particles estimates pose distribution. Algorithm details Parameters Node Install source Note: mcl_3dl_msgs package required build mcl_3dl package. clone /path/to/your/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/at-wat/mcl_3dl.git git clone https://github.com/at-wat/mcl_3dl_msgs.git build /path/to/your/catkin_ws rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src Install dependencies catkin_make -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release Release build recommended apt repository ROS Indigo/Kinetic/Lunar Ubuntu) sudo apt-get install ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-mcl-3dl Running demo bag file 2+4-DOF tracked vehicle Hokuyo YVT-X002 LIDAR online. Pre-processed (filtered) pointcloud, IMU pose, odometry, map data packed bag. Download bag wget ~/Downloads https://openspur.org/~atsushi.w/dataset/mcl_3dl/short_test3.bag Running demo roslaunch mcl_3dl test.launch use_pointcloud_map:=false use_cad_map:=false use_bag_file:=true bag_file:=${HOME}/Downloads/short_test3.bag map data bag generated cartographer_ros filtered pcl_outlier_removal pcl_voxel_grid utilities. MarkerArray shows mcl_3dl internal information. Purple spheres: sampled points likelihood-model calculation Red lines: casted rays beam-model calculation Red boxes: detected collisions raycasting global localization, call /global_localization command. rosservice call /global_localization Demos odometry IMU Contributing mcl_3dl package developed GitHub flow. Feel free open Issue and/or Pull Request. code repository ROS Style Guide. configuration file clang-format https://github.com/seqsense/ros_style/. License mcl_3dl BSD license. Backport codes Point Cloud Library (PCL) BSD license.

description: The mongodb_store_msgs package

This package provides common interfaces for navigation specific robot actions. It contains the AbstractPlanner, AbstractController and AbstractRecovery plugin interfaces. This interfaces have to be implemented by the plugins to make the plugin available for Move Base Flex. The abstract classes provides a meaningful interface enabling the planners, controllers and recovery behaviors to return information, e.g. why something went wrong. Derivided interfaces can, for example, provide methods to initialize the planner, controller or recovery with map representations like costmap_2d, grid_map or other representations.

Derived packages mbf_abstract_nav derived interfaces mbf_abstract_core method initialization plugin, handing pointer map representation choice. plugins loaded Move Base Flex derived package mbf_abstract_nav) implement derived interfaces interfaces defined package. mbf_abstract_core::AbstractPlanner interface planner plugin plan global paths. mbf_abstract_core::AbstractController interface controller plugin control move robot. mbf_abstract_core::AbstractRecovery interface recovery plugin recover robot deadlocked situations.

description: The mbf_abstract_nav package contains the abstract navigation server implementation of Move Base Flex (MBF). The abstract navigation server is not bound to any map representation. It provides the actions for planning, controlling and recovering. MBF loads all defined plugins at the program start. Therefor, it loads all plugins which are defined in the lists *planners*, *controllers* and *recovery_behaviors*. Each list holds a pair of a *name* and a *type*. The *type* defines which kind of plugin to load. The *name* defines under which name the plugin should be callable by the actions.
readme: Move Base Flex Abstract Navigation Server mbf_abstract_nav package abstract navigation server implementation Move Base Flex (MBF). abstract navigation server bound map representation. Actions planning, controlling recovering. time start MBF loads defined plugins. Therefor, loads plugins defined lists planners, controllers recovery_behaviors. list holds pair type. type defines kind plugin load. defines plugin callable actions.

description: This package provides common interfaces for navigation specific robot actions. It contains the CostmapPlanner, CostmapController and CostmapRecovery interfaces. The interfaces have to be implemented by the plugins to make them available for Move Base Flex using the mbf_costmap_nav navigation implementation. That implementation inherits the mbf_abstract_nav implementation and binds the system to a local and a global costmap.

description: The mbf_utility package

Move Base Flex (MBF) is a backwards-compatible replacement for move_base. MBF can use existing plugins for move_base, and provides an enhanced version of the planner, controller and recovery plugin ROS interfaces. It exposes action servers for planning, controlling and recovering, providing detailed information of the current state and the plugin’s feedback. An external executive logic can use MBF and its actions to perform smart and flexible navigation strategies. Furthermore, MBF enables the use of other map representations, e.g. meshes or grid_map
       
       This package is a meta package and refers to the Move Base Flex stack packages.The abstract core of MBF – without any binding to a map representation – is represented by the
The move_base_sequence package

Startup robot MoveIt: roslaunch ur_gazebo ur5.launch roslaunch ur5_moveit_config ur5_moveit_planning_execution.launch sim:=true roslaunch ur5_moveit_config moveit_rviz.launch config:=true RViz, “plan execute” motion non-singular position joint angles) close joint limit. Switch compatible type ros-control controller. JointGroupVelocityController JointGroupPositionController, trajectory controller MoveIt requires. rosservice call /controller_manager/switch_controller "start_controllers: 'joint_group_position_controller' stop_controllers: 'arm_controller' strictness: Launch servo node. commands SpaceNavigator joystick-like device: roslaunch moveit_servo spacenav_cpp.launch dont SpaceNavigator, send commands rostopic pub /servo_server/delta_twist_cmds geometry_msgs/TwistStamped "header: auto twist: linear: angular: warning “close singularity”, changing direction motion. Running Tests Run tests moveit_servo folder: catkin run_tests --no-deps

description: A pure-python interaface to the MoveIt! ROS API.
readme: moveit_python set pure-python bindings ROS interface MoveIt! based earlier moveit_utils package developed part chess_player package. Overview interfaces MoveGroupInterface move arm move_group action. PlanningSceneInterface add/remove collision attached objects. set colors objects. PickPlaceInterface interface pick place actions. MoveGroupInterface MoveGroupInterface easy import rospy moveit_python import rospy.init_node("moveit_py") MoveGroupInterface("planning_group_name", "base_link") values base_link, planning group called “planning_group_name”. PlanningSceneInterface PlanningSceneInterface easily insert remove objects MoveIt! planning scene. Starting version module newer service-based approach apply planning scene updates, robust publishing messages topics asynchronously. import rospy moveit_python import rospy.init_node("moveit_py") create planning scene interface, provide root link PlanningSceneInterface("base_link") add cube size, base_link frame p.addCube("my_cube", remove cube p.removeCollisionObject("my_cube") reason prefer service, simply set “use_service” false add/remove calls. Calling waitForSync republish messages needed synchronize planning scene script MoveIt. p.addCube("my_cube", use_service=False) p.addCube("my_other_cube", use_service=False) p.waitForSync() PickPlaceInterface import rospy moveit_python import moveit_msgs.msg import Grasp, PlaceLocation rospy.init_node("moveit_py") provide arm group gripper group names takes parameter "plan_only" defaults False PickPlaceInterface("arm", "gripper") Grasp() fill setup object named object_name PlanningSceneInterface p.pickup("object_name", support_name "supporting_surface") PlaceLocation() fill p.place("object_name" goal_is_eef True, support_name "supporting_surface") Migration moveit_utils GraspingInterface renamed PickPlaceInterface pick/place functions return entire action result, moveit_utils returned error_code. access error code returned, result.error_code.val ObjectManager renamed PlanningSceneInterface remove function removeCollisionObject removeAttachedObject ArmInterface renamed MoveGroupInterface

description:
Maintains a local obstacle map (point cloud,
   voxels or occupancy grid) from recent sensor readings within a
   configurable time window.

Package for robot 2D self-localization using dynamic or static (MRPT or ROS) maps.
	The interface is similar to amcl (https://wiki.ros.org/amcl)
   but supports different particle-filter algorithms, several grid maps at
   different heights, range-only localization, etc.
The mrpt_map is able to publish a mrpt map as ros occupancy grid like the map_server
C++ library to convert between custom mrpt_msgs messages and native MRPT classes

description: ROS package for Multiple objects detection, tracking and classification from LIDAR scans/point-clouds
readme: Multiple objects detection, tracking classification LIDAR scans/point-clouds PCL based ROS package Detect/Cluster Track Classify static dynamic objects real-time LIDAR scans implemented Features: tree based point cloud processing object feature detection point clouds Unsupervised euclidean cluster extraction k-means clustering based detected features refinement RANSAC Stable tracking (object data association) ensemble Kalman Filters Robust compared k-means clustering mean-flow tracking Usage: Follow steps (multi_object_tracking_lidar) package: Create catkin workspace setup Navigate src folder catkin workspace: ~/catkin_ws/src Clone repository: git clone https://github.com/praveen-palanisamy/multiple-object-tracking-lidar.git Compile build package: ~/catkin_ws catkin_make Add catkin workspace ROS environment: source ~/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash Run kf_tracker ROS node package: rosrun multi_object_tracking_lidar kf_tracker ROS node running! long point clouds published filtered_cloud rostopic, outputs node published obj_id, cluster_0, cluster_1, cluster_5 topics markers topic visualize RViz. Supported point-cloud streams/sources: input point-clouds real LiDAR simulated LiDAR point cloud dataset data source produces point clouds Note: package expects valid point cloud data input. point clouds publish “filtered_cloud” expected NaNs. point cloud filtering task application dependent module. PCL library pcl::removeNaNFromPointCloud method filter NaN points. refer code snippet easily filter NaN points point cloud. Citing code snippets repository work, cite: @software{praveen_palanisamy_2019_3559187, author {Praveen Palanisamy}, title {{praveen-palanisamy/multiple-object-tracking-lidar: Multiple-Object-Tracking-from-Point-Clouds_v1.0.2}}, month dec, year publisher {Zenodo}, version doi {10.5281/zenodo.3559187}, url {https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.3559186} Wiki Checkout Wiki pages Multiple-object tracking pointclouds Velodyne VLP-16

description:
Discover the running ROS Masters in local network. The 
     discovering is done by sending an echo heartbeat messages to a defined 
     multicast group.
     The alternative is to use a zeroconf/avahi daemon to register the ROS 
     master as service and discover other ROS masters.

Synchronize the local ROS master to the remote masters 
     discovered by fkie_master_discovery node. The registration
     of topics and services is only perform by local ROS master.

The metapackage to combine the nodes required to establish and manage a multimaster network.
    This requires no or minimal configuration. The changes are automatically detected and synchronized.
The messages required by multimaster packages.

Graphical interface, written in PySide, to manage the running and 
     configured ROS nodes on different hosts. For discovering 
     the running ROS master master_discovery node will be used.

description: This package provides implementations of the Trajectory Rollout and Dynamic Window approaches to local robot navigation on a plane. Given a plan to follow and a costmap, the controller produces velocity commands to send to a mobile base. This package supports both holonomic and non-holonomic robots, any robot footprint that can be represented as a convex polygon or circle, and exposes its configuration as ROS parameters that can be set in a launch file. This package's ROS wrapper adheres to the BaseLocalPlanner interface specified in the

description:
This package provides an implementation of a 2D costmap that takes in sensor
        data from the world, builds a 2D or 3D occupancy grid of the data (depending
        on whether a voxel based implementation is used), and inflates costs in a
        2D costmap based on the occupancy grid and a user specified inflation radius.
        This package also provides support for map_server based initialization of a
        costmap, rolling window based costmaps, and parameter based subscription to
        and configuration of sensor topics.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, David V. Lu!!, Dave Hershberger, contradict <AT> gmail.com

description:
This package provides an implementation of the Dynamic Window Approach to

        base. This package supports any robot who's footprint can be represented as
        a convex polygon or cicrle, and exposes its configuration as ROS parameters
        that can be set in a launch file. The parameters for this planner are also
        dynamically reconfigurable. This package's ROS wrapper adheres to the
        BaseLocalPlanner interface specified in the

description: This package provides common interfaces for navigation specific robot actions. Currently, this package provides the BaseGlobalPlanner, BaseLocalPlanner, and RecoveryBehavior interfaces, which can be used to build actions that can easily swap their planner, local controller, or recovery behavior for new versions adhering to the same interface.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy

description:
navfn provides a fast interpolated navigation function that can be used to create plans for
        a mobile base. The planner assumes a circular robot and operates on a costmap to find a
        minimum cost plan from a start point to an end point in a grid. The navigation function is
        computed with Dijkstra's algorithm, but support for an A* heuristic may also be added in the
        near future. navfn also provides a ROS wrapper for the navfn planner that adheres to the
        nav_core::BaseGlobalPlanner interface specified in
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy
authors: Kurt Konolige, Eitan Marder-Eppstein, contradict <AT> gmail.com

description:
A 2D navigation stack that takes in information from odometry, sensor

maintainers: Michael Ferguson, David V. Lu!!, Aaron Hoy

description: voxel_grid provides an implementation of an efficient 3D voxel grid. The occupancy grid can support 3 different representations for the state of a cell: marked, free, or unknown. Due to the underlying implementation relying on bitwise and and or integer operations, the voxel grid only supports 16 different levels per voxel column. However, this limitation yields raytracing and cell marking performance in the grid comparable to standard 2D structures making it quite fast compared to most 3D structures.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Eric Berger, contradict <AT> gmail.com

description: Meta-Package containing modules for 2D-Navigation

This package holds a collection of plugins for the RobotNavigator, that provide
    different cooperative exploration strategies for a team of mobile robots.

Graph-based Simultaneous Localization and Mapping module.
    Includes OpenKarto GraphSLAM library by "SRI International".

Wrapper around Particle Filter implementation.
    The SelfLocalizer can be used as library or as a ros-node.
Messages used for 2D-Navigation.

This package provides a node for higher level navigation of a mobile
    robot in a planar environment. It needs a map and the robot's position
    within this map to create a plan for navigation. When used together with
    a SLAM module it can also be used to perform autonomous exploration of
    the robot's workspace.

The operator is a lightweight, purely reactive obstacle-avoidance
    module for mobile robots moving in a planar environment. The operator node
    works by evaluating a set of predefined motion primitives based on a local
    costmap and a desired direction. The best evaluated motion command will be
    send to the mobile base.

This package is used to manually control a robot that uses the operator and
    navigator node from navigation_2d. Currently there is one node to control one
    robot with a joystick and one to control multiple robots in simulation.
    It can send commands directly to the operator or start and stop navigator actions.
Contains a set of tutorials that run 2D-Navigation within Stage-Simulator.

The assisted_teleop node subscribes to a desired trajectory topic
    (geometry_msgs/Twist) and uses TrajectoryPlannerROS to find a valid
    trajectory close to the desired trajectory before republishing. Useful for
    filtering teleop commands while avoiding obstacles. This package also
    contains LaserScanMaxRangeFilter, which is a LaserScan filter plugin that
    takes max range values in a scan and turns them into valid values that are
    slightly less than max range.
readme: assisted_teleop assisted_teleop node subscribes desired trajectory topic (geometry_msgs/Twist) TrajectoryPlannerROS find valid trajectory close desired trajectory republishing. filtering teleop commands avoiding obstacles. package LaserScanMaxRangeFilter, LaserScan filter plugin takes max range values scan turns valid values slightly max range.

description: Object_recognition_msgs contains the ROS message and the actionlib definition used in object_recognition_core

The dynamicEDT3D library implements an inrementally updatable Euclidean distance transform (EDT) in 3D. It comes with a wrapper to use the OctoMap 3D representation and hooks into the change detection of the OctoMap library to propagate changes to the EDT.

The OctoMap library implements a 3D occupancy grid mapping approach, providing data structures and mapping algorithms in C++. The map implementation is based on an octree. See
  http://octomap.github.io for details.

description:
A simple, analytical inverse kinematic library for industrial robots with parallel bases and
    spherical wrists. Based on the paper "An Analytical Solution of the Inverse Kinematics Problem
    of Industrial Serial Manipulators with an Ortho-parallel Basis and a Spherical Wrist" by
    Mathias Brandstötter, Arthur Angerer, and Michael Hofbaur.
readme: Intro simple, analytical inverse kinematic library industrial robots parallel bases spherical wrists. Based paper Analytical Solution Inverse Kinematics Problem Industrial Serial Manipulators Ortho-parallel Basis Spherical Wrist Mathias Brandstötter, Arthur Angerer, Michael Hofbaur. Purpose package meant provide simpler alternative IK-Fast based solutions situations industrial robot parallel base spherical wrist. configuration extremely common industrial robots. kinematics parameterized primary values robot’s spec sheet set joint-zero offsets. structure, setup required. description: Ethernet interface to OxTS GPS receivers (NCOM packet structure)
readme: Ethernet interface OxTS GPS receivers NCOM packet structure Tested RT3000v2 receiver. Published Topics gps/fix (sensor_msgs/NavSatFix) GPS position geodetic coordinates (latitude longitude) gps/vel (geometry_msgs/TwistWithCovarianceStamped) GPS velocity ENU frame linear.x Velocity due East linear.y Velocity due North linear.z Velocity gps/odom (nav_msgs/Odometry) GPS position heading UTM frame, velocity vehicle body frame pose.position.x UTM Easting meters pose.position.y UTM Northing meters pose.orientation Yaw quaternion representing heading UTM. mind UTM heading relative UTM grid, necessarily aligned due East. phenomenon “grid convergence”. Wikipedia details. twist.linear.x Local frame velocity vehicle twist.linear.y Local frame lateral velocity vehicle twist.angular.z Yaw rate vehicle rad/s imu/data (sensor_msgs/Imu) Orientation, angular rates, linear accelerations reported OxTS unit gps/pos_type (std_msgs/String) Position solution type reported OxTS unit (point position, differential pseudorange, RTK float, RTK integer, gps/nav_status (std_msgs/String) Navigation status reported OxTS unit. fix, vel, odom, imu/data topics published topic string “READY”. gps/time_ref (sensor_msgs/TimeReference) UTC equivalent GPS timestamp reported OxTS unit. Parameters interface Restrict single network interface, eth0. Default <empty> ip_address Local listen address. Default <empty> port Local UDP listen port. Default frame_id_gps frame-ID geodetic GPS position IMU. Default gps frame_id_vel frame-ID ENU GPS velocity. Default enu frame_id_odom frame-ID UTM odometry. Default utm usage rosrun oxford_gps_eth gps_node roslaunch oxford_gps_eth gps.launch FAQ Connected Oxford GPS <ip_address>:3000 GPS position velocity messages published. Data published OxTS unit reports completely initialized. Drive faster short period time powering OxTS unit initialize sensor. gps/nav_status string topic status initialization process. string shows “READY”, GPS position velocity messages start publishing.

description:
This package provides a specialized joint_trajectory_controller that can be moved into holding state via service call.
  No further trajectories will be accepted/followed in this state.
readme: Overview package joint trajectory controller put holding mode service call. specialized version ros_controllers::JointTrajectoryController providing holding mode functionality. controlled stop hold trajectory performed stopping manipulator mechanical stress hard brake. Topic interface deprecated: Due safety reasons, command interface PilzJointTrajectoryController deactivated. information issue ros-controls/ros_controllers. Speed monitoring controller perform speed monitoring robot links cartesian space. robot links move faster mm/s, controlled stop executed. requirement operation mode DIN ISO prbt_hardware_support. speed monitoring activated default. Additionally controller limits joint acceleration performed trajectories. file manipulator_controller.yaml limits adjusted. ROS API Advertised service is_executing (std_srvs/Trigger) Detect controller executing trajectory monitor_cartesian_speed (std_srvs/SetBool) Activate/deactivate speed monitoring hold (std_srvs/Trigger) Switch holding mode unhold (std_srvs/Trigger) Leave holding mode

readme: DRACO POINT CLOUD TRANSPORT Plugin ROS package point_cloud_transport, Google Draco compression library low-bandwidth lossy transportation PointCloud2 messages. Publisher plugin dynamic reconfiguration parameters, change compression runtime. Draco encode invalid values, pointclouds invalid values turned unstructured dense clouds NaNs, height Encode Decode Speed adjusting encode_speed dedode_speed parameters, adjust speed plugin encodes point cloud. higher number, higher speed encoding/decoding, worse compression. detailed information, documentation Draco. Encode Method Auto method decides encoding method based parameter default method. KD-tree method forces encoder KD-tree encoding. attribute point cloud type float32, kd-tree encoding requires attribute quantized. Sequential method forces encoder sequential encoding. Quantization sequential encoding. Sequential encoding worse compression KD-tree, faster arrangement points point cloud intact. sequential encoding encode point clouds Kinect. Deduplicate Deduplicate option tells encoder delete duplicate points point cloud, allowing transport smaller point clouds. Force Quantization Force_quantization option forces quantization KD-tree encoding method. Quantization Attribute Types Quantization_POSITION, Quantization_NORMAL, Quantization_COLOR tells encoder bits quantization attribute type. Attribute type point cloud attribute recognized based list names: POSITION POSITION POSITION “pos” POSITION “position” POSITION “rgb” COLOR “rgba” COLOR COLOR COLOR COLOR COLOR NORMAL NORMAL NORMAL encoded GENERIC custom quantization, edit list recognized names expert_quantization expert_attribute_type options. Expert Quantization Expert_quantization option encoder custom quantization values point cloud attributes. Multiple POSITION attribute encoded varying quantization levels. set quantization PointField entry point cloud advertised base topic base_topic, set parameter: /base_topic/draco/attribute_mapping/quantization_bits/x. rosparam set /base_topic/draco/attribute_mapping/quantization_bits/x expert_quantization, user quantization bits PointField entries point cloud. Expert Attribute Types Expert_attribute_types option encoder custom attribute types encoding point cloud attributes. set type PointField entry point cloud advertised base topic base_topic, set parameter: /base_topic/draco/attribute_mapping/attribute_type/x. rosparam set /base_topic/draco/attribute_mapping/attribute_type/x "'POSITION'" expert_attribute_types, user type PointField entries point cloud. Accepted types POSITION NORMAL COLOR TEX_COORD GENERIC encoding rgb/rgba COLOR, user common rgba tweak ROS (encoding rgba instances Byte instance float32). inform encoder, PointField entry “rgb” handled tweak, set parameter: rosparam set /base_topic/draco/attribute_mapping/rgba_tweak/rgb true Subscriber Set Skip Dequantization Attribute Types SkipDequantizationPOSITION, SkipDequantizationNORMAL, SkipDequantizationCOLOR options decoder skip dequantization attribute types.

The pr2_gripper_action provides an action interface for using the
  gripper. Users can specify what position to move to (while limiting the
  force) and the action will report success when the position is reached or
  failure when the gripper cannot move any longer.

The PR2 head action is a node that provides an action interface for
  pointing the head of the PR2.  It passes trajectory goals to the
  controller, and reports success when they have finished executing.

The pr2_gripper_sensor package

The pr2_gripper_sensor_action package provides an action interface for talking to the pr2_gripper_sensor_controller real-time controller.

  It provides several different actions for getting high-level sensor information from the PR2 palm-mounted accelerometers, fingertip pressure arrays, and gripper motor/encoder, as well as several sensor-based gripper control actions that respond with low-latency in real-time.

description:
The pr2_gripper_sensor_msgs package contains various actions and messages that are used in the pr2_gripper_sensor* packages. The structure of the API used by pr2_gripper_sensor_action, and pr2_gripper_sensor_controller packages is as follows: 

Users will send a goal to an Action in the message format of PR2Gripper*Command (where * replaces the name of the particular Action from pr2_gripper_sensor_action). Feedback and Result information for the action is then returned in the format of PR2Gripper*Data.

  components that are controlled over EtherCAT. This includes the
  motors and encoders needed to control the PR2 mechanism, as well as
  components like the pressure sensors in the fingertips, camera
  triggers, etc... All of the hardware components in this interface are
  directly available to the controllers inside the hard realtime
  control loop.
The pr2_mechanism stack contains the infrastructure to control the PR2 robot in a hard realtime control loop.
The `pr2_mechanism_diagnostics` node subscribes to `mechanism_statistics` and publishes diagnostics data for joints and controllers on `/diagnostics`.

This package provides a node that monitors the state of the run stops of the pr2_robot. When the state of the
   run stop changes from off to on, this node will automatically enable the power to the motors, and reset
   the motors. This allows you to use the run stop as a 'pause' button. By using the run stop as a tool to
   power up the robot, the run stop is also in reach of the user once the robot starts moving.

description: quaternion operations
readme: pyquaternion fully featured, pythonic library quaternion representation, manipulation, animation geometry. visit pyquaternion homepage full information latest documentation. Designed Python Smooth animation interpolation random orientations pyquaternion module. Quickstart Install PyPI pip install pyquaternion Run basic overview. copy [demo.py](./demo/demo.py). ```python import pyquaternion Create quaternion representing rotation degrees positive axis. my_quaternion pyquaternion.Quaternion(axis=[0, degrees=90) my_vector my_rotated_vector my_quaternion.rotate(my_vector) print('\nBasic Rotation') print('--------------') print('My Vector: {}'.format(my_vector)) print('Performing rotation {angle} deg {axis}'.format(angle=my_quaternion.degrees, axis=my_quaternion.axis)) print('My Rotated Vector: {}'.format(my_rotated_vector)) Create quaternion representing rotation null_quaternion pyquaternion.Quaternion(axis=[0, angle=0) print('\nInterpolated Rotation') print('---------------------') create sequence intermediate quaternion rotation objects pyquaternion.Quaternion.intermediates(null_quaternion, my_quaternion, include_endpoints=True): my_interpolated_point q.rotate(my_vector) print('My Interpolated Point: {point}\t(after rotation {angle} deg {axis})'.format( point=my_interpolated_point, angle=round(q.degrees, axis=q.axis print('Done!') output: Basic Rotation Vector: Performing rotation deg Rotated Vector: Interpolated Rotation Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg

description:
This stack provides Python bindings for Qt.
    There are two providers: pyside and pyqt.  PySide is released under
    the LGPL.  PyQt is released under the GPL.

    Both the bindings and tools to build bindings are included from each
    available provider.  For PySide, it is called "Shiboken".  For PyQt,
    this is called "SIP".

    Also provided is adapter code to make the user's Python code
    independent of which binding provider was actually used which makes
    it very easy to switch between these.
authors: Dave Hershberger, Dorian Scholz, Dirk Thomas

description:
The rc_dynamics_api provides an API for easy handling of the dynamic-state data
      streams provided by Roboception's stereo camera with self-localization.
      See http://rc-visard.com

      Dynamic-state estimates of the rc_visard relate to its self-localization and
      ego-motion estimation. These states refer to rc_visard's current pose,
      velocity, or acceleration and are published on demand via several data streams.
      For a complete list and descriptions of these dynamics states and the
      respective data streams please refer to rc_visard's user manual.
authors: Heiko Hirschmueller, Christian Emmerich, Felix Endres

description:
GenICam/GigE Vision Convenience Layer.

      This package combines the Roboception convenience layer for images with the
      GenICam reference implementation and a GigE Vision transport layer. It is a
      self contained package that permits configuration and image streaming of
      GenICam / GigE Vision 2.0 compatible cameras like the Roboception rc_visard.

      This package also provides some tools that can be called from the command line
      for discovering cameras, changing their configuration and streaming images.
      Although the tools are meant to be useful when working in a shell or in a
      script, their main purpose is to serve as example on how to use the API for
      reading and setting parameters, streaming and synchronizing images.

      See LICENSE.md for licensing terms of the different parts.

description: The rc_genicam_driver provides data from a Roboception rc_visard 3D sensor or rc_cube on several ROS topics.
readme: rc_genicam_driver Nodelet/node providing ROS interface configure Roboception rc_visard rc_cube receive images. consult manuals details: https://doc.rc-visard.com https://doc.rc-cube.com Installation Debian/Ubuntu add ROS sources sudo apt-get install ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-rc-genicam-driver Source rc_genicam_driver depends rc_genicam_api dependencies installed rosdep. rosdep install --from-paths rc_genicam_driver --ignore-src rc_genicam_driver Building installing package typical ROS catkin workflow. alternative, cmake build-flow mkdir build build cmake -DCATKIN_BUILD_BINARY_PACKAGE="1" -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX="/opt/ros/$ROS_DISTRO" -DCMAKE_PREFIX_PATH="/opt/ros/$ROS_DISTRO" -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release install Alternatively, final install, package sudo dpkg install ros-melodic-rc-genicam-driver_*.deb. GenICam GenTL Transport Layer rc_genicam_driver rc_genicam_api interfacing rc_visard rc_cube GenICam/GigE Vision requires transport layer called GenTL producer (shared library suffix .cti). convenience rc_genicam_api producers Baumer common architectures. path producer set GENICAM_GENTL64_PATH environment variable GENICAM_GENTL32_PATH bit systems). set, rc_genicam_driver fall searching Baumer producer rc_genicam_api installed. producer .cti error message [ERROR] transport layers path /opt/ros/melodic/lib/rc_genicam_api case install rc_genicam_api properly set environment variable running export: GENICAM_GENTL64_PATH=/path/to/rc_genicam_api/baumer/Ubuntu-14.04/x86_64 Configuration Parameters Parameters set ROS param server run-time. device: device, Roboception rc_visard sensor rc_cube. serial number, IMPORTANT: preceed colon passing commandline, setting rosparam launch file https://github.com/ros/ros_comm/issues/1339). user defined (factory default rc_visard’s model), unique reachable sensors internal generated GenTL producer. MAC address change implementation transport layer. https://github.com/roboception/rc_genicam_api#device-id details. default parameter set works compatible device network. gev_access: gev_access mode, ‘control’ Configuration streaming possibility clients read GenICam parameters. default. ‘exclusive’ Exclusive access sensor. prevents clients read GenICam parameters. Dynamic-reconfigure Parameters parameters changed runtime dynamic reconfigure: ptp_enabled: Enable PTP slave (PrecisionTimeProtocol, IEEE1588) camera_fps: Frames published nodelet. Publishing frames slowed depending setting. Setting higher real framerate specific device effect. camera_exp_auto: parameter removed. camera_exp_control camera_exp_control: Expose control mode “Manual” setting exposure time gain camera_exp_value camera_gain_value, “Auto” auto exposure “HDR” high dynamic range mode. Default: Auto. camera_exp_auto_mode Auto-exposure mode “Normal”, “Out1High” “AdaptiveOut1”. Default: Normal. camera_exp_max: Maximum exposure time seconds exp_auto true. camera_exp_auto_average_max: auto exposure set exposure time gain factor average image intensity upper bound. parameter defines upper bound. reached saturation due reflections). camera_exp_auto_average_min: camera_exp_auto_average_max. parameter defines bound. average image intensity reduced reduce avoid saturation due reflections). camera_exp_value: Exposure time seconds exp_auto false. camera_gain_value: Gain factor decibel exp_auto false. camera_gamma: Gamma factor. Default: Auto exposure region: Definition region left image, region size image, full left image determine auto exposure. camera_exp_width: Width auto exposure region. image. camera_exp_height: Height auto exposure region. image. camera_exp_offset_x: column auto exposure region camera_exp_offset_y: row auto exposure region depth_acquisition_mode: SingleFrame Continuous. letter checked, giving sufficient. depth_quality: Quality “Medium”, “High” “Full”. letter checked, specification sufficient. quality setting effectively downscales image downscale factor Full downscale image, factor NOTE: mode requires ‘stereo_plus’ license rc_visard. High downscales factor Medium downscales factor downscales factor depth_static_scene: parameter set true scene camera static. effect quality High Full. active, input images accumulated averaged reduce noise. limits frame rate maximum timestamp disparity image image accumulation. depth_fill: Higher numbers fill gaps measurments potentielly higher errors. depth_seg: Maximum size isolated disparity regions invalidated, related full resolution. depth_smooth: Switching smoothing disparities NOTE: Smoothing requires ‘stereo_plus’ license rc_visard. depth_minconf: Minimal confidence. disparities confidence set invalid. depth_mindepth: Minimum depth meter. disparities depth set invalid. depth_maxdepth: Maximum depth meter. disparities higher depth set invalid. depth_maxdeptherr: Maximum depth error meter. disparities higher depth error set invalid. depth_exposure_adapt_timeout: Maximum time seconds wait triggering SingleFrame modes auto exposure finished adjustments. out1_mode: Mode digital GPIO values switching permanently High switching permanently ExposureActive switching exposure time image. ExposureAlternateActive switching exposure time image. changed rc_visard Control license. default out2_mode: Mode digital GPIO functionality out1_mode. default color sensors, dynamic-reconfigure parameters additionally camera_wb_auto: true, white balancing automatically. false, red blue green ratios chosen manually. camera_wb_ratio_red: Red green ratio color balancing camera_wb_auto false. camera_wb_ratio_blue: Blue green ratio color balancing camera_wb_auto false. Topics topics nodelet request data images, poses) sensor subscriber topic. Images, Stereo Data, Point Clouds /stereo/left/camera_info (sensor_msgs::CameraInfo) /stereo/right/camera_info (sensor_msgs::CameraInfo) /stereo/left/camera_param (rc_common_msgs::CameraParam) /stereo/right/camera_param (rc_common_msgs::CameraParam) /stereo/left/image_rect (sensor_msgs::Image, MONO8) /stereo/right/image_rect (sensor_msgs::Image, MONO8) /stereo/disparity (stereo_msgs::DisparityImage) /stereo/disparity_color (sensor_msgs::Image, RGB8, visually pleasing) /stereo/depth (sensor_msgs::Image, TYPE_32FC1) /stereo/confidence (sensor_msgs::Image, TYPE_32FC1, values /stereo/error_disparity (sensor_msgs::Image, TYPE_32FC1) /stereo/error_depth (sensor_msgs::Image, TYPE_32FC1) /stereo/points2 (sensor_msgs::PointCloud2) proprietary CameraParam messages image information exposure time, gain values digital inputs outputs time image capture. color sensors, topics additionally /stereo/left/image_rect_color (sensor_msgs::Image, format: RGB8) /stereo/right/image_rect_color (sensor_msgs::Image, format: RGB8) connected rc_visard Control license, topics additionally images GPIO high. topics out1_mode set special mode ExposureAlternateActive. /stereo/left/image_rect_out1_low (sensor_msgs::Image, MONO8) /stereo/left/image_rect_out1_high (sensor_msgs::Image, MONO8) /stereo/right/image_rect_out1_low (sensor_msgs::Image, MONO8) /stereo/right/image_rect_out1_high (sensor_msgs::Image, MONO8) color sensors Control license, topics additionally /stereo/left/image_rect_color_out1_low (sensor_msgs::Image, format: RGB8) /stereo/left/image_rect_color_out1_high (sensor_msgs::Image, format: RGB8) /stereo/right/image_rect_color_out1_low (sensor_msgs::Image, format: RGB8) /stereo/right/image_rect_color_out1_high (sensor_msgs::Image, format: RGB8) Running multiple rc_visard’s ros environment operating multiple rc_visard’s ros environment, ros node started separate namespaces, my_visard. result, frame_ids ros messages prefixed, my_visard_world my_visard_camera. Additionally client host parameter set address hostname device rc_cube rc_visard) pipeline parameter defaults run april tag detection client pipeline rosrun rc_reason_clients rc_april_tag_detect_client _host:=10.0.2.40 _pipeline:=1 rc_april_tag_detect_client rc_qr_code_detect_client Clients interface TagDetect (AprilTag QRCode detection) running device. TagDetect documentation details. TagDetect parameters clients additional parameters enable publishing detected tags markers. child_frame_id set <tagId>_<instanceId>. publish_tf: Publish detected tags (default: True) publish_markers: Publish detected tags visualization markers (default: True) TagDetect services AprilTag detection: rosrun rc_reason_clients rc_april_tag_detect_client _host:=10.0.2.40 QRCode detection: rosrun rc_reason_clients rc_qr_code_detect_client _host:=10.0.2.40 rc_silhouettematch_client Client interface SilhouetteMatch running device. ItemPick documentation details. ItemPick parameters client additional parameters enable publishing detected load carriers grasps markers: publish_tf: Publish detected instances (default: True) publish_markers: Publish detected instances visualization markers (default: True) ItemPick services run client: rosrun rc_reason_clients rc_itempick_client _host:=10.0.2.40 rc_boxpick_client Client interface BoxPick running device. description: The rc_hand_eye_calibration_client package
readme: ROS Hand-eye calibration client Roboception’s rc_visard package developed anymore. client rc_reason_clients_ros repository. node ROS service calls topics calibrate rc_visard robot (aka hand-eye calibration). pre-existing calibration /tf_static. default behavior request existing calibration rc_visard startup, broadcast (latched) /tf_static broadcast advertised ROS services calibrate get_calibration called. calibration routine consists steps: Setting calibration parameters, grid size mounting, dynamic reconfigure. user-defined number robot calibration poses repeat Move robot pose (calibration grid visible rc_visard’s view). Send robot pose rc_visard (set_pose) Trigger calibration tranformation calculated (calibrate). calibration transform calculated tested, saved rc_visard (save_calibration). detailed instructions calibration routine consult rc_visard manual: https://doc.rc-visard.com. Installation Debian/Ubuntu add ROS sources sudo apt-get install ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-rc-hand-eye-calibration-client Source package relies git submodules cpr library initialized building source. git submodule update --init --recursive Configuration Parameters device: device, Roboception rc_visard sensor. serial number, IMPORTANT: preceed colon passing commandline setting rosparam https://github.com/ros/ros_comm/issues/1339). neccessary string launch file. user defined (factory default rc_visard’s model), unique reachable sensors. host: device address hostname rc_visard calibrated. rc_visard_frame_id: frame rc_visard calibrating. Default: “camera” end_effector_frame_id: frame calibrated robot_mounted rc_visard. Default: “end_effector”. base_frame_id: frame calibrated statically (externally) mounted rc_visard (robot_mounted false). Default: “base_link” calibration_request_period: Decimal number, controls requesting calibration rc_visard. Default: positive: Interval seconds automated requests latest calibration. request startup (default). negative, calibration requested. case, results broadcast services calibrate get_calibration node called. calibration_publication_period: Decimal number, controls broadcasting calibration /tf_static. Default: positive: Interval seconds. negative calibration broadcast /tf_static changed manual periodic calibration request (default). Dynamic reconfigure parameters grid_width: width calibration pattern meters. grid_height: height calibration pattern meters. robot_mounted: camera mounted robot tcp_rotation_axis:, TCP rotation axis DOF robot calibration general robot) tcp_offset: Offset TCP DOF robot calibration Services services offered follow calibration routine: reset_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Trigger]: Deletes previously poses images. saved calibration result reloaded. service (re-)start hand-eye calibration scratch. set_pose [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/SetCalibrationPose]: robot pose calibration pose hand-eye calibration routine. calibrate [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Calibration]: Calculates returns hand-eye calibration transformation robot poses configured set_pose service. Broadcasts result /tf_static. get_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Calibration]: Returns existing hand-eye calibration transformation. Broadcasts result /tf_static. save_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Trigger]: Persistently saves result hand-eye calibration rc_visard overwrites existing stored result retrieved time get_calibration service. remove_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Trigger]: Removes stored hand-eye calibration rc_visard. call get_calibration service reports hand-eye calibration Periodic broadcasting stopped. set_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/SetCalibration]: Sets hand-eye calibration transformation. calibration transformation expected format returned calibrate get_calibration calls. save_calibration called calibration transformation persistent. Launching command line parameters: rosrun rc_hand_eye_calibration_client rc_hand_eye_calibration_client_node _device:=:<serial_number>

readme: remote_rosbag_record ROS nodes start/stop rosbag-record remote trigger Usage Launch recorder nodes favourite nodes. respawn options record multiple times. <launch> <group ns="console"> <include file="console.machine"/> console nodes <node name="console-record" pkg="remote_rosbag_record" type="record" respawn="true"> <rosparam file="console_topics.yml"/> <param name="prefix" value="console"/> <param name="apped_date" value="true"/> </node> </group> <group ns="robot"> <include file="robot.machine"/> robot nodes <node name="robot-record" pkg="remote_rosbag_record" type="record" respawn="true"> <rosparam file="robot_topics.yml"/> <param name="prefix" value="robot"/> <param name="apped_date" value="true"/> </node> </group> </launch> xxx_topics.yml topics: /joint_states /imu Start rosbag-record timing method remote_rosbag_record/trigger node rosrun remote_rosbag_record trigger _regex:="/.*/start" method remote_rosbag_record/joy_listener node rosrun remote_rosbag_record joy_listener _start_regex:="/.*/start" _start_button:=10 method call trigger services teleoperation node #include<remote_rosbag_record/call.hpp> #include<boost/regex.hpp> (start_button_pressed) remote_rosbag_record::call(boost::regex("/.*/start")); Stop rosbag-record (similar previous step) Nodes record starts/stops rosbag-record coressponding service called Services start (std_srvs/Empty) stop (std_srvs/Empty) Parameters parameters optional function original rosbag-record. http://wiki.ros.org/rosbag/Commandline#record ~record_all (bool) ~regex (bool) ~quiet (bool) ~append_date (bool) ~verbose (bool) ~compression (string) “uncompressed”, ~prefix (string) (string) ~topics (string array) ~buffer_size (int) ~exclude_regex (string) ~node (string) trigger calls multiple start/stop services Parameters ~regex (string, required) regular expression matches services call ~verbose (bool, default: true) verbose console output calling services joy_listener subscribes joystick messages calls start/stop services button pressed Subscribed topics joy (sensor_msgs/Joy) Parameters ~start_button (int, default: button trigger start services ~start_regex (string, default: “<node’s namespace>/start”) regular expression matches start services ~stop_button (int, default: button trigger stop services ~stop_regex (string, default: “<node’s namespace>/stop”) regular expression matches stop services ~verbose (bool, default: true) verbose console output calling services

description:
This package retrieves data from url-format files such as http://,
   ftp://, package:// file://, etc., and loads the data into memory.
   The package:// url for ros packages is translated into a local
   file:// url.  The resourse retriever was initially designed to load
   mesh files into memory, but it can be used for any type of
   data. The resource retriever is based on the the libcurl library.

     and (registered) point clouds.

description: Locomotor is an extensible path planning coordination engine that replaces move_base. The goal is to provide a mechanism for controlling what happens when the global and local planners succeed and fail. It leverages ROS callback queues to coordinate multiple threads.

description: The robot_navigation package

The Robot Pose EKF package is used to estimate the 3D pose of a robot, based on (partial) pose measurements coming from different sources. It uses an extended Kalman filter with a 6D model (3D position and 3D orientation) to combine measurements from wheel odometry, IMU sensor and visual odometry. The basic idea is to offer loosely coupled integration with different sensors, where sensor signals are received as ROS messages.

description:
Tools for directing, throttling, selecting, and otherwise messing with
    ROS topics at a meta level. None of the programs in this package actually
    know about the topics whose streams they are altering; instead, these
    tools deal with messages as generic binary blobs. This means they can be
    applied to any ROS topic.

Rationale OEMs provide native Cartesian interfaces robot drivers. offering hardware interfaces, implementers ROS controllers direct access Cartesian buffers reading writing. advantages classic joint-based interfaces depends personal cases. main difference Cartesian case robot takes care Inverse Kinematics. Usage package PoseCommandInterface TwistCommandInterface read/write access CartesianStateInterface pure read access. add hardware abstraction #include <cartesian_interface/cartesian_command_interface.h> #include <cartesian_interface/cartesian_state_handle.h> class YourRobot public hardware_interface::RobotHW interfaces Cartesian ROS-controllers ros_controllers_cartesian::CartesianStateInterface cart_interface_; ros_controllers_cartesian::TwistCommandInterface twist_interface_; ros_controllers_cartesian::PoseCommandInterface pose_interface_; Buffers read/write access geometry_msgs::Pose cart_pose_; geometry_msgs::Twist cart_twist_; geometry_msgs::Accel cart_accel_; geometry_msgs::Accel cart_jerk_; geometry_msgs::Twist twist_command_; geometry_msgs::Pose pose_command_; Registering YourRobot::YourRobot() ros_controllers_cartesian::CartesianStateHandle cart_state_handle("base", "tip", &cart_pose_, &cart_twist_, &cart_accel_, &cart_jerk_); cart_interface_.registerHandle(cart_state_handle); twist_interface_.registerHandle( ros_controllers_cartesian::TwistCommandHandle(cart_interface_.getHandle("tip"), &twist_command_)); twist_interface_.getHandle("tip"); pose_interface_.registerHandle( ros_controllers_cartesian::PoseCommandHandle(cart_interface_.getHandle("tip"), &pose_command_)); pose_interface_.getHandle("tip"); Register interfaces registerInterface(&twist_interface_); registerInterface(&pose_interface_); Note strings base tip instantiation Cartesian state handle. represent frames robot kinematics names vary robot robot. convention assumes base reference frame tip tip end-point robot control represents unique identifier handle. resource names joint-based ROS control. Cartesian ROS controllers implementing ROS controller, add handle read/write access, case Cartesian pose control: #include <cartesian_interface/cartesian_command_interface.h> class YourController public controller_interface::Controller<ros_controllers_cartesian::PoseCommandInterface> private: ros_controllers_cartesian::PoseCommandHandle handle_; instantiation handle, end-point control: handle_ hw->getHandle("tip"); handle implement control loop update(...): current state robot hardware geometry_msgs::Pose pose handle_.getPose(); geometry_msgs::Twist twist handle_.getTwist(); geometry_msgs::Accel accel handle_.getAccel(); geometry_msgs::Pose target; Implement control law handle_.setCommand(target); Acknowledgement Developed collaboration Supported ROSIN ROS-Industrial Quality-Assured Robot Software Components. information: rosin-project.eu project received funding European Union’s Horizon research innovation programme grant agreement

description: Metapackage for Cartesian ROS controllers

A ros_control controller accepting Cartesian twist messages in order to move a robot manipulator.
    It uses a Cartesian interface to the robot, so that the robot hardware takes care about
    doing the inverse kinematics. This could be used e.g. for visual servoing applications.

description: Contains aligned allocation functions, as well as an STL-compatible AlignedAllocator class.

The lockfree package contains lock-free data structures for use in multithreaded programming.  These
     kinds of data structures are generally not as easy to use as single-threaded equivalents, and are not
     always faster.  If you don't know you need to use one, try another structure with a lock around it
     first.
The ros_realtime package

rosatomic provides the C++11-style atomic operations by pulling symbols from the proposed Boost.Atomic
     package into the ros namespace.  Once C++11-style atomics (std::atomic) are available from compilers, rosatomic will
     conditionally use those instead.
rosrt provides classes for interfacing with ROS from within realtime systems, such as realtime-safe Publisher and Subscriber classes.

The rt_usb_9axisimu_driver package
readme: English rt_usb_9axisimu_driver rt_usb_9axisimu_driverは株式会社アールティが販売しているUSB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュール用のROSパッケージです。 License: 3-Clause BSD License Source: https://github.com/rt-net/rt_usb_9axisimu_driver.git (branch: master) 現在、以下のROSのディストリビューションに対応しております。 Kinetic (kinetic-devel) Melodic (melodic-devel) Noetic (noetic-devel) Dashing (dashing-devel) Foxy (foxy-devel) industrial_ci source build amd64 binary arm64 binary main develop(master) ROS develop(ros2-devel) Xenial Kinetic(kinetic-devel) Bionic Melodic(melodic-devel) Focal Noetic(noetic-devel) Bionic Dashing(dashing-devel) Focal Foxy(foxy-devel) このパッケージは、USB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールをROSから使用するためのドライバを提供するものです。 USB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールは、センサとしてInvenSense社のMPU9250を使用しております。 このセンサの磁気センサの座標系はNED座標系(x-north, y-east, z-down)ですが、 モジュール内のマイコン(LPC1343)においてENU座標系(x-east, y-north, z-up)に変換され、 これはROSで使われる座標系のルールにも適合しています。詳しくは、REP-0103をご覧ください。 USB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールはオープンハード・オープンソースのため、モジュール内のマイコンのファームウェアの変更が可能です。 このROSパッケージはデフォルトのファームウェアにのみ対応しております。ファームウェアを変更された場合、正常な動作ができなくなる恐れがございますので、ご了承ください。 ver2.0でのご利用について 2018年10月現在、販売されているUSB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールはver2.0となります。 このバージョンのデフォルトのファームウェアには、ASCII出力とBinary出力の２つのデータ出力形式があります。 センサ出荷時点ではASCII出力に設定されています。出力形式の切り替え方法は、以下のリポジトリにあるマニュアルをご参照ください。 https://github.com/rt-net/RT-USB-9AXIS-00 ROS 2のパッケージについてはros2-develのREADMEをご覧ください。 ROS Kineticの場合 sudo apt install ros-kinetic-rt-usb-9axisimu-driver ROS Melodicの場合 sudo apt install ros-melodic-rt-usb-9axisimu-driver catkinワークスペースを~/catkin_wsとすると、以下のような手順になります。 ~/catkin_ws/src git clone $ROS_DISTRO-devel https://github.com/rt-net/rt_usb_9axisimu_driver.git ~/catkin_ws catkin build 2のようにインストールを行った場合、ドライバを起動しようとする端末(Terminal)で、 source ~/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash roscore rosrun rt_usb_9axisimu_driver rt_usb_9axisimu_driver roslaunch rt_usb_9axisimu_driver rt_usb_9axisimu_driver.launch [ERROR] Error opening sensor device, re-check devices. sudo chmod /dev/ttyACM0 rt_usb_9axisimu_driver rt_usb_9axisimu_driverはUSB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールの出力を受信し、角速度と並進加速度・磁束密度をパブリッシュします。 /imu/data_raw(sensor_msgs/Imu) /imu/mag(sensor_msgs/MagneticField) /imu/temperature(std_msgs/Float64) ~frame_id (string, default: imu_link) IMUデータのヘッダーにセットされるフレーム名 ~port (string, default: /dev/ttyACM0) ~linear_acceleration_stddev (double, default: ~angular_velocity_stddev (double, default: 角速度の共分散行列の対角成分の平方根(rad/s) ~magnetic_field_stddev (double, default:

This package provides an interface for operating the schunk dexterous hand (SDH), including the tactile sensors.

This package provides simulated tactile sensors for the Schunk Dextrous
     Hand (SDH) which is mounted on the Care-O-bot arm. The node subscribes to
     the Gazebo bumper topics of the SDH. It transforms the Gazebo feedback to
     the "tactile_data" topic to provide the same tactile sensor interface as
     the schunk_sdh package.

     The following parameters can be set:
     * cells_x: The number of patches on the tactile sensor in the direction
                perpendicular to the finger. Defaults to 6.
     * cells_y: The number of patches on the tactile sensor along the direction
                of the finger. Defaults to 14.
     * output_range: The maximum output value of one patch. Defaults to 3500.
     * sensitivity: The change of output in one patch per Newton. Defaults to
                    350. The sensitivity can be approximated by the following
                    formula: S = output_range / (measurement_range * cell_area)
                    - The measurement range of the tactile pads is 250 kPa (from
                      the data sheet).
                    - The output range can be determined by experiment from the
                      real SDH. It is about 3500.
                    - The cell area is the size of one patch. Length and width
                      of the area are determined by dividing the length/width
                      of the collision surface by the number of cells in the
                      respective direction.
                      Important: In most cases this is NOT the cell area that is
                                 given in the data sheet!
     * filter_length: The length of the moving average filter which smoothes
                      the values from simulation. Defaults to 10.

     The node subscribes to the following topics to receive data from the
     simulation:
     * thumb_2/state
     * thumb_3/state
     * finger_12/state
     * finger_13/state
     * finger_22/state
     * finger_23/state

     The node publishes the processed data on the following topic:
     * tactile_data

     The simulated bumper must obtain the collision data in the link that the
     sensor is attached to. This is achieved by setting the "frameName" property
     in the gazebo_ros_bumper controller.

description:
Serial is a cross-platform, simple to use library for using serial ports on computers.
    This library provides a C++, object oriented interface for interacting with RS-232
    like devices on Linux and Windows.
