description: Package for using SkyWay from ROS
readme: SkyWay ROS ROSからSkyWayを利用するためのパッケージです。 端末間でP2P接続を行うための規格としてWebRTCが標準化されています。 SkyWayはWebRTCプラットフォームであり、SkyWayを利用することで、ブラウザ・iOS・Androidなどの環境で相互にWebRTC接続を簡単に行うことができます。 WebRTCは映像と音声を双方向通信するためのMediaStreamと、データを転送するためのDataChannelが定義されています。 Skyway ROSを利用することで、MediaStreamとDataChannelをROSパッケージから利用することができます。 SkyWay ROSでは2つのサービスが提供されており、これらを利用し操作を行うことで、外部プログラム(Peer)に対してMediaStreamとDataConnection(over DataChannel)の確立を行います。 SkyWayControl SkyWayEvents SkyWay ROS内のMediaSocketに対してRTP/RTCPを送信することで、確立されたMedia Connectionを経由して、Peerに対して映像と音声を送ることができます。 また、データ転送用のROS Pluginをユーザ側で定義することができ、そのPluginに対してROS Topic, Service, Actionなどを用いてデータを送受信することで、 Peerとデータのやりとりを行うことができます。 操作方法とAPI仕様 まずSkyWay ROS内に、SkyWayサーバとやり取りするために　Peer Objectを生成します。以下のドキュメントを参照してください。 PeerObjectの生成 PeerObjectの状態確認 Peer Objectが生成できたら、MediaConnection, DataConnectionの接続処理及び待ち受けが行えるようになります。以下のページを参照してください。 MediaConnectionの確立 MediaConnectionの待ち受け DataConnectionの確立 DataConnectionの待ち受け DataConnectionが確立できたら、Pluginを介して外部ROS Moduleとデータのやり取りを行えます。 Pluginの仕様についてはこちらのドキュメントを参照してください。 tipsも参照して下さい。 examplesディレクトリ及びskyway_for_ros_examplesリポジトリを参照して下さい。

description:
This package contains a ROS wrapper for OpenSlam's Gmapping. 
  The gmapping package provides laser-based SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping), 
  as a ROS node called slam_gmapping. Using slam_gmapping, you can create a 2-D occupancy
  grid map (like a building floorplan) from laser and pose data collected by a mobile robot.
slam_gmapping contains a wrapper around gmapping which provides SLAM capabilities.

This package pulls in the Karto mapping library, and provides a ROS
     wrapper for using it.
maintainers: Michael Ferguson, Luc Bettaieb, Russell Toris

SMACC is a ROS/C++ library to implement in easy and systematic way UML StateCharts (AKA state machines). SMACC is inspired by the SMACH ROS package and it is built on top of Boost StateChart library. Developed by Reel Robotics.

Published topics configured ~topics parameter. Usage run snmp_reader node pass required configuration. config folder examples node configuration.

The teb_local_planner package implements a plugin
    to the base_local_planner of the 2D navigation stack.
    The underlying method called Timed Elastic Band locally optimizes
    the robot's trajectory with respect to trajectory execution time,
    separation from obstacles and compliance with kinodynamic constraints at runtime.

description:
The ROS packages in this repository were created to provide an improved
    alternative Inverse Kinematics solver to the popular inverse Jacobian
    methods in KDL.  TRAC-IK handles joint-limited chains better than KDL
    without increasing solve time.
This package contains the source code for testing and comparing trac_ik
readme: package examples programs standalone TRAC-IK solver related code. exists ik_tests program compares KDL’s Pseudoinverse Jacobian solver TRAC-IK. pr2_arm.launch files runs test default robot’s 7-DOF arm chain. package part ROS Indigo/Jade binaries: sudo apt-get install ros-jade-trac-ik

description:
TRAC-IK is a faster, significantly more reliable drop-in replacement for
    KDL's pseudoinverse Jacobian solver.

    The TRAC-IK library has a very similar API to KDL's IK solver calls,
    except that the user passes a maximum time instead of a maximum number of
    search iterations.  Additionally, TRAC-IK allows for error tolerances to
    be set independently for each Cartesian dimension (x,y,z,roll,pitch.yaw).
readme: TRAC-IK kinematics solver built trac_ik_lib library tested ROS Indigo Catkin). headers shared objects package linked user programs. package part ROS Noetic binaries: sudo apt-get install ros-noetic-trac-ik package part ROS Indigo/Jade binaries: sudo apt-get install ros-jade-trac-ik requires Ubuntu packages NLOpt Libraries installed ros-indigo-nlopt packages proper headers). running sudo apt-get install libnlopt-dev trusty standard Ubuntu distros. Alternatively, run ```rosdep update rosdep install trac_ik_lib KDL KDL::ChainFkSolverPos_recursive fk_solver(chain); KDL::ChainIkSolverVel_pinv vik_solver(chain); KDL::ChainJntToJacSolver jac_solver(chain); KDL::ChainIkSolverPos_NR_JL ik_solver(KDL::Chain chain, KDL::JntArray lower_joint_limits, KDL::JntArray upper_joint_limits, fk_solver, vik_solver, int num_iterations, double error); int ik_solver.CartToJnt(KDL::JntArray joint_seed, KDL::Frame desired_end_effector_pose, KDL::JntArray& return_joints); NOTE: CartToJnt succeeded NOTE: timeout seconds iterations set called loop timer. NOTE: error acceptable purposes TRAC-IK: #include <trac_ik/trac_ik.hpp> TRAC_IK::TRAC_IK ik_solver(KDL::Chain chain, KDL::JntArray lower_joint_limits, KDL::JntArray upper_joint_limits, double timeout_in_secs=0.005, double error=1e-5, TRAC_IK::SolveType type=TRAC_IK::Speed); TRAC_IK::TRAC_IK ik_solver(string base_link, string tip_link, string URDF_param="/robot_description", double timeout_in_secs=0.005, double error=1e-5, TRAC_IK::SolveType type=TRAC_IK::Speed); NOTE: arguments constructors optional. type Speed: returns quickly solution Distance: runs full timeout_in_secs, returns solution minimizes SSE seed Manip1: runs full timeout, returns solution maximizes sqrt(det(J*J^T)) product singular values Jacobian) Manip2: runs full timeout, returns solution minimizes ratio min max singular values Jacobian. int ik_solver.CartToJnt(KDL::JntArray joint_seed, KDL::Frame desired_end_effector_pose, KDL::JntArray& return_joints, KDL::Twist tolerances); NOTE: CartToJnt succeeded NOTE: tolerances effector pose optional, default ABS() values set tolerances -tol..0..+tol Cartesian dimensions effector pose.

You’ll extra output instantiating class WARN] root link base_footprint inertia URDF, KDL support root link inertia. workaround, add extra dummy link URDF. finishing program you’ll error apparently): terminate called throwing instance 'boost::exception_detail::clone_impl<boost::exception_detail::error_info_injector<boost::lock_error> boost: mutex lock failed pthread_mutex_lock: Invalid argument Aborted (core dumped) final note didn’t simplify letting Pose/PoseStamped messages input, transforming coordinate frames, adding kinematics, simple.

description: Efficient Task Space Inverse Dynamics (TSID) based on Pinocchio
readme: TSID Task Space Inverse Dynamics TSID library optimization-based inverse-dynamics control based rigid multi-body dynamics library Pinocchio. Documentation project wiki overview design library. exercises folder find examples TSID Python robot manipulators, humanoids, quadrupeds. website Andrea Del Prete find slides video lessons TSID. Memmo summer school Installation Conda dive TSID Python, single line sufficient (assuming Conda installed): conda install tsid conda-forge Installation Debian/Ubuntu packages, robotpkg added robotpkg’s software repository commands: sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/robotpkg.list <<EOF deb [arch=amd64] http://robotpkg.openrobots.org/packages/debian/pub $(lsb_release robotpkg EOF curl http://robotpkg.openrobots.org/packages/debian/robotpkg.key sudo apt-key add sudo apt update install TSID python bindings (replace Python version) sudo apt install robotpkg-py3*-tsid Installation sources install dependencies: boost (unit_test_framework) eigen3 pinocchio eiquadprog example-robot-data running examples) install eigen3 Ubuntu apt-get: sudo apt-get install libeigen3-dev install pinocchio follow instruction website. compile TSID: $DEVEL/openrobots/src/ git clone --recursive git@github.com:stack-of-tasks/tsid.git tsid mkdir _build-RELEASE _build-RELEASE cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=RELEASE -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=$DEVEL/openrobots install Python Bindings library python, offer python bindings based Boost.Python EigenPy. install EigenPy compile source code: git description: 3D models of the TurtleBot3 for simulation and visualization

This package provides four TurtleBot3 basic example include move using interactive marker, move and stop using LDS, move to goal position, move to custom routes. The interactions node is that you can control the TurtleBot3 front and back side or rotate to goal position. The obstacle node is that when the robot meets an obstacle, it stops. The patrol node is that TurtleBot3 move to custom route. There are 3 route(square, triangle, circle) in this package. You can add your route and move the TurtleBot3. The pointop node is that you can insert goal position include distance x-axis, y-axis and angluar z-axis.
The turtlebot3_navigation provides roslaunch scripts for starting the navigation.

This repository includes ros packages to plan routes for multiple robots on a search graph. It creates a search graph out of a pixel map and tries to find a path for multiple robots using an extended approach for prioritized planning. The inputs are the tuw_multi_robot_msgs/RobotInfo messages which include the robots pose, the map and the desired goal poses. The output are multiple synchronized routes given to the individual robots.
maintainers: Benjamin Binder, Markus Bader, Florian Beck

description:
The tuw_multi_robot_goal_generator package was designed to generate, store, and read goal lists for the tuw_multi_robot_router.
    The goal_saver is able to store publish goal msgs of type tuw_multi_robot_msgs::RobotGoalsArray in a human readable form. 
    The goal_server is able to read and publish this msgs and to read patches of saved goals for testing.
    The goals_random node subscribes to the occupancy grid and is able to auto generate valid goals for testing.

readme: urg_stamped Precisely accurately stamped URG driver ROS Background Algorithm 2D-LIDAR URG series resolution timestamp exclusive clock synchronization mode. hard compensate clock drift measurement resolution timestamp high-speed motion sensor. urg_stamped estimates sub-millisecond algorithm: Select algorithms based sensor model UTM: UTM-30LX-EW UST(UUST1): UST-*LX, UST-*LC firmware version UST(UUST2-Unfixed): UST-*LX, UST-*LC firmware version UST(UUST2-Fixed): UST-*LX, UST-*LC firmware version Determine sensor internal clock state (clock offset gain) command (Triggered timer default) UTM/UST(UUST1)/UST(UUST2-Fixed): models respond command expected SCIP2 protocol) Observe sub-millisecond clock offset finding increment millisecond resolution sensor timestamp Observe clock gain multiple observations clock offset UST(UUST2-Unfixed): model responds command frame breaks SCIP2’s time synchronization logic) Request command times timing Filter responses large delay Collect sensor response timings synchronized sensor timestamp increment Observe sub-millisecond clock offset based sensor response timings sensor timestamps Observe clock gain multiple observations clock offset Determine scan origin time interval UTM: (UTM sends scan data scan finished) Observe scan timing based scan data arrival time UST: (UST sends scan data frame scan finished) Find sensor scan timestamp jitter Observe scan origin time scan interval scan timestamp jitter Calculate sub-millisecond scan timestamp based observed scan origin time scan interval LaserScan data stopped sensor internal clock estimation takes UTM/UST(UUST1)/UST(UUST2-Fixed) UST(UUST2-Unfixed). avoid stopping sensors multi-sensor configuration, urg_stamped automatically adjusts timing sensor internal clock estimation based messages urg_stamped_sync_start topic. `image_geometry` contains C++ and Python libraries for interpreting images
    geometrically. It interfaces the calibration parameters in sensor_msgs/CameraInfo
    messages with OpenCV functions such as image rectification, much as cv_bridge
    interfaces ROS sensor_msgs/Image with OpenCV data types.
Tests the enumerants of the ROS Image message, and functionally tests the Python and C++ implementations of CvBridge.
Packages for interfacing ROS with OpenCV, a library of programming functions for real time computer vision.
Virtual package providing ViSP related packages.

Online automated pattern-based object tracker relying on visual servoing.

    visp_auto_tracker wraps model-based trackers provided by ViSP visual
    servoing library into a ROS package. The tracked object should have a
    QRcode, Flash code, or April tag pattern. Based on the pattern, the object is
    automaticaly detected. The detection allows then to initialise the
    model-based trackers. When lost of tracking achieves a new detection
    is performed that will be used to re-initialize the tracker.

    This computer vision algorithm computes the pose (i.e. position and
    orientation) of an object in an image. It is fast enough to allow
    object online tracking using a camera.
readme: visp_auto_tracker visp_auto_tracker wraps model-based trackers ViSP visual servoing library ROS package. tracked object QRcode, Flash code April tag pattern. Based pattern, object automaticaly detected. detection initialise model-based trackers. lost tracking achieves detection performed re-initialize tracker. computer vision algorithm computes pose position orientation) object image. fast object online tracking camera. package composed node called ‘visp_auto_tracker’. node detect QRcode, Flash April tag code object. detection performed, node tracks object. lost tracking occurs node detect object restart tracking. viewer comming visp_tracker package monitor tracking result. Initialize workspace fetch source files wstool: rosdep update wstool init src wstool merge src warehouse.rosinstall wstool update src Install missing dependencies: rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src Build workspace catkin: catkin config --install --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release catkin build activate workspace: source install/setup.bash plugin installed, refer warehouse_ros documentation usage interface test files test/. Demo videos Build matrix status Distro Build dev Build releases Stable version ROS Noetic amd64 arm64 armhf ROS Humble amd64 arm64 ROS Jazzy amd64 arm64 ROS Rolling amd64 arm64 EOL distro Stable version ROS Melodic ROS Foxy ROS Iron Docs Main documentation site clone repository, remember checkout git submodules git clone https://github.com/MRPT/mvsim.git --recursive Launch demos steps Standalone: mvsim launch mvsim_tutorial/demo_warehouse.world.xml mvsim launch mvsim_tutorial/demo_2robots.world.xml mvsim launch mvsim_tutorial/test_mesh.world.xml ROS roslaunch mvsim demo_depth_camera.launch ROS ros2 launch mvsim demo_warehouse.launch.py ros2 launch mvsim demo_depth_camera.launch.py Main features Lightweight memory, CPU library requirements. description: A beluga extension to facilitate the use of OpenVDB.
maintainers: Gerardo Puga, Ivan Paunovic, Nahuel Espinosa
readme: Beluga VDB Overview BelugaVDB library extension beluga integrates OpenVDB, enabling advanced localization capabilities. extension OpenVDB efficiently process maps pointcloud data. Mapping BelugaVDB requires level-set maps vdb format order work properly. VDB maps generated suitables party packages VDB Mapping, resulting map converted level-set map. simple post-processing code adapting map generated VDB Mapping vdb level-set map shown #include <openvdb/openvdb.h> #include <openvdb/tools/TopologyToLevelSet.h> int main() openvdb::initialize(); Create VDB file object. openvdb::io::File file("vdb_mapping_map.vdb"); Open file. reads file header, grids. file.open(); Print names grid (openvdb::io::File::NameIterator nameIter file.beginName(); nameIter file.endName(); ++nameIter) std::cout "Grid nameIter.gridName() std::endl; Retrieve shared pointer openvdb::GridBase::Ptr baseGrid; baseGrid file.readGrid("[0]"); Close file file.close(); Cast generic grid pointer FloatGrid pointer. openvdb::FloatGrid::Ptr grid openvdb::gridPtrCast<openvdb::FloatGrid>(baseGrid); Transform level set openvdb::FloatGrid::Ptr grid_levelset nullptr; grid_levelset openvdb::tools::topologyToLevelSet(*grid, Save grid openvdb::io::File("levle_set_map.vdb").write({grid_levelset}); vdb level-set maps generated pcd maps (created SLAM libraries FAST-LIO). simple code generate vdb level-set map pcd file shown #include <openvdb/openvdb.h> #include <openvdb/tree/Tree.h> #include <openvdb/tools/TopologyToLevelSet.h> #include <openvdb/math/Transform.h> #include <pcl/io/pcd_io.h> #include <pcl/point_types.h> #include <iostream> int main() openvdb::initialize(); pcl::PointCloud<pcl::PointXYZ>::Ptr cloud pcl::PointCloud<pcl::PointXYZ>); Open PCD file (pcl::io::loadPCDFile<pcl::PointXYZ> ("map.pcd", *cloud) load file PCL_ERROR read file map.pcd return std::cout "Loaded cloud succesfuly"<< std::endl; Create VDB grid resolution openvdb::FloatGrid::Ptr grid openvdb::FloatGrid::create(); grid->setTransform(openvdb::math::Transform::createLinearTransform(0.5)); grid->setGridClass(openvdb::GRID_LEVEL_SET); accessor coordinate-based access voxels. openvdb::FloatGrid::Accessor accessor grid->getAccessor(); Fill grid (const auto& point: *cloud) World coordinates openvdb::Vec3f world_vect(point.x, point.y, point.z); Transform index world openvdb::math::Transform transform; openvdb::math::Coord ijk transform.worldToIndexCellCentered(world_vect); Set voxel accessor.setValue(ijk, Activate voxel accessor.setActiveState(ijk); Transform level set openvdb::FloatGrid::Ptr grid_levelset nullptr; grid_levelset openvdb::tools::topologyToLevelSet(*grid, grid "Map". grid_levelset->setName("Map"); Save grid openvdb::io::File("pcdgrid.vdb").write({grid_levelset}); resulting level-set maps visualize OpenVDBViewer. External Dependencies OpenVDB Ubuntu newer distributions, OpenVDB installed apt: sudo apt install libopenvdb-dev older distributions, installed sources Step Download OpenVDB Clone OpenVDB machine Jammy version recommended): git clone https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/openvdb.git Step Compile OpenVDB build OpenVDB: openvdb mkdir build build cmake -j$(nproc) Step Install OpenVDB install OpenVDB sudo install [!NOTE] information OpenVDB refer official documentation.

Check build/depthai-core-integration.txt build/depthai-opencv-integration.txt date define options. generated integration file specifies include paths requiring installation. Installing install optional prefix build target install cmake -Bbuild -D'CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=[path/to/install/dir]' cmake --build build --target install CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX isn’t library installed build folder install. Environment variables environment variables set alter default behavior library recompile Environment variable Description DEPTHAI_LEVEL Sets logging verbosity, ‘trace’, ‘debug’, ‘warn’, ‘error’ XLINK_LEVEL Sets logging verbosity XLink library, ‘debug’. ‘info’, ‘warn’, ‘error’, ‘fatal’ DEPTHAI_INSTALL_SIGNAL_HANDLER Set disable installing signal handler stack trace printing DEPTHAI_WATCHDOG Sets device watchdog timeout. debugging (DEPTHAI_WATCHDOG=0), prevent device reset process paused. DEPTHAI_WATCHDOG_INITIAL_DELAY Specifies delay device watchdog starts. DEPTHAI_SEARCH_TIMEOUT Specifies timeout milliseconds device searching blocking functions. DEPTHAI_CONNECT_TIMEOUT Specifies timeout milliseconds establishing connection device. DEPTHAI_BOOTUP_TIMEOUT Specifies timeout milliseconds waiting device boot sending binary. DEPTHAI_PROTOCOL Restricts default search protocol. Options: usb, tcpip. DEPTHAI_DEVICE_MXID_LIST Restricts default search MXIDs. Accepts comma separated list MXIDs. Lists filter results manner DEPTHAI_DEVICE_ID_LIST Alias MXID list. Lists filter results manner DEPTHAI_DEVICE_NAME_LIST Restricts default search NAMEs. Accepts comma separated list NAMEs. Lists filter results manner DEPTHAI_DEVICE_BINARY Overrides device Firmware binary. internal debugging purposes. DEPTHAI_BOOTLOADER_BINARY_USB Overrides device USB Bootloader binary. internal debugging purposes. DEPTHAI_BOOTLOADER_BINARY_ETH Overrides device Network Bootloader binary. internal debugging purposes. DEPTHAI_ALLOW_FACTORY_FLASHING Internal DEPTHAI_LIBUSB_ANDROID_JAVAVM JavaVM pointer passed libusb rootless Android interaction devices. Interpreted decimal uintptr_t DEPTHAI_CRASHDUMP Directory save crash dump. DEPTHAI_CRASHDUMP_TIMEOUT Specifies duration seconds wait device reboot obtaining crash dump. Crash dump retrieval disabled DEPTHAI_ENABLE_ANALYTICS_COLLECTION Enables automatic analytics collection (pipeline schemas) improve library DEPTHAI_DISABLE_CRASHDUMP_COLLECTION Disables automatic crash dump collection improve library Running tests run tests build library options cmake -Bbuild -D'DEPTHAI_TEST_EXAMPLES=ON' -D'DEPTHAI_BUILD_TESTS=ON' -D'DEPTHAI_BUILD_EXAMPLES=ON' cmake --build build navigate build folder run ctest labels denote device type test labels: usb poe build Run tests USB devices ctest usb Run tests PoE devices ctest poe Style check library clang format enforce coding style. style check failing, run clangformat target, check output push target clang format installed, preferably clang-format-10 sudo apt install clang-format-10 apply formatting cmake --build build --target clangformat Documentation generation Doxygen generate documentation. Follow doxygen download install required binaries platform. CMake define -D'DEPTHAI_BUILD_DOCS=ON’ build target doxygen Debugging tips Debugging Visual Studio Code GDB LLDB (extension ‘CodeLLDB’). description: A meta-package to aggregate all the FlexBE packages
flexbe_core provides the core components for the FlexBE behavior engine.
flexbe_input enables to send data to onboard behavior when required.
flexbe_mirror implements functionality to remotely mirror an executed behavior.
flexbe_msgs provides the messages used by FlexBE.
flexbe_onboard implements the robot-side of the behavior engine from where all behaviors are started.

flexbe_states provides a collection of predefined states.
     Feel free to add new states.
flexbe_testing provides a framework for unit testing states.
flexbe_widget implements some smaller scripts for the behavior engine.

description: ROS interface to Rexroth ROKIT Locator
readme: General information repository, including legal information build instructions README.md repository root. bosch_locator_bridge Overview package ROS interface Rexroth ROKIT Locator. translates ROS messages ROKIT Locator API ROKIT Locator API documentation) vice versa. control ROKIT Locator ROS service calls. package tested ROS Noetic Ubuntu bridge compatible ROKIT Locator version earlier version, Support earlier versions ROKIT Locator. Quick Start section describes record environment, create map record localize Ensure ROKIT Locator reachable computer ROKIT Locator installed running computer network. test running command terminal (replace <LOCATOR_IP> address computer running ROKIT Locator): curl --header "Content-Type: application/json" --request POST --data '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"aboutModulesList","params":{"query":{}},"id":1}' http://<LOCATOR_IP>:8080 Start Bridge Node Start bridge node roslaunch bosch_locator_bridge bridge.launch bridge_ip:=<HOST_IP> locator_ip:=<LOCATOR_IP> locator_user:=<USER> locator_password:=<PASSWORD> scan_topic:=<SCAN_TOPIC> enable_odometry:=<ENABLE_ODOM> odom_topic:=<ODOM_TOPIC> <HOST_IP> address computer bridge started <LOCATOR_IP> address computer ROKIT Locator running <USER> <PASSWORD> credentials log ROKIT Locator <SCAN_TOPIC> topic laser scans <ENABLE_ODOM> boolean describes odometry ROS messages ROKIT Locator <ODOM_TOPIC> topic odometry Laser Localization Software running docker, <HOST_IP> set docker0 address localhost address user program providing laser data runs machine. additional parameters refer launch file bridge.launch. Start Visual Recording laser scans published <SCAN_TOPIC>, start visual recording rosrun rviz rviz `rospack find bosch_locator_bridge`/config/locator_bridge_visual_recording.rviz rosservice call /bridge_node/start_visual_recording 'ROS-Quickstart-$(date -Isecond)'" laser scans, robot position previous path, recording built time. Stop Visual Recording stop recording rosservice call /bridge_node/stop_visual_recording Create Map Recording create map recording rosrun rviz rviz `rospack find bosch_locator_bridge`/config/locator_bridge_map_creation.rviz rosservice call /bridge_node/start_map map created, map server set active map. rosservice call /bridge_node/send_map rosservice call /bridge_node/set_map Start Localization Finally, start localization rosrun rviz rviz `rospack find bosch_locator_bridge`/config/locator_bridge_localization.rviz rosservice call /bridge_node/start_localization set initial pose robot clicking Pose Estimate” button RViz, set pose map. Stop Localization stop localization rosservice call /bridge_node/stop_localization Map Expansion Record build prior map (map priormap) usual. Start localization prior map move system localized. note pose move Enable map expansion rosservice call /bridge_node/enable_map_expansion "prior_map_name: priormap" Start visual recording visualize prior map rosrun rviz rviz `rospack find bosch_locator_bridge`/config/locator_bridge_visual_recording_with_prior_map.rviz pose step set current pose recording rosservice call /bridge_node/recording_set_current_pose "pose: theta: Drive record area extension Stop visual recording Start mapping recording including initial pose (map expansion enabled) rosrun rviz rviz `rospack find bosch_locator_bridge`/config/locator_bridge_map_creation_with_prior_map.rviz Disable map expansion rosservice call /bridge_node/disable_map_expansion Nodes bridge_node node interface localization client. ROKIT Locator Configuration bridge ROS parameters /bridge_node/localization_client_config sets ROKIT Locator configuration. start bridge_node. ROKIT Locator API documentation section list options. correctly laser scan data, important ClientSensor.laser.type set simple, ClientSensor.laser.address set address port) computer bridge running. Subscribed Topics /scan (sensor_msgs/LaserScan) laserscan topic laser translate ROKIT Locator. /scan2 (sensor_msgs/LaserScan) laserscan topic laser translate ROKIT Locator. /odom (nav_msgs/Odometry) odometry topic translate ROKIT Locator. enable_odometry set true. /initialpose (geometry_msgs/PoseWithCovarianceStamped) Set seed pose localization. RViz default sends message type topic user clicks Pose Estimate” button toolbar. Published Topics /bridge_node/client_control_mode (bosch_locator_bridge/ClientControlMode) state modules. ROKIT Locator API Documentation, chapter “Client Control Mode”. Map Creation /bridge_node/client_map_map (sensor_msgs/PointCloud2) map localization point cloud. /bridge_node/client_map_visualization (bosch_locator_bridge/ClientMapVisualization) Describes current state map creation mode. /bridge_node/client_map_visualization/pose (geometry_msgs/PoseStamped) current pose laser sensor map creation mode. /bridge_node/client_map_visualization/scan (sensor_msgs/PointCloud2) current laser scan worked map creation mode. /bridge_node/client_map_visualization/path_poses (geometry_msgs/PoseArray) path laser sensor map creation mode. /bridge_node/client_global_align_visualization (bosch_locator_bridge/ClientGlobalAlignVisualization) Information global alignment landmarks observations. /bridge_node/client_global_align_visualization/poses (geometry_msgs/PoseArray) Previous poses visited laser sensor. /bridge_node/client_global_align_visualization/landmarks/poses (geometry_msgs/PoseArray) Poses estimated landmarks. Map Recording /bridge_node/client_recording_map (sensor_msgs/PointCloud2) map point cloud recording mode. /bridge_node/client_recording_visualization (bosch_locator_bridge/ClientRecordingVisualization) Visualization information recording process. /bridge_node/client_recording_visualization/pose (geometry_msgs/PoseStamped) Latest estimated pose laser sensor recording process. /bridge_node/client_recording_visualization/scan (sensor_msgs/LaserScan) Latest processed laser scan recording process. /bridge_node/client_recording_visualization/path_poses (geometry_msgs/PoseArray) Latest path laser sensor recording process. Localization /bridge_node/client_localization_map (sensor_msgs/PointCloud2) Map point cloud localization. /bridge_node/client_localization_visualization (bosch_locator_bridge/ClientLocalizationVisualization) Visualization information localization process. /bridge_node/client_localization_visualization/pose (geometry_msgs/PoseStamped) Current estimated pose localization process. /bridge_node/client_localization_visualization/scan (sensor_msgs/PointCloud2) processed laser scan localization. /bridge_node/client_localization_pose (bosch_locator_bridge/ClientLocalizationPose) Localization information. /bridge_node/client_localization_pose/pose (geometry_msgs/PoseStamped) DoF pose laser sensor localization process. /bridge_node/client_localization_pose/lidar_odo_pose (geometry_msgs/PoseStamped) current pose laser sensor, relative reference frame. Map Expansion /bridge_node/client_expandmap_priormap (sensor_msgs/PointCloud2) Prior map point cloud. /bridge_node/client_expandmap_visualization (bosch_locator_bridge/ClientExpandMapVisualization) Map expansion information, zones. Services /bridge_node/get_config_entry (bosch_locator_bridge/ClientConfigGetEntry) Returns config entry /bridge_node/start_visual_recording (bosch_locator_bridge/StartRecording) Starts visual map recording map /bridge_node/stop_visual_recording (std_srvs/Empty) Stops visual map recording. /bridge_node/start_map (bosch_locator_bridge/ClientMapStart) Start create map data contained recording. /bridge_node/stop_map (std_srvs/Empty) Stop map creation. /bridge_node/send_map (bosch_locator_bridge/ClientMapSend) Send map map server. prerequisite map localization. /bridge_node/set_map (bosch_locator_bridge/ClientMapSet) Set map localization. /bridge_node/start_localization (std_srvs/Empty) Start self-localization map. /bridge_node/stop_localization (std_srvs/Empty) Stop self-localization map. /bridge_node/enable_map_expansion (bosch_locator_bridge/ClientExpandMapEnable) Enable map expansion /bridge_node/disable_map_expansion (std_srvs/Empty) Disable map expansion /bridge_node/recording_set_current_pose (bosch_locator_bridge/ClientRecordingSetCurrentPose) Set current pose recording. rosservice call /bridge_node/recording_set_current_pose "pose: theta: server_bridge_node node interface map server. Start Server Bridge Node Start server bridge node roslaunch bosch_locator_bridge server_bridge.launch bridge_ip:=<HOST_IP> locator_ip:=<LOCATOR_IP> locator_user:=<USER> locator_password:=<PASSWORD> <HOST_IP> address computer bridge started <LOCATOR_IP> address computer ROKIT Locator running <USER> <PASSWORD> credentials log ROKIT Locator Services /server_bridge_node/get_map_with_resolution (bosch_locator_bridge/ServerMapGetImageWithResolution) Stores requested map pair files (YAML PNG), usual ROS. /server_bridge_node/list_server_maps (bosch_locator_bridge/ServerMapList) Returns list maps map server. Caveats ROKIT Locator closes connection ROKIT Locator performs strict input validation checks closes connection, receives data range. error messages kind output ROS bridge: Connection reset peer Double check data compare acceptable values ROKIT Locator API documentation. peek LocalizationClient’s syslog file ROKIT Locator documentation) hints validation failed). Error message: SENSOR_NOT_AVAILABLE happen switch ROKIT Locator mode requires laser data, laser data miliseconds mode switch). avoid LaserScan messages bridge switching ROKIT Locator mode. description:
Static reflection for enums (to string, from string, iteration) for modern C++,
    work with any enum type without any macro or boilerplate code
readme: Magic Enum Header-only library static reflection enums, work enum type macro boilerplate code. project, donating funds victims war Ukraine: https://u24.gov.ua. Documentation Reference Limitations Integration Features Examples Basic #include <magic_enum/magic_enum.hpp> #include <iostream> enum class Color RED BLUE GREEN int main() Color Color::RED; std::cout magic_enum::enum_name(c1) std::endl; RED return Enum string Color color Color::RED; auto color_name magic_enum::enum_name(color); color_name "RED" String enum std::string color_name{"GREEN"}; auto color magic_enum::enum_cast<Color>(color_name); (color.has_value()) color.value() Color::GREEN case insensitive enum_cast auto color magic_enum::enum_cast<Color>(value, magic_enum::case_insensitive); enum_cast BinaryPredicate auto color magic_enum::enum_cast<Color>(value, [](char lhs, char rhs) return std::tolower(lhs) std::tolower(rhs); enum_cast default auto color_or_default magic_enum::enum_cast<Color>(value).value_or(Color::NONE); Integer enum int color_integer auto color magic_enum::enum_cast<Color>(color_integer); (color.has_value()) color.value() Color::BLUE auto color_or_default magic_enum::enum_cast<Color>(value).value_or(Color::NONE); Indexed access enum std::size_t Color color magic_enum::enum_value<Color>(i); color Color::RED Enum sequence constexpr auto colors magic_enum::enum_values<Color>(); colors {Color::RED, Color::BLUE, Color::GREEN} colors[0] Color::RED Number enum elements constexpr std::size_t color_count magic_enum::enum_count<Color>(); color_count Enum integer Color color Color::RED; auto color_integer magic_enum::enum_integer(color); magic_enum::enum_underlying(color); color_integer Enum names sequence constexpr auto color_names magic_enum::enum_names<Color>(); color_names {"RED", "BLUE", "GREEN"} color_names[0] "RED" Enum entries sequence constexpr auto color_entries magic_enum::enum_entries<Color>(); color_entries {{Color::RED, "RED"}, {Color::BLUE, "BLUE"}, {Color::GREEN, "GREEN"}} color_entries[0].first Color::RED color_entries[0].second "RED" Enum fusion multi-level switch/case statements switch (magic_enum::enum_fuse(color, direction).value()) case magic_enum::enum_fuse(Color::RED, Directions::Up).value(): case magic_enum::enum_fuse(Color::BLUE, Directions::Down).value(): Enum switch runtime constexpr constant Color color Color::RED; magic_enum::enum_switch([] (auto val) constexpr Color c_color val; color); Enum iterate enum constexpr constant magic_enum::enum_for_each<Color>([] (auto val) constexpr Color c_color val; Check enum magic_enum::enum_contains(Color::GREEN); true magic_enum::enum_contains<Color>(2); true magic_enum::enum_contains<Color>(123); false magic_enum::enum_contains<Color>("GREEN"); true magic_enum::enum_contains<Color>("fda"); false Enum index sequence constexpr auto color_index magic_enum::enum_index(Color::BLUE); color_index.value() color_index.has_value() true Functions flags enum Directions std::uint64_t Left template struct magic_enum::customize::enum_range<Directions> static constexpr bool is_flags true; magic_enum::enum_flags_name(Directions::Up Directions::Right); "Directions::Up|Directions::Right" magic_enum::enum_flags_contains(Directions::Up Directions::Right); true magic_enum::enum_flags_cast(3); "Directions::Left|Directions::Down" Enum type Color color Color::RED; auto type_name magic_enum::enum_type_name<decltype(color)>(); type_name "Color" IOstream operator enum magic_enum::iostream_operators::operator<<; out-of-the-box ostream operators enums. description: A repertory of multi primitive-to-primitive (MP2P) ICP algorithms in C++
readme: Distro Build dev Build releases Stable version ROS1 Noetic amd64 arm64 armhf ROS Humble amd64 arm64 ROS Jazzy amd64 arm64 ROS Rolling amd64 arm64 EOL Distro release ROS Iron mp2p_icp libraries multi primitive-to-primitive (MP2P) ICP algorithms flexible point cloud processing pipelines. mp2p_icp mola_lidar_odometry framework: Main library documentation API License: BSD 3-Clause (Note module MOLA license) Introduction project libraries: mp2p_icp_map: mp2p_icp::metric_map_t generic metric map container. Metric map files extension serializations instances class. mp2p_icp: ICP algorithms. depends mp2p_icp_map. mp2p_icp_filters: point cloud filtering manipulation algorithms. depends mp2p_icp_map. applications: kitti2mm: Converts KITTI-like .bin files files. mm-filter: CLI tool apply pipeline input metric map saving result metric map file. mm-info: CLI tool read metric map describe contents. mm-viewer: GUI tool visualize (metric map) files. mm2txt: CLI tool export layers metric map CSV/TXT. icp-log-viewer: GUI inspect results ICP runs. icp-run: Standalone program run ICP pipelines. sm2mm: CLI tool convert simple map *.simplemap SLAM mapping session) metric map configurable pipeline configuration file. sm-cli: CLI tool inspect, visualize, modify simple maps *.simplemap SLAM mapping session). txt2mm: CLI tool convert pointclouds CSV/TXT files mp2p_icp Key classes project full docs): mp2p_icp::metric_map_t: generic data type store raw processed point clouds, segmented, discrete extracted features. Note filtering point clouds intentionally left scope library. MOLA implementations. mp2p_icp::ICP_Base: uniform API matching generic point clouds. Implementations/wrappers ICP algorithms uniform API. library exposes complete iterative ICP algorithms, underlying optimal transformation estimators run ICP iteration.

Path planning and navigation algorithms for robots/vehicles moving on planar environments. This library builds upon mrpt-nav and the theory behind PTGs to generate libraries of "motion primitives" for vehicles with arbitrary shape and realistic kinematics and dynamics.

readme: libqi libqi middle-ware framework RPC, type-erasure, cross-language interoperability, abstractions, logging facilities, asynchronous task management, dynamic module loading. Compilation compile libqi qibuild give cmake functions libqi's CMakeLists.txt. {.sourceCode pip2 install –user qibuild git clone git@github.com:aldebaran/libqi.git libqi mkdir BUILD BUILD cmake -DQI_WITH_TESTS=OFF install DESTDIR=./output shows features framework, refer documentation details. {.sourceCode .cpp} #include <boost/make_shared.hpp> #include <qi/log.hpp> #include <qi/applicationsession.hpp> #include <qi/anyobject.hpp> qiLogCategory("myapplication"); class MyService public: void myFunction(int val) qiLogInfo() "myFunction called val; qi::Signal<int> eventTriggered; qi::Property<float> angle; register service type-system QI_REGISTER_OBJECT(MyService, myFunction, eventTriggered, angle); void print() qiLogInfo() "print called"; int main(int argc, char* argv[]) qi::ApplicationSession app(argc, argv); connect session included app app.start(); qi::SessionPtr session app.session(); register service session->registerService("MyService", boost::make_shared<MyService>()); service middleware qi::AnyObject obj session->service("MyService"); call myFunction obj.call<void>("myFunction", call print seconds qi::async(&print, qi::Seconds(2)); block ctrl-c app.run(); run program {.sourceCode .console} ./myservice –qi-standalone standalone server ./myservice –qi-url tcp://somemachine:9559 connect galaxy sessions Links git repository: http://github.com/aldebaran/libqi Documentation: http://doc.aldebaran.com/libqi/ IRC Channel: freenode. Maintainers: Joël Lamotte <<jlamotte@aldebaran.com>> Jérémy Monnon <<jmonnon@aldebaran.com>> Matthieu Paindavoine <<matthieu.paindavoine@softbankrobotics.com>> Vincent Palancher <<vincent.palancher@external.softbankrobotics.com>>

Diagnostic messages status code status message. status code numbers: (normal operation) WARN=1 (warning) ERROR=2 (error, occur) INIT=3 (initialization startup reconnection) EXIT=4 (sick_scan_xd exiting) status message descriptional C-string. typical sequence status code INIT startup, lidar initialization completed: change (normal operation) run, EXIT shutdown. Diagnostic messages generated status changed ERROR occured. Status code error) occur normal operation. SickScanApiRegisterLogMsg SickScanApiDeregisterLogMsg: Register resp. deregister callback receive log messages. callback receive informational error messages printed console. log messages log level (Info=1, Warn=2, Error=3, Fatal=4) log message. SickScanApiGetStatus queries current status. function returns current status code normal operation, WARN=1, ERROR=2, INIT=3 initialization startup reconnection EXIT=4) descriptional status message. SickScanApiSendSOPAS sends SOPAS command (Cola-A) lidar returns response device. char sopas_response_buffer[1024] SickScanApiSendSOPAS(apiHandle, "sRN SCdevicestate", &sopas_response_buffer[0], (int32_t)sizeof(sopas_response_buffer); returns "sRA SCdevicestate sopas_response_buffer Python sopas_response SickScanApiSendSOPAS(sick_scan_library, api_handle, "sRN SCdevicestate")` returns "sRA SCdevicestate telegram listing valid SOPAS commands. SickScanApiSetVerboseLevel SickScanApiGetVerboseLevel sets resp. returns verbose level. verbose level 0=DEBUG, 1=INFO, 2=WARN, 3=ERROR, 4=FATAL 5=QUIET (equivalent ros::console::levels). Default verbose level (INFO), sick_scan_xd prints informational, warnings error messages console. Logging callbacks registered SickScanApiRegisterLogMsg receive informational, warnings error messages independent verbose level. monitor sick_scan_xd resp. lidar, recommended register callback diagnostic messages SickScanApiRegisterDiagnosticMsg display error message case status code (error). sick_scan_xd_api_test.cpp sick_scan_xd_api_test.py Simulation unit test sick_scan_xd tiny server offline tests simulates basic lidar. accepts TCP connections, responds sopas requests predefined responses sends lidar data file. readme: Install development tools Install development tools smooth installation. sudo apt install curl git Installation Install ROS (Noetic) http://wiki.ros.org/noetic/Installation Install related packages sudo apt-get install ros-noetic-teleop-twist-keyboard sudo apt-get install libgeographic-dev sudo apt-get install ros-noetic-geographic-msgs Install Clober Packages mkdir ~/catkin_ws/src ~/catkin_ws/ wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/CLOBOT-Co-Ltd/clober/noetic-devel/clober.repos vcs import src clober.repos ~/catkin_ws catkin_make source ~/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash Install dependencies ~/catkin_ws/ source /opt/ros/noetic/setup.bash rosdep update rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src --rosdistro noetic Launch Bringup package ~/catkin_ws/src/clober/clober_bringup/rules ./create_udev_rules.sh roslaunch clober_bringup base.launch

readme: Install development tools Install development tools smooth installation. sudo apt install curl git Clober Simulations Gazebo Simulation utilizes ROS Gazebo package, Gazebo version ROS1 noetic properly installed running instruction, recommend installing full version ROS noetic. sudo 'echo "deb http://packages.ros.org/ros/ubuntu $(lsb_release main" /etc/apt/sources.list.d/ros-latest.list' curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ros/rosdistro/master/ros.asc sudo apt-key add sudo apt update sudo apt install ros-noetic-desktop-full echo "source /opt/ros/noetic/setup.bash" ~/.bashrc source ~/.bashrc Installation Install Required ROS Packages Install needed packages simulation environment. sudo apt-get install ros-noetic-teleop-twist-keyboard ros-noetic-gmapping ros-noetic-urdf ros-noetic-xacro ros-noetic-map-server ros-noetic-amcl ros-noetic-navigation ros-noetic-move-base ros-noetic-dwa-local-planner Install Clober Packages Clober Simulation Package requires clober clober_msgs packages. package simulation launched. mkdir ~/catkin_ws/src ~/catkin_ws/src/ git clone noetic-devel https://github.com/clobot-git/clober.git git clone noetic-devel https://github.com/clobot-git/clober_msgs.git ~/catkin_ws catkin_make source ~/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash Launch Simulation World Simulation environments prepared, accessible launch file. Empty World roslaunch clober_simulation empty_world.launch Clobot Logo World roslaunch clober_simulation logo_world.launch Warehouse World roslaunch clober_simulation warehouse_env_world.launch Grid World grid world roslaunch clober_simulation 3x3_world.launch grid world roslaunch clober_simulation 4x4_world.launch grid world roslaunch clober_simulation 5x5_world.launch grid world roslaunch clober_simulation 10x10_world.launch Operate Clober teleoperate simulated Clober keyboard, launch teleoperation node terminal window. rosrun teleop_twist_keyboard teleop_twist_keyboard.py SLAM Simulation SLAM Gazebo simulator, select create virtual environemnts robot models. SLAM simulation works pretty similar real world ordinary circumstances. fine tuning SLAM package review SLAM package README.md Launch Simulation World worlds prepared, recommend Clobot Logo World. roslaunch clober_simulation logo_world.launch Launch SLAM Node terminal run SLAM node. Gmapping SLAM default. roslaunch clober_slam clober_slam.launch slam_methods:=gmapping Run Teleoperation Node terminal run teleoperation node explore map Gazebo world. rosrun teleop_twist_keyboard teleop_twist_keyboard.py Save Map successful SLAM map creation, open terminal save map. rosrun map_server map_saver ~/map you’ve saved map successfully Navigation Simulation Navigation Gazebo simulator navigation works pretty similar velocity circumstances. fine tuning Navigation package review Navgiation package README.md Launch Simulation World recommend Clobot Logo World Navigation simulation roslaunch clober_simulation logo_world.launch Run Navigation terminal run Navgitaion node. roslaunch clober_navigation navigation.launch Estimate Initial Pose Initial Pose Estimation performed Navigation intialize AMCL parameters critical Navigation quality. Click Pose Estimate Button RVIZ menu Click map clober located drag green arrow dirction robot facing. Repeat step increase data precision. Launch keyboard teleoperation increase precision. rosrun teleop_twist_keyboard teleop_twist_keyboard.py Move robot order narrow estimated location. Terminate keyboard teleoperation(Ctrl+C) navigation step. Publish Navigation Goal RVIZ menu click Nav Goal Click destination drag green arrow dirction robot map.

Tuning Guide Gmapping parameters change performances environments. information parameters ROS WiKi. tuning guide tips configuring gmapping parameters. optimize SLAM performances environments, section helpful. Parameters defined clober_slam/config/gmapping_params.yaml file. maxUrange parameter set maximum usable range lidar sensor. default maxUrange: map_update_interval parameter defines time updating map. smaller frequent map updated. setting small require processing power map calculation. Set parameter depending map environment. default map_update_interval: minimumScore parameter sets minimum score determines success failure sensor’s scan data matching test. reduce errors expected position robot large area. default minimumScore: parameter set properly, information similar shown Average Scan Matching Score=278.965 neff= Registering Scans:Done update frame update ld=2.95935e-05 Laser Pose= set high, warnings. description: ROS driver for CANLAB CLPE-G-NVP2650D
readme: repository ROS ROS drivers CANLAB CLPE-G-Series system. CLPE-G-Series multi camera grabber board GMSL camera. drivers exposes camera images information CLPE-G-Series ROS messages image_transport framework. branch ROS driver. ROS driver, switch main branch. Environment(PC Specification) Model: Nuvo-8208GC CPU Cores CPU Clock 3.7Ghz DRAM Size DRAM Type DDR4 System Requirements Requirements: CANLAB CLPE-G-Series supplied running Ubuntu CLPE_G_Series_SDK ROS Noetic Note: strongly recommended install Ubuntu shipped CLPE-G-Series. ROS binaries installed debian packages. running Ubuntu ROS built source officially supported Ubuntu Alternatively, containers CLPE-G-Series drivers properly passed Setup System dependencies sudo apt update sudo apt install git cmake wget gcc libgstreamer-plugins-base1.0-dev python3-colcon* python3-rosdep python3-vcstool ROS Installation Depending version Ubuntu installed, follow instructions links install ROS Noetic binaries source. source ROS source /opt/ros/noetic/setup.bash binaries installed source ~/ros1_noetic/install/setup.bash installed source link Install CLPE SDK ROS Driver Create workspace mkdir ~/ws_clpe/src ~/ws_clpe/ wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/canlab-co/clpe_ros/noetic/clpe.repos vcs import src clpe.repos Install dependencies ~/ws_clpe source ROS workspace command sudo rosdep init rosdep update rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src --rosdistro noetic Build driver ~/ws_clpe source ROS workspace catkin_make install --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release build benchmarking scripts catkin_make install --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DCLPE_ROS_BUILD_BENCHMARKS=On Run driver Launch ~/ws_clpe source install/setup.bash UYVY roslaunch clpe_ros clpe_ros.launch password:=<sudo-password> slave:=n encoding:=yuv422 timestamp:=xavier JPEG roslaunch clpe_ros clpe_ros.launch password:=<sudo-password> slave:=n encoding:=jpeg timestamp:=local Note: sudo-password number, pass password:=\'42\' equipped slave CLPE-G-Series, enter slave:=n default driver publish topics camera /clpe_ros/cam_X/image_raw: raw image published sensor_msgs::Image. /clpe_ros/cam_X/compressed: JPEG image published sensor_msgs::CompressedImage. default encoding yuv422. supported encodings, Configuration Frame rate sensor CLCC-G-01X UYVY fps JPEG fps CLCC-G-02X UYVY fps JPEG fps Visualizing Rviz default, images published yuv encoding. Rviz support yuv converted format. roslaunch clpe_ros clpe_ros.launch password:=<sudo-password> slave:=y encoding:=bgr8 timestamp:=xavier Note conversion incurs significant overhead avoided performance important. Configuration driver supports ROS parameters configure behavior. password, slave, encoding timestamp parameters overwritten run time. parameters modified config file Note: encoding “jpeg”, timestamp set “local”. Key Description Default password sudo password *Required* slave check slave, supported arguments Defaults encoding Image encoding, supported formats bgr8, bgra8, rgb8, rgba8, mono16, yuv422, jpeg. Defaults yuv422. Note encodings yuv422 jpeg incurs conversion overhead. yuv422 timestamp Image timestamp, supported formats xavier, local. Defaults xavier. xavier cam_{n}_enable Enable camera true cam_{n}_pose Pose relative base, values roll, pitch, yaw] cam_{n}_frame_id Defines frame_id static transformations refers base_link cam_{n}_image_latch Controls latching camera image messages false cam_{n}_image_queue_size Controls queue size camera image messages cam_{n}_info_latch Controls latching camera info messages false cam_{n}_info_queue_size Controls queue size camera info messages parameters read-only, initialized startup changed runtime.

description: grep for ROS bag files and live topics: read, filter, export
readme: grepros grep ROS bag files live topics: read, filter, export. Searches ROS messages matches message field regular expression patterns plain text, field type. specific values specific message fields default, matches printed console. Additionally, matches written bagfile HTML/CSV/MCAP/Parquet/Postgres/SQL/SQLite, published live topics. Supports ROS1 ROS2. ROS environment variables set, ROS_VERSION. Supported bag formats: .bag (ROS1), (ROS2), .mcap (ROS1, ROS2). ROS1, messages grepped Python packages message types installed. ROS1 live topics requires ROS master running. ROS2 requires Python packages message types path. Supports loading custom plugins, additional output formats. Usable Python library, LIBRARY.md. Full API documentation https://suurjaak.github.io/grepros. usage Installation Inputs Outputs Matching filtering Plugins Notes ROS1 ROS2 command-line arguments Dependencies Attribution License usage Search text” bags current directory subdirectories: grepros text" Print lines message live ROS topic: grepros --max-per-topic --lines-per-message --live Find message “future” (case-sensitive) my.bag: grepros future --max-count my.bag Find messages, geometry_msgs package, “map” frame, bags current directory, reindexing unindexed bags: grepros frame_id=map --type geometry_msgs/* --max-count --reindex-if-unindexed Pipe diagnostics messages “CPU usage” live ROS topics my.bag: grepros "CPU usage" --type *DiagnosticArray --no-console-output --write my.bag Find messages field “key” topics “diagnostics”, bags “/tmp”: grepros key=0xA002 --topic *diagnostics --path /tmp Find diagnostics_msgs messages bags current directory, “navigation” fields “message”, print header stamp values: grepros --type diagnostic_msgs/* --select-field message --emit-field header.stamp status.values navigation Print message lidar topic ROS1 host highlight: ROS_MASTER_URI=http://1.2.3.4::11311 grepros --live --topic *lidar* --max-per-topic --no-highlight Export bag messages SQLite Postgres, print export progress: grepros my.bag --write my.bag.sqlite --no-console-output --no-verbose --progress grepros my.bag --write postgresql://user@host/dbname --no-console-output --no-verbose --progress Patterns Python regular expression syntax, message matches match. wildcards simple globbing characters, target matches matches. Note expressions quoted avoid shell auto-unescaping auto-expanding linear.x=2.?5 "linear.x=2\.?5". Care unquoted wildcards, auto-expanded shell happen match paths disk. Installation grepros written Python, supporting Python Python Developed tested ROS1 Noetic ROS2 Foxy, works ROS2 versions (Galactic, Humble, Iron, Rolling); works earlier ROS1 version Melodic (lacking MCAP Parquet support). pip pip install grepros add grepros command path. Requires ROS Python packages (ROS1: rospy, roslib, rosbag, genpy; ROS2: rclpy, rosidl_parser, rosidl_runtime_py, builtin_interfaces). ROS1, bag files live topics, minimal ROS1 Python packages installed separately pip install rospy rosbag roslib roslz4 --extra-index-url https://rospypi.github.io/simple/ apt ROS apt repository added system: sudo apt install ros-noetic-grepros ROS1 Noetic sudo apt install ros-foxy-grepros ROS2 Foxy add grepros command global ROS1 ROS2 environment. catkin ROS1 workspace, source directory: git clone https://github.com/suurjaak/grepros.git grepros catkin build add grepros command local ROS1 workspace path. colcon ROS2 workspace, workspace root: git clone https://github.com/suurjaak/grepros.git src/grepros colcon build --packages-select grepros add grepros command local ROS2 workspace path. description: MassRobotics AMR Interop Sender

Package installation binary packages Coming Building source ros installed properly. clone repository src folder local workspace build package executing commands: Create ROS workspace mkdir ~/ros_ws/src ros_ws/ Clone repo workspace git clone --branch noetic-devel https://github.com/inorbit-ai/ros_amr_interop.git ./src Install dependencies rosdep update rosdep install --ignore-src --from-paths src/ Run build catkin config --install catkin build Node configuration configuration file define ROS1 messages mapped AMR Interop Standard messages. sample_config.yaml reference. Running sender node node takes MassRobotics AMR config file path parameter. assumed file current directory. Remember source ROS environment binary installation workspace overlay source devel/setup.bash Launch node pointing configuration file roslaunch massrobotics_amr_sender massrobotics_amr_sender.launch config_file:=/path/to/config.yaml Tests TODO.

description: Bridge communication between ROS and Ignition Transport
readme: Bridge communication ROS Ignition Transport package network bridge enables exchange messages ROS Ignition Transport. bridge implemented point support service calls. support limited message types: ROS type Ignition Transport type std_msgs/Bool ignition::msgs::Boolean std_msgs/ColorRGBA ignition::msgs::Color std_msgs/Empty ignition::msgs::Empty std_msgs/Int32 ignition::msgs::Int32 std_msgs/Float32 ignition::msgs::Float std_msgs/Float64 ignition::msgs::Double std_msgs/Header ignition::msgs::Header std_msgs/String ignition::msgs::StringMsg geometry_msgs/Quaternion ignition::msgs::Quaternion geometry_msgs/Vector3 ignition::msgs::Vector3d geometry_msgs/Point ignition::msgs::Vector3d geometry_msgs/Pose ignition::msgs::Pose geometry_msgs/PoseArray ignition::msgs::Pose_V geometry_msgs/PoseStamped ignition::msgs::Pose geometry_msgs/Transform ignition::msgs::Pose geometry_msgs/TransformStamped ignition::msgs::Pose geometry_msgs/Twist ignition::msgs::Twist mav_msgs/Actuators Noetic) ignition::msgs::Actuators nav_msgs/OccupancyGrid ignition::msgs::OccupancyGrid nav_msgs/Odometry ignition::msgs::Odometry rosgraph_msgs/Clock ignition::msgs::Clock sensor_msgs/BatteryState ignition::msgs::BatteryState sensor_msgs/CameraInfo ignition::msgs::CameraInfo sensor_msgs/FluidPressure ignition::msgs::FluidPressure sensor_msgs/Imu ignition::msgs::IMU sensor_msgs/Image ignition::msgs::Image sensor_msgs/JointState ignition::msgs::Model sensor_msgs/LaserScan ignition::msgs::LaserScan sensor_msgs/MagneticField ignition::msgs::Magnetometer sensor_msgs/NavSatFix ignition::msgs::NavSat sensor_msgs/PointCloud2 ignition::msgs::PointCloudPacked tf_msgs/TFMessage ignition::msgs::Pose_V visualization_msgs/Marker ignition::msgs::Marker visualization_msgs/MarkerArray ignition::msgs::Marker_V Run rosmaster rosrun ros_ign_bridge parameter_bridge instructions. Ignition Transport talker ROS listener start ROS roscore: Shell /opt/ros/noetic/setup.bash roscore start parameter bridge watch topics. Shell ~/bridge_ws/install/setup.bash rosrun ros_ign_bridge parameter_bridge /chatter@std_msgs/String@ignition.msgs.StringMsg start ROS listener. Shell /opt/ros/noetic/setup.bash rostopic echo /chatter start Ignition Transport talker. Shell ign topic /chatter ignition.msgs.StringMsg 'data:"Hello"' ROS talker Ignition Transport listener start ROS roscore: Shell /opt/ros/noetic/setup.bash roscore start parameter bridge watch topics. Shell ~/bridge_ws/install/setup.bash rosrun ros_ign_bridge parameter_bridge /chatter@std_msgs/String@ignition.msgs.StringMsg start Ignition Transport listener. Shell ign topic /chatter start ROS talker. Shell /opt/ros/noetic/setup.bash rostopic pub /chatter std_msgs/String "data: 'Hi'" Run bridge exchange images generate Ignition Transport images Gazebo classic, converted ROS images, visualized rqt_viewer. start ROS roscore: Shell /opt/ros/noetic/setup.bash roscore start Gazebo. Shell gazebo Gazebo running, click Insert tab, insert Camera object scene. let’s topic camera images published. Shell ign topic grep image /default/camera/link/camera/image start parameter bridge previous topic. Shell ~/bridge_ws/install/setup.bash rosrun ros_ign_bridge parameter_bridge /default/camera/link/camera/image@sensor_msgs/Image@ignition.msgs.Image start ROS GUI: Shell /opt/ros/noetic/setup.bash rqt_image_view /default/camera/link/camera/image current images rqt_image_view coming Gazebo (published Ignition Msgs Ignition Transport). screenshot shows shell windows expected content ROS Kinetic):

description: The adi_3dtof_image_stitching package
readme: Analog Devices 3DToF Image Stitching Overview ADI 3DToF Image Stitching ROS (Robot Operating System) package stitching depth images multiple Time-Of-Flight sensors ADI’s ADTF3175D ToF sensor. node subscribes captured Depth images multiple ADI 3DToF ADTF31xx nodes, stitches create single expanded field view publishes stitched Depth, PointCloud ROS topics. node publishes Stitched Depth Images bits image) resolution 10FPS realtime mode AAEON BOXER-8250AI, stitching inputs EVAL-ADTF3175D giving expanded FOV Degrees. Stitched Depth frames, Stitched Point Cloud published 10FPS. Background Supported Time-of-flight boards: ADTF3175D Supported ROS distro: Noetic (Ubuntu Supported platform: armV8 64-bit (arm64) Intel Core x86_64(amd64) processors(Core Core Core Hardware tested setup GPU inference support, EVAL-ADTF3175D AAEON BOXER-8250AI External power supply Usb type-c type-A cables 5gbps data speed support Minimum requirements test setup host laptop/computer CPU: EVAL-ADTF3175D Host laptop intel higher cpu running Ubuntu-20.04LTS WSL2 Ubuntu-20.04 USB type-c type-A cables 5gbps data speed support USB power hub :memo: _Note: Refer User Guide ensure eval module adequate power supply operation. image shows connection diagram setup labels): image shows actual setup reference): Hardware setup Follow mentioned steps hardware setup ready: Setup ToF devices adi_3dtof_adtf31xx_sensor node steps mentioned repository. Ensure devices running 10fps steps. Position cameras properly dimentions shown Diagram. :memo: Notes: contact Maintainers CAD design baseplate setup shown Finally, connect devices host(Linux AAEON BOXER-8250AI) usb cables shown Software Software Dependencies Assumptions building package: Linux System WSL2(Only Simulation Mode supported) running Ubuntu 20.04LTS ROS Noetic: installed, follow steps. Setup catkin workspace workspace folder named “catkin_ws”). follow steps. System Date/Time updated: Date/Time set properly compiling running application. Connecting WiFi network Date/Time set properly. NTP server setup device Synchronization. refer Link setup NTP server Host. Software Requirements Running AAEON BOXER-8250AI Nvidia Jetpack CUDA preinstalled Jetpack follow steps. Clone Clone repo checkout corect release branch/ tag catkin workspace directory ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/analogdevicesinc/adi_3dtof_image_stitching.git Setup Build proper exports source /opt/ros/<ROS version>/setup.bash “ROS version” user’s actual ROS version setup GPU inference support CUDA: ~/catkin_ws/ catkin_make clean catkin_make -DENABLE_GPU=true -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release source devel/setup.bash setup CPU inference support openMP: ~/catkin_ws/ catkin_make clean catkin_make -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release source devel/setup.bash Preparing sensors Setting address default address sensor set essential sensor avoid conflicts. ssh sensor credentials: Username: analog Password: analog Update “Address” field /etc/systemd/network/20-wired-usb0.network file. Update ROS_MASTER_URI ROS_IP values ~/.bashrc. export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://10.43.0.100:11311 export ROS_IP=10.43.0.1 Update server address /etc/ntp.conf (server iburst) Reboot device login Sharing launch file sensor modules missing required launch files designate transforms sensors space, essential image stitching algorithm work appropriately. transfer launch file, refer table transfer sensor. Refer CAD diagram determine location camera tag. |Camera|Launch file| |cam1|adi_3dtof_adtf31xx_cam1.launch| |cam2|adi_3dtof_adtf31xx_cam2.launch| |cam3|adi_3dtof_adtf31xx_cam3.launch| |cam4|adi_3dtof_adtf31xx_cam4.launch| transfer launch file ~/catkin_ws/src/adi_3dtof_image_stitching/launch scp adi_3dtof_adtf31xx_cam1.launch analog@10.42.0.1:/home/analog/catkin_ws/adi_3dtof_adtf31xx/launch Nodes adi_3dtof_image_stitching_node :memo: _Note: topic names, ideally names assigned EVAL-ADTF3175D. cameras names cam1 cam2 subscribed topics depth_image, specifically /tmc_info_0 camra /tmc_info_1 camera Published topics default topic names, topic names modified ROS parameter. /adi_3dtof_image_stitching/depth_image Stitched 16-bit output Depth image size /adi_3dtof_image_stitching/ir_image Stitched 16-bit output image size /adi_3dtof_image_stitching/point_cloud Point cloud model stitched environment size Subscriber topics **//ir_image** 16-bit image sensor node **//depth_image** 16-bit Depth image sensor node **//camera_info** Camera information sensor Parameters :memo: Notes: parameters set/declared, default values adi_3dtof_image_stitching ROS Node Parameters param_camera_prefixes (vector, default: ["cam1","cam2","cam3","cam4"]) list camera names sensors connected image stitching. support sensors minimum sensors maximum horizontal setup moment. camera names match camera assigned adi_3dtof_adtf31xx sensor node respective sensors. param_camera_link (String, default: “adi_camera_link”) camera Link image_transport (String, default: “compressedDepth”) Depth compression enabled inputs comming adi_3dtof_adtf31xx sensor nodes set “raw” Depth compression disabled param_output_mode (int, default: Enables/disables saving stitched output. set enable video output saving. :memo: _Notes:_Enabling video output slows speed image stitching algorithm. param_out_file_name (String, default: “stitched_output.avi”) output location save stitched output “param_output_mode” enabled. Launch Simulation Mode quick test image Stitching Algorithm, simulation FILEIO setup run. Idea adi_3dtof_adtf31xx sensor nodes run FILEIO mode recorded vectors, publising sensor Depth data subscribed processed adi_3dtof_image_stitching node: adi_3dtof_adtf31xx reads binary recorded files publishes depth_image ir_image camera_info adi_3dtof_image_stitching node subscribes incoming data runs image stitching algorithm publishes stitched Depth images, stitched Point-cloud. adi_3dtof_input_video_files folder binary video files obtained running get_video.sh script release file. folder located source folder workspace. ~/catkin_ws/src. :memo: Notes: Running adi_3dtof_image_stitching node Simulation mode requires setup adi_3dtof_adtf31xx_sensor node hand. assumed adi_3dtof_image_stitching node adi_3dtof_adtf31xx node built location “~/catkin_ws/”. assumed demo vectors folder “adi_3dtof_input_video_files” copied location “~/catkin_ws/src/”. refer “arg_in_file_name” launch files) proceed test, execute command: Terminal <pre>$cd ~/catkin_ws/ $source /opt/ros/noetic/setup.bash $source devel/setup.bash $roslaunch adi_3dtof_image_stitching adi_3dtof_image_stitching.launch running launch file, ensure sensor mode dependant launch files set sets launch FILEIO mode. ROS wrapper for the Orbbec Astra SDK
readme: astra_ros astra_ros wraps Orbbec Astra SDK ROS. library ROS), nodelet, node. Installation /path/to/your_catkin_workspace/src git clone https://github.com/semio-ai/astra_ros.git Compilation Astra SDK installed system. Set environment variable ASTRA_ROOT root Astra SDK /path/to/your_catkin_workspace export ASTRA_ROOT=/path/to/astra_sdk catkin_make Documentation Node Tutorial View node tutorial https://github.com/semio-ai/astra_ros/wiki/Tutorial. API Documentation View API documentation https://semio-ai.github.io/astra_ros/annotated.html. ROS Node Parameters ~/devices map devices open publish ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME Parameters device $DEVICE_NAME. $DEVICE_NAME determine topic namespacing. Color Stream ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/color present color stream enabled. sub-keys set true. stream enabled, setting running ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/color/running Set color stream running. Defaults true. ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/color/mirrored Set color frames mirrored horizontally. Defaults false. Stream ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/ir present stream enabled. sub-keys set true. stream enabled, setting running ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/ir/running Set stream running. Defaults true. Depth Stream ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/depth present depth stream enabled. sub-keys set true. stream enabled, setting running ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/depth/running Set depth stream running. Defaults true. ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/depth/mirrored Set depth frames mirrored horizontally. Defaults false. Body Stream ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/body present body stream enabled. sub-keys set true. stream enabled, setting running ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/body/publish_tf Set publish frames. Defaults true. ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/body/running Set body stream running. Defaults true. ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/body/publish_body_markers Set visualization markers published tracked bodies. Defaults false. ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/body/publish_body_mask Set body mask image published. Defaults true. ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/body/publish_floor_mask Set floor mask image published. Defaults true. ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/body/license license string Orbbec Body Tracking SDK. Depending SDK version set. ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/body/frame_id set orbbec camera’s optical frame. Hand Stream ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/hand present hand stream enabled. sub-keys set true. stream enabled, setting running ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/hand/running Set hand stream running. Defaults true. Masked Color Stream ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/masked_color present masked color stream enabled. sub-keys set true. ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/masked_color/running Set masked color stream running. Defaults true. Colorized Body Stream ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/colorized_body present colorized body stream enabled. sub-keys set true. stream enabled, setting running ~/devices/$DEVICE_NAME/colorized_body/running Set colorized body stream running. Defaults true. description: An automated testing framework (ATF) written for ROS which supports executing integration and system tests, running benchmarks and monitor the code behaviour over time. The ATF provides basic building blocks for easy integration of the tests into your application. Furthermore the ATF provides everything to automate the execution and analysis of tests as well as a graphical web-based frontend to visualize the results.
ATF functionalities for generating tests, recording data, analysing data and aggregating results.
Metrics for the ATF.
Messages, services and actions for the ATF.
ATF plotting functionalities for ATF results.
Plugins for the ATF recorder.
Example test configuration to verifying the correct behaviour of the ATF.
Support package for the ATF test app packages.
The bcr_bot package

description: CMake macro to run catkin_lint as a catkin_run_test.
readme: catkin_lint_cmake Catkin macro run catkin_lint catkin run_test. catkin_lint library wrapped convert xml scheme JUnit scheme. Usage Add catkin_lint_cmake package.xml test dependency. <test_depend>catkin_lint_cmake</test_depend> cmake file, find package call macro add test. if(CATKIN_ENABLE_TESTING) find_package(catkin_lint_cmake REQUIRED) catkin_add_catkin_lint_test([ARGS]) endif() default, runs quiet mode XML output. overrule quiet mode, XML output. default arguments overridden. arguments function passed catkin_lint (catkin_lint command line arguments). Important: catkin_lint give non-zero error-code case errors. macro issues account based selected severity level. notice test fail.

description:
The scenario_test_tools package implements helpers for scriptable scenario testing.
    It allows to set up a test harness for eg. a state machine or other high level behavior by
    providing mocked implementations for various action servers and services that work together
readme: Automated testing automated tests scenario_test_tools provide basic framework mocked, scriptable introspectable ROS services action servers. operated process offer correct, integral mock aspects robot. guide high-level behavior edge-cases verify handled intended. core idea tests specifies sequence services/actions behavior call. test inspect request/goal reply success failure check failure handled correctly Framework core framework ScriptableBase, ScriptableActionserver ScriptableServiceServer based. ScriptableBase introspect goals received_goals. simply list received goals. combined remember_goals context manager, goals forgotten entering context exiting multiple tests sequence. set result to-be-received goal reply method. similar methods: reply_conditionally: takes callable returns True False based goal sends result await_goal reply_directly. await_goal blocks returns goal received, goal inspected. Based reply reply_directly. Set default results default_result bothering reply* time, actions expected success real robot, text-to-speech. override temporarily, syntax: scriptable_thing.custom_reply(): scriptable_thing.reply(SomeActionResult('non-default')) Examples scripts directory scripts: example_test.py dummy_behavior.py work dummy behavior wait commands robot charge plug test sends commands checks behavior handles command correctly. Run dummy_behavior rosrun scenario_test_tools dummy_behavior.py start test rosrun scenario_test_tools example_test.py --start_scenario=test_all move_base_success.py default_result feature move_base implementation succeeds time example_move_base elaborate version handling goal differently show features framework. goal received succeeds, aborted goals expected handled

description: Service tools
This Package contains Care-O-bot specific action definitions.

The cob_common stack hosts common packages that are used within the Care-O-bot repository. E.g. utility packages or common message and service definitions etc. Also the urdf desciption of the robot is located in this stack.

This package contains the description (mechanical, kinematic, visual,
  etc.) of the Care-O-bot robot. The files in this package are parsed and used by
  a variety of other components. Most users will not interact directly
  with this package.
authors: Nadia Hammoudeh Garcia, Florian Weisshardt, Felix Messmer

description: Messages for representing state information, such as battery information and emergency stop status.
This Package contains Care-O-bot specific service definitions.

This package contains the description (mechanical, kinematic, visual,
  etc.) of the Care-O-bot robot. The files in this package are parsed and used by
  a variety of other components. Most users will not interact directly
  with this package.
The cob_base_controller_utils package contains common utils for various base_controllers.
The 'cob_base_velocity_smoother' reads velocity messages and publishes messages of the same type for "smoothed" velocity to avoid shaking behavior.

This package provides nodes that broadcast tf-frames along various (model-based) Cartesian paths (e.g. Linear, Circular).
    The tf-frames are interpolated using a given velocity profile (e.g. Ramp, Sinoid) and can be used as targets for the cob_frame_tracker/cob_twist_controller.
The cob_collision_velocity_filter package is a package for collision avoidance using teleoperation.
cob_control meta-package
The cob_control_mode_adapter package provides a node that automatically loads respective ros_controllers depending on required control mode.
Common messages and services used in various packages within cob_control.
The cob_footprint_observer package adjusts the footprint of the robot based on the setup (e.g. arm and/or tray).
The cob_frame_tracker package contains nodes that publish Twist commands based on the distance to the desired tf frame target.
The cob_hardware_emulation package provides idealized nodes emulating real robot hardware and/or physics simulation.

The cob_mecanum_controller_node provides a lightweight base controller for mecanum drive robots. 
    The out/input for the wheel command/state are wheel velocities in rad/s for the wheels 
    [front left, front right, rear left, rear right]
The cob_model_identifier package provides nodes to analyse the system response behavior of actuators to optimally tune PID controllers to be used with cob_twist_controller framework.

description: The cob_obstacle_distance package calculates distances between both robot links and obstacles to be used for obstacle avoidance within cob_twist_controller framework.
The cob_omni_drive_controller package provides a ros_controller plugin for the Care-O-bot omni-directional base platform.
This package provides a trajectory controller which controlls velocities for a chain of joints. This controller can be used e.g. with [[schunk_powercube_chain]].
The cob_omni_drive_controller package provides a ros_controller plugin for the Care-O-bot tricycle base platform.

The main purpose of the cob_twist_controller is to convert target twists into joint velocities. 
  Therefore it makes use of several implemented inverse kinematics approaches at the first order differential level. 
  The inverse differential kinematics solver considers kinematic chain extensions, singularity robustness, 
  redundancy resolution and priority-based methods.
  To avoid hardware destruction there is a limiter interface active as well. 
  Via parameter server users can dynamically configure the solving strategy.
authors: Felix Messmer, Marco Bezzon, Christoph Mark, Francisco Moreno

description: This package contains classes that are able to control the platform of the Care-O-Bot. This means to establish a CAN communication to drive and steering motors of the platform and later send motion commands and receive motor information.
Driver package for interfacing the battery management system (BMS) on Care-O-bot.

The package cob_canopen_motor implements a controller-drive component which is connected to a can-bus and works with a canopen-interface. "CanDriveItf" provides a - more or less - generic interface to the controller-drive components. "CanDrvie..." then implements a specific setup, e.g. an ELMO Harmonica Controller in case of the "CanDriveHarmonica".
The cob_driver stack includes packages that provide access to the Care-O-bot hardware through ROS messages, services and actions. E.g. for mobile base, arm, camera sensors, laser scanners, etc...
This packagae implements the special homing procedure that is needed for old cob4/raw bases

The package cob_generic_can provides an interface for nodes on a can-bus and examplary wrappers for two PeakSys-can-libs. When a can-bus-device is generated (for an example see base_dirve_chain) you can use generic_can to create as many itfs as there will be components communicating via this can-bus. Assign type of the can communication device (e.g. usb-to-can or can-card of a specific vendor) and can-address of the target device. This package comes with wrappers for PeakSys and PeakSysUSB adapters.
This package contains scripts to operate the LED lights on Care-O-bot.
This package implements the Care-O-bot mimic
The cob_phidget_em_state package publishes emergency state based on phidgets signals.
The cob_phidget_power_state package publishes power state based on phidgets signals.
cob_phidgets
cob_relayboard

description: This package implements a sound play module using text2wave and aplay through python.

cob_undercarriage_ctrl implements a controller for the omnidirectional base of Care-O-bot 3 on joint level. For a given Pltf-Twist the according wheel steering angles and linear wheel velocities are calculated based on the principle of rigid body motion. Each joint is than controlled individually to achieve the computed position and velocity

Deprecated
    "cob_utilities" subsumes a number of classes, which are used in the original COb3 software. E.g. "IniFile.h" supports the original inifile structure of Care-O-bot 3. "MathSup.h" provides some basic functions like conversion from degree to radion or norming of angles within +/- PI. The package is currently used while the drivers are ported to ROS and Orocos respectively. Midterm it shall be removed and the ROS structures shall be used for reading parameters during initialization. So, don't use this package in new code!
Interface to IO board that manages emergency stop and battery voltage on rob@work 3

The laser_scan_densifier takes in a LaserScan message and densifies it.
    Node is inspired by laser_scan_sparsifier (http://wiki.ros.org/laser_scan_sparsifier).
Fiducial recognition. Implementation of different 2D tags like PI-tag from Bergamasco et al. for recognition with a single 2D camera.

Azimuth points East increases counter-clockwise. ORIENTATION_NED: azimuth measured North-East-Down frame. Azimuth points North increases clockwise. reference: North reference REFERENCE_MAGNETIC: Magnetic North. REFERENCE_GEOGRAPHIC: Geographic North. REFERENCE_UTM: UTM grid North.
