description: Calibration and removing of magnetometer bias.
readme: magnetometer_pipeline Calibration removing magnetometer bias. Node magnetometer_bias_remover nodelet magnetometer_pipeline/magnetometer_bias_remover magnetometer work correctly, required measure bias. node listens imu/mag_bias topic measurement, message arrives, node publish node publishing bias, alternatively provide parameters. Depending application, required re-estimate bias time time runtime. Subscribed topics imu/mag (sensor_msgs/MagneticField): 3-axis magnetometer measurements (bias removed). imu/mag_bias (sensor_msgs/MagneticField): Bias magnetometer. subtracted incoming magnetometer measurements. Messages topic repeatedly bias change. magnetic_field_covariance field “misused” carry bias scaling matrix. Published topics explanation) imu/mag_unbiased (sensor_msgs/MagneticField): magnetic field measurement bias removed. remap topics namespace imu, sufficient remap imu:=new_imu. Parameters ~initial_mag_bias_x (double, default, optional): Magnetometer bias axis. ~initial_mag_bias_y (double, default, optional): Magnetometer bias axis. ~initial_mag_bias_z (double, default, optional): Magnetometer bias axis. ~initial_scaling_matrix (double[9], optional): Magnetometer scaling matrix (row-major). ~initial_mag_bias_* params, node receive bias messages. Node magnetometer_bias_observer Magnetometer bias estimation node. Subscribed topics imu/mag (sensor_msgs/MagneticField): raw magnetometer measurements. “args” package convenience script combines system plugin `gazebo_ros` system plugins single commandline, run gazebo ```bash script rosrun gazebo_custom_sensor_preloader gzserver "args" convenience script, implement custom Gazebo launcher script. my_great_robot.sdf sensor type attribute argument GZ_REGISTER_STATIC_SENSOR. <sdf <model <link <sensor name="great_sensor" type="example_custom_sensor"> </sensor> </link> </model> </sdf> Debugging set correctly, lines console launch gzserver --verbose flag: [Msg] CustomSensorPreloader: Preloaded custom sensor ExampleCustomSensor library /some/path/lib/libexample_custom_sensor.so [Msg] CustomSensorPreloader: Adding GAZEBO_PLUGIN_PATH /some/path/lib custom plugin preloader missing wrongly configured, you’ll [Err] [SensorManager.cc:295] Unable create sensor type[your_custom_sensor] works plugin fact function GZ_REGISTER_STATIC_SENSOR creates global function RegisterCustomPlugin() sensor’s shared library, description: ROS interface to IOT2050 robotic extension shield
readme: iotbot package comprises ROS interface EduArt’s Eduard-Plattform, aka IOTbot. includes kinematic concepts: Mecanum steering skid steering. dependency mounted wheels. Installation IOT2050 Devices Debian Buster Scratch apt update apt upgrade reboot sudo 'echo "deb http://packages.ros.org/ros/ubuntu buster main" /etc/apt/sources.list.d/ros-latest.list' apt-key adv --keyserver 'hkp://keyserver.ubuntu.com:80' --recv-key C1CF6E31E6BADE8868B172B4F42ED6FBAB17C654 apt update apt install ros-noetic-ros-base ros-noetic-joy git echo "source /opt/ros/noetic/setup.bash" ~/.bashrc source /opt/ros/noetic/setup.bash mkdir catkin_ws/src catkin_ws/src/ catkin_init_workspace git clone https://github.com/eduart-robotik/iotbot.git catkin_make Additionally, UART interface configured iot2050setup Configure UART pins. Reboot device: reboot Launching Robot order run robot, launch launch file. launch folder, prepared templates. Choose kinematic concept motor variant (basic performance). roslaunch iotbot skid_performance.launch everthing initialized output: WARN] Lag detected deactivate motor control bug, feature, robot stop immediately, frequent communication controlling node established. notice ROS variables ROS_MASTER_URI ROS_IP set properly. changing IP-address robot, bash code ~/.bashrc: MYIP=`hostname awk '{print export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://${MYIP}:11311 export ROS_IP=${MYIP} Steering Joystick Extreme Pro Install ROS joy interface host machine, Extreme Pro. sudo apt install ros-noetic-joy installation, ensure configured ROS communication export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://<IP_OF_IOTBOT>:11311 export ROS_IP=<HOST_IP> Finally, launch joy node rosrun joy joy_node _autorepeat_rate:=10 output warnings IOT2050 device stop IOTbot steered. find button axis mapping joystick Button Function Beam light Warning light Flash left Flash Rotational light Running light Enable robot Important: robot enabled, move! readme: l3cam_ros package ROS driver L3Cam device manufactured Beamagine. driver relies library libL3Cam Beamagine part L3Cam SDK. info L3Cam check L3Cam User Manual. package ROS2, l3cam_ros2 package. package supported Linux systems tested ROS noetic Ubuntu system. Installation Dependencies install L3Cam SDK. Download package Beamagine’s L3Cam SDK release repository install required package depending hardware architecture: sudo dpkg <PACKAGE> ROS Driver Clone repository catkin workspace catkin_ws) build: ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/beamaginelidar/l3cam_ros catkin_make Access Permission give permission cfg files. ~/catkin_ws/src/l3cam_ros/cfg chmod Network.cfg Lidar.cfg Polarimetric.cfg Rgb.cfg Thermal.cfg AlliedWide.cfg AlliedNarrow.cfg Operational Advice Jumbo frames enable jumbo frames ethernet adapter increasing MTU (Maximum Transmission Unit) network interface attached camera. jumbo frame Ethernet frame larger bytes. Ethernet adapters support jumbo frames, turned default. note order set byte packet size camera, Ethernet adapter support jumbo frame size bytes higher. check current MTU setting running command: ifconfig grep mtu increase MTU jumbo frames. Network Manager, opening network interface settings editing “MTU” box “Identity” tab. “Linux host configuration” section L3Cam User Manual full details. Receive Buffer Size recommended increase network default maximum receive buffer size. check current buffer size sudo sysctl 'net.core.rmem_max' sudo sysctl 'net.core.rmem_default' Update buffer size commands: sudo "echo 'net.core.rmem_default=268435456' /etc/sysctl.conf" sudo "echo 'net.core.rmem_max=268435456' /etc/sysctl.conf" sudo sysctl Port usage libL3Cam ports streaming communications: PROTOCOL PORT CONFIGURATION TCP (L3CAM) Fixed UDP (LIDAR) Fixed UDP (Allied Wide, Polarimetric) Fixed UDP (Allied Narrow, RGB) Fixed UDP (LWIR) Fixed RTSP (LIDAR) Reconfigurable RTSP (Allied Wide, Polarimetric) Reconfigurable RTSP (Allied Narrow, RGB) Reconfigurable RTSP (LWIR) Reconfigurable TCP internally libL3Cam transparent user. system host required port Launch l3cam run l3cam_ros driver, launch l3cam.launch file wanted) stream launch file, configure launch file, rviz visualization GUI) rqt_reconfigure dynamic reconfigure GUI) launched default, values roslaunch l3cam_ros l3cam.launch stream:=true configure:=true rviz:=false rqt_reconfigure:=false launch l3cam_ros_node, main node connects controls L3Cam, stream_l3cam.launch configure_l3cam.launch files. parameters set wanted default network sensors parameters, parameters section. default parameters passed main node (l3cam_ros_node) configure launch file. stream_l3cam launch file launches stream nodes sensors rviz main node connects L3Cam open stream nodes sensors L3Cam shut automatically. stream nodes stream automatically sensor data sensor topic data configure_l3cam launch file launches configure nodes sensors rqt_reconfigure main node connects L3Cam open configure nodes sensors L3Cam shut automatically. configure nodes function dynamic reconfigure interface change parameters, user friendly. reality, parameters changed main node services, configure nodes call services dynamic reconfigure parameter changed. rqt_reconfiugre, window show driver connected L3Cam, click refresh show parameters. description: A simple planner that attempts to keep the robot close to a line between the starting and target goal.
readme: Simple Projecting Line Planner local planner takes goals projected goal close line segment goals. Designed resist strong side forces, wind water current. (seconds)--> <param name="abort_timeout" </node> Here’s diagram showing states planner, distances parameter affects: Subscribed Topics /move_base_simple/goal (PoseStamped), takes current position starting point moves goal /move_base_simple/clear (Empty), stops movement immediately /move_base_simple/waypoints (Path), takes consecutive points navigates line Published Topics /cmd_vel (JointState), publishes velocity wheels/tracks/propellers line_planner/active (Bool), publishes acitvity state planner line_planner/plan (Path), publishes nav plan, entire route line_planner/markers (MarkerArray), publishes debug markers shown Dynamic Reconfigure Params publish_debug_markers (bool_t), set True, node publish markers debugging purposes. max_turning_velocity (double_t), maximum velocity robot turn. max_linear_velocity (double_t), maximum linear velocity robot. linear_acceleration (double_t), linear acceleration/deceleration robot. max_line_divergence (double_t), maximum distance robot diverge line goals. min_project_dist (double_t), minimum projection distance goal. max_project_dist (double_t), maximum projection distance goal. goal_distance_threshold (double_t), distance goal considered reached. (double_t), proportional gain PID controller controls heading robot. (double_t), integral gain PID controller. (double_t), derivative gain PID controller. side_offset_mult (double_t), multiplier side projection robot’s position. rate (int_t), rate robot updates position velocity. Based code you’ve here’s addition documentation describes functionality newly added node: Bounding Box Path Helper package small helper demo node takes rectangle defined PolygonStamped turns patterns published Path line planer execute. patterns: lawnmower, expanding square, victor sierra. lawnmower pattern creates path back-and-forth, “mowing lawn” manner. expanding square pattern creates square outward spiral path. victor sierra pattern coast guard sector search. accepts home polygon, add final waypoint points home path, robot doesn’t stuck wifi range. Params <node name="area_to_path_node" pkg="line_planner" type="area_to_path_node.py" output="screen"> <param name="step_size" determines distance paralel lines </node> Subscribed Topics /area_to_path/lawnmower (PolygonStamped), takes vertex square creates lawnmower path /area_to_path/expanding_square (PolygonStamped), takes vertex square creates expanding square (spiral) path /area_to_path/victor_sierra (PolygonStamped), takes vertex square creates victor sierra (star) path /area_to_path/home (PolygonStamped), takes vertex square sets center point home position. Published Topics /move_base_simple/waypoints (Path), publishes created path.

readme: Nicla Vision ROS package :rocket: Check ROS2 version: Nicla Vision ROS2 repository :rocket: Description ROS package enables Arduino Nicla Vision board ready-to-use ROS world! :boom: implemented architecture image: Arduino Nicla Vision board streams sensors data ROS-running machine TCP/UDP socket. package running ROS-running machine, allowing deserialize received info, stream ROS topics list sensors respective ROS topics: color camera streams /nicla/camera/camera_info /nicla/camera/image_raw /nicla/camera/image_raw/compressed Time-of-Flight (distance) sensor streams /nicla/tof Microphone streams /nicla/audio /nicla/audio_info /nicla/audio_stamped /nicla/audio_recognized Imu streams /nicla/imu user easily configure package, launch parameters, receive sensors data UDP TCP socket connections, socket address. user decide sensor streamed ROS environment. repository find Python code optimised receiving data board, subsequently publishing ROS topics. Table Contents Installation Usage Package List Video License Cite Installation Step-by-step instructions ROS package running Binaries noetic: sudo apt install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-nicla-vision-ros ROS2, check https://github.com/ADVRHumanoids/nicla_vision_ros2.git Source installation Usual catkin build <your_workpace>/src git clone https://github.com/ADVRHumanoids/nicla_vision_ros.git <your_workpace> catkin build source <your_workpace>/devel/setup.bash Arduino Nicla Vision setup completed setup steps Nicla Vision Drivers repository, turn Arduino Nicla Vision. power Arduino Nicla Vision, automatically connect network start streaming ROS-running machine. Optional Audio Recognition VOSK run speech recognition feature module, publish recognized words /nicla/audio_recognized topic. moment, VOSK utilized. Arduino version supported. VOSK setup pip install vosk Download VOSK model https://alphacephei.com/vosk/models Check recognition arguments nicla_receiver.launch file Usage Follow steps enjoying Arduino Nicla Vision board ROS! Run ROS package Launch package: roslaunch nicla_vision_ros nicla_receiver.launch receiver_ip:="x.x.x.x" connection_type:="tcp/udp" <optional arguments> Set `receiver_ip` address ROS-running machine. address executing command: ifconfig "inet" address "enp" voice. Set socket type TCP UDP (`connection_type:="tcp"` `"udp"`). `<optional arguments>`, decide: sensor streamed ROS `enable_imu:=true enable_range:=true enable_audio:=true enable_audio_stamped:=false enable_camera_compressed:=true enable_camera_raw:=true`), socket port (default `receiver_port:=8002`). run ready receiving sensors data Simulated board simulating Arduino Nicla Vision Gazebo Rviz: roslaunch nicla_vision_ros nicla_sim.launch <optional arguments> `<optional arguments>`, decide run simulation Gazebo Rviz, sensor simulate set true default). ![Alt Text](assets/nicla_rviz.jpg) Optional Micropython Version Note version supported maintained anymore, defitively moved Arduino version. micropython driver nicla setup simply run nicla_receiver.launch driver_version:=micropython argument. description: The quori_face package
readme: quori_face quori_face accepts image displays Quori face. image treated radial pixels polar azimuthal axes spherical face. Calibration calibrate, run roslaunch quori_face calibrate.launch. spawn quori_face_node grid image dynamic_reconfigure user interface adjusting calibration. exit, calibration written quori_face/config/params.yaml. Usage run roslaunch quori_face quori_face.launch. Parameters Transform Parameters parameter names correspond paper COST OPTICAL MECHANICAL SYSTEM HUMAN ROBOT INTERACTION” (IMECE2018-87885) face projection transform algorithm. ~transform/R: double (default: Radius spherical head. ~transform/r_m: double (default: Radius mirror. ~transform/r_o: double (default: Radius base circle bottom head. ~transform/h: double (default: Distance center projection bottom head. ~transform/L: double (default: distance projection center intersection projection axis sphere. ~transform/epsilon: double (default: angle axis roation circle intersection projection axis sphere. ~transform/delta/x: double (default: X-axis projector installation error. ~transform/delta/y: double (default: Y-axis projector installation error. ~transform/screen_size/x: uint32 (default: Width projector screen pixels. ~transform/screen_size/y: uint32 (default: Height projector screen pixels. Lookup Table Parameters quori_face aggressively caches expensive transform algorithm lookup table. ~lookup_table_resolution/x: uint32 (default: Width transform lookup table pixels. ~lookup_table_resolution/y: uint32 (default: Height transform lookup table pixels. Image Parameters input image fixed width height execution quori_face_node. received image size, resized transform. optimal performance, match expected input image resolution. ~image_resolution/x: uint32 (default: Expected width received images display. ~image_resolution/y: uint32 (default: Expected height received images display. Spherical Mapping Parameters Quori optionally configured helmet. adjust spherical region mapped input image adjusting min max coordinates. ~center/theta: double (default: Central polar corresponds center image), radians, map. ~center/psi: double (default: Central azimuthal corresponds center image), radians, map. ~min/theta: double (default: Minimum polar radians, map. ~min/psi: double (default: Minimum azimuthal radians, map. ~max/theta: double (default: Maximum polar radians, map. ~max/psi: double (default: Maximum azimuthal radians, map. Dynamic Reconfigure Options quori_face ROS parameters adjusted on-the-fly easy calibration. double ~transform/delta/x double ~transform/delta/y center_theta: double ~center/theta center_psi: double ~center/psi min_theta: double ~min/theta min_psi: double ~min/psi max_theta: double ~max/theta max_psi: double ~max/psi Subscribed Topics image: sensor_msgs/Image image display face. image treated composed radial pixels. optimal performance, image match configured ~image_resolution BGR8 encoded.

description:
rose2 is a package used to find the structural parts given a map of an indoor environment. rose2 is capable

description: The laser_scan_sparsifier takes in a LaserScan message and sparsifies it.

The laser_scan_splitter takes in a LaserScan message and splits it into a number of other LaserScan messages. Each of the resulting laser scans can be assigned an arbitrary coordinate frame, and is published on a separate topic.

description: ROS diagnostics created from SNMP agent data
readme: snmp_diagnostics ROS diagnostics created SNMP agent data Nodes snmp_diagnostics ROS node reads data SNMP turns ROS diagnostics messages. Parameters ~agent_address (str, default Address SNMP agent. ~agent_port (int, default UDP port SNMP agent. ~community (str, default ‘public’): SNMP community. ~snmp_v2 (bool, default True): SNMPv2c SNMPv1. ~udpv6 (bool, default False): UDPv6 transport UDPv4. ~rate (float, default rate SNMP agent polled. ~plugins (dict): Plugin-specific configuration. Keys config_key attributes plugins if_mib). Values dictionaries plugin. MIB Processing Plugins single plugin, planned added host resources). if_mib plugin reads IF-MIB extracts information network ports status. Parameters num_ports (int, optional): diagnostics check number ports reported. ports (dict port_name port_params): Specification expected ports. port_name string identifying port eth0), mangled ifAlias, ifName ifDescr port_params dict keys: connected (bool, optional): Check connection state port speed (int tuple (int, int), optional): expected speed port Mbps. tuple denotes (min, max) range. mtu (int tuple (int, int), optional): expected MTU port tuple denotes (min, max) range. dummy port present, require properties state, dummy parameter port’s key present ports dictionary. port_name mangled valid ROS graph resource matching regex ^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_]*$. perform mangling, iconv //TRANSLIT feature find “closest” ASCII character non-ASCII non-alphanumeric characters replaced underscores spaces), multiple underscores coalesced single resulting valid graph resource starts number), “source” order ifAlias, ifName, ifDescr. Writing plugin create plugin processing MIBs, write normal Python ROS package exports Python package plugin. plugin inherit class snmp_diagnostics.SnmpDiagPlugin implement abstract methods. Add lines package’s package.xml: <exec_depend>snmp_diagnostics</exec_depend> <export> <snmp_diagnostics class="my_package.module.Class" config_key="my_plugin" </export> my_package.module.Class fully qualified plugin class. config_key configure plugin ROS parameter server, configuration ~/plugins/<config_key>. Easy configuration local machine provide SNMP info simplified version process https://askubuntu.com/a/223734/153828 Install system packages snmp, snmpd, snmp-mibs-downloader Run echo "view systemonly included sudo tee /etc/snmp/snmpd.conf MIB enable. substitute exact MIB number. sudo sed 's/^mibs mibs /etc/snmp/snmp.conf sudo systemctl enable snmpd sudo systemctl start snmpd able walk local machine: snmpwalk public restarting snmpd daemon.

description: Plugin-replacement for the default costmap_2d::InflationLayer.
readme: Small-Objects-Big (Sob) Layer SobLayer offers faster alternative commonly costmap_2d::InflationLayer. implements distance transform algorithm Felzenszwalb Huttenlocher Install/Build you’re ros-noetic, install package apt: sudo apt install ros-noetic-sob-layer you’re older ros-version build package sources, follow “normal” build process catkin-based packages: ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/dorezyuk/sob_layer.git catkin build sob_layer --no-deps --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release additional performance boost compiling optimization, enables auto-vectorization: catkin build sob_layer --no-deps --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DCMAKE_CXX_FLAGS="-O3 -march=native" library offers benchmark-target. benchmarking figure SobLayer offers reasonable improvement costmap_2d::InflationLayer specific usecase/platform. benchmark-target requires benchmark library. build add -Dsob_layer_BENCHMARK=ON build command: catkin build sob_layer --no-deps --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -Dsob_layer_BENCHMARK=ON Requirements library requires tested Ubuntu Ubuntu running ros-melodic ros-noetic, compilers work GCC Clang Benchmarks library benchmarked costmap_2d::InflationLayer. benchmarks focus extreme scenarios. scenario measure performance large, connected obstacles. place filled square middle map. square set size NxN. ranges times map’s edge (assuming map square size ExE). increasing increases “load-factor” (number occupied cells). scenario measure performance unconnected obstacles. flattened map, mark N-th cell occupied. decreasing values cells marked occupied “load-factor” increases. N-values image shows results. results obtained AMD Ryzen PRO CPU. library compiled GCC -march=native” flags. y-axis cpu-time. x-axis shows “load-factor” (increasing left scenarios run map-sizes: upper row shows results smaller size, larger. Config SobLayer configuration costmap_2d::InflationLayer, easing change users. <common|local|global>_costmap.yaml plugins: inflation_layer, type: "sob_layer::SobLayer"} inflation_layer: meters. positive suppress automatic calculation footprint inflation_radius: meters, costmap_2d::InflationLayer inscribed_radius: decay, costmap_2d::InflationLayer cost_scaling_factor: behavior costmap_2d::Layer: calls updateCosts updateBounds skipped, false enabled: true set true, costs costmap_2d::NO_INFORMATION overwritten cost larger costmap_2d::FREE_SPACE. inflate_unknown: true References Felzenszwalb, Huttenlocher, Distance Transforms Sampled Functions Theory Computing,

description: Script to delay the launch of a roslaunch file
readme: timed_roslaunch script delay launch roslaunch file. Usage script delay launch roslaunch file. file executable (chmod timed_roslaunch.sh) Run command line rosrun timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.sh [number seconds delay] [rospkg] [roslaunch file] [arguments (optional)] roslaunch timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.launch time:=[number delay] pkg:=[rospkg] file:=[roslaunch file] value:=[arguments (optional)] rosrun timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.sh turtlebot_navigation amcl_demo.launch initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0" Run roslaunch file <launch> <include file="$(find timed_roslaunch)/launch/timed_roslaunch.launch"> <arg name="time" <arg name="pkg" value="turtlebot_navigation" <arg name="file" value="amcl_demo.launch" <arg name="value" value="initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0" <arg name="node_name" value="timed_roslaunch" optional argument <arg name="output" value="screen" optional argument </include> </launch> <launch> <node pkg="timed_roslaunch" type="timed_roslaunch.sh" args="2 turtlebot_navigation amcl_demo.launch initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0"  name="timed_roslaunch" output="screen" </launch> LICENSE software released BSD 3-Clause License, LICENSE.txt.
